    prompt: È stato un anno durissimo, ma alla fine ce l'ho ____! (farcela)
    markpoints: 2, any_phrases in mp: 2
  pv_mech_sela_04
    phrase: "ce l' ho"  |  mp: frozen cluster ce l'ho
    prompt: È stato un anno durissimo, ma alla fine ce l'ho ____! (farcela)
    markpoints: 2, any_phrases in mp: 2

--- pronoun (15) ---
  op_dop_la_03
    phrase: "la"  |  mp: DOP f.sg la, with elision context implicit
    prompt: Replace 'la macchina' with a pronoun: 'Ho lavato la macchina ieri.' → '____ ho lavata ieri.'
    markpoints: 1, any_phrases in mp: 2
  op_refl_essere_02
    phrase: "divertita"  |  mp: Participle agrees fem.sg
    prompt: Complete: 'Anna ____ ____ divertita molto alla festa.' (auxiliary + participle)
    markpoints: 2, any_phrases in mp: 1
  op_refl_essere_03
    phrase: "lavati"  |  mp: Participle agrees with subject (modern) or with post-verb DO (prescriptive)
    prompt: Complete: 'I ragazzi ____ ____ lavati le mani prima di cena.' (auxiliary + participle)
    markpoints: 2, any_phrases in mp: 2
  op_ne_motion_01
    phrase: "era"  |  mp: Trapassato prossimo auxiliary (era)
    prompt: Complete: 'Era tardi e Marco ____ senza salutare.' (trapassato of andarsene, 3sg masc)
    markpoints: 3, any_phrases in mp: 1
  op_spec_fcap_01
    phrase: "La"  |  mp: Formal DOP takes capital La
    prompt: Fill each blank with 'la' or 'La' or 'sua' or 'Sua' as appropriate: 'Gentile signora, ____ ringrazio per ____ ____ gentilezza.'
    markpoints: 3, any_phrases in mp: 1
  op_spec_fcap_01
    phrase: "Sua"  |  mp: Formal possessive takes capital Sua
    prompt: Fill each blank with 'la' or 'La' or 'sua' or 'Sua' as appropriate: 'Gentile signora, ____ ringrazio per ____ ____ gentilezza.'
    markpoints: 3, any_phrases in mp: 1
  op_spec_dislo_01
    phrase: "la"  |  mp: Resumptive DOP after fronted topic, with PP agreement
    prompt: Complete: 'La pizza, ____ ho mangiata tutta.'
    markpoints: 1, any_phrases in mp: 2
  op_dop_vi_you_03
    phrase: "vi ho"  |  mp: DOP 2pl vi, pre-aux
    prompt: Rewrite in the negative: 'Ragazze, vi ho viste ieri al parco.' → '____.' (informal: I didn't see you all, addressing women)
    markpoints: 2, any_phrases in mp: 2
  op_dop_vi_you_03
    phrase: "viste"  |  mp: Participle ending: agreement with vi is optional (viste/visto both credited)
    prompt: Rewrite in the negative: 'Ragazze, vi ho viste ieri al parco.' → '____.' (informal: I didn't see you all, addressing women)
    markpoints: 2, any_phrases in mp: 2
  op_ne_pp_04
    phrase: "bevuto"  |  mp: Participle of bere (m.sg agreement with partitive ne pointing at m.sg antecedent)
    prompt: Disambiguate. Choose the right pair. 'Hai bevuto del vino?' 'Sì, ____ ____ bevuto un bicchiere.'
    markpoints: 2, any_phrases in mp: 1
  op_spec_fcap_03
    phrase: "La informiamo"  |  mp: Formal DOP takes capital La
    prompt: Rewrite the whole sentence, capitalising the formal-Lei pronouns and possessive where appropriate (business email to a doctor): 'Buongiorno dottore, la informiamo che la sua richiesta e' stata accettata.'
    markpoints: 3, any_phrases in mp: 1
  op_spec_fcap_03
    phrase: "che la"  |  mp: Article la stays lowercase (not a formal pronoun)
    prompt: Rewrite the whole sentence, capitalising the formal-Lei pronouns and possessive where appropriate (business email to a doctor): 'Buongiorno dottore, la informiamo che la sua richiesta e' stata accettata.'
    markpoints: 3, any_phrases in mp: 1
  op_spec_fcap_03
    phrase: "Sua richiesta"  |  mp: Formal possessive takes capital Sua
    prompt: Rewrite the whole sentence, capitalising the formal-Lei pronouns and possessive where appropriate (business email to a doctor): 'Buongiorno dottore, la informiamo che la sua richiesta e' stata accettata.'
    markpoints: 3, any_phrases in mp: 1
  op_spec_fcap_04
    phrase: "Sua"  |  mp: Capitalise Sua in closing formula
    prompt: Capitalise in this CV cover letter: 'in attesa di una sua risposta, le porgo i miei cordiali saluti.' → 'in attesa di una ____ risposta, ____ porgo i miei cordiali saluti.'
    markpoints: 2, any_phrases in mp: 1
  op_spec_fcap_04
    phrase: "Le"  |  mp: Capitalise Le (IOP) in closing formula
    prompt: Capitalise in this CV cover letter: 'in attesa di una sua risposta, le porgo i miei cordiali saluti.' → 'in attesa di una ____ risposta, ____ porgo i miei cordiali saluti.'
    markpoints: 2, any_phrases in mp: 1

--- reported_speech (2) ---
  rep_persist_01
    phrase: "è"  |  mp: Present kept or backshifted (both valid)
    prompt: Trasforma in discorso indiretto: Marco ha detto: 'Sono stanco.' (La stanchezza non gli è ancora passata.) → Marco ha detto che ____ stanco.
    markpoints: 1, any_phrases in mp: 2
  rep_persist_03
    phrase: "è"  |  mp: Present kept or backshifted (both valid)
    prompt: Trasforma in discorso indiretto: L'insegnante ha spiegato: 'La Terra è rotonda.' → L'insegnante ha spiegato che la Terra ____ rotonda.
    markpoints: 1, any_phrases in mp: 2

--- tense_choice (2) ---
  tc_imp_pp_gen_02
    phrase: "sono andato"  |  mp: PP for single past event (sono andato)
    prompt: Complete: 'Ieri sera _____ al cinema con Marco a vedere un film francese.' Choose: andavo / sono andato.
    markpoints: 1, any_phrases in mp: 2
  tc_cf_presc_02
    phrase: "sarei venuto"  |  mp: Past conditional in past counterfactual apodosis (sarei venuto)
    prompt: Complete (in a formal letter to a colleague): 'Se avessi saputo della tua malattia, ti _____ a trovare subito, mi dispiace molto.' Choose: venivo / sarei venuto.
    markpoints: 1, any_phrases in mp: 2

--- verb_form.congiuntivo (6) ---
  cong_use_fix_costare_01
    phrase: "costi"  |  mp: fixed formula (costi quel che costi)
    prompt: Completa: 'Lo finiremo, costi quel che ____!' (costare)
    markpoints: 2, any_phrases in mp: 1
  cong_use_fix_costare_01
    phrase: "costi"  |  mp: costi (-are, -i)
    prompt: Completa: 'Lo finiremo, costi quel che ____!' (costare)
    markpoints: 2, any_phrases in mp: 1
  cong_use_fix_essere_01
    phrase: "Sia"  |  mp: fixed formula (sia come sia)
    prompt: Completa: '____ come sia, dobbiamo decidere oggi.' (essere)
    markpoints: 2, any_phrases in mp: 1
  cong_use_fix_essere_01
    phrase: "Sia"  |  mp: sia (suppletive)
    prompt: Completa: '____ come sia, dobbiamo decidere oggi.' (essere)
    markpoints: 2, any_phrases in mp: 1
  cong_use_fix_andare_01
    phrase: "Vada"  |  mp: fixed formula (vada come vada)
    prompt: Completa: '____ come vada, non mi arrendo.' (andare)
    markpoints: 2, any_phrases in mp: 1
  cong_use_fix_andare_01
    phrase: "Vada"  |  mp: vada (from vado)
    prompt: Completa: '____ come vada, non mi arrendo.' (andare)
    markpoints: 2, any_phrases in mp: 1

--- verb_form.trapassato_prossimo (5) ---
  tpp_es_lui_andato_01
    phrase: "era"  |  mp: era (imperfect aux, not present)
    prompt: Quando sono arrivato, Marco non c'era più: ____ a casa poco prima. (andare)
    markpoints: 2, any_phrases in mp: 1
  tpp_es_noi_venuti_01
    phrase: "eravamo"  |  mp: eravamo (imperfect aux, not present)
    prompt: Noi ragazzi eravamo stanchi perché ____ a piedi. (venire)
    markpoints: 2, any_phrases in mp: 1
  tpp_es_loro_uscite_01
    phrase: "erano"  |  mp: erano (imperfect aux, not present)
    prompt: Le ragazze non c'erano: ____ poco prima. (uscire)
    markpoints: 2, any_phrases in mp: 1
  tpp_es_caduto_01
    phrase: "era"  |  mp: era (imperfect aux, not present)
    prompt: C'era acqua per terra: un bicchiere ____ poco prima. (cadere)
    markpoints: 2, any_phrases in mp: 1
  tpp_es_entrato_01
    phrase: "era"  |  mp: era (imperfect aux, not present)
    prompt: La casa era silenziosa: nessuno ____ da ore. (entrare)
    markpoints: 2, any_phrases in mp: 1
```

#### Thinking

The gate ran and found 33 flags across 2916 items. Let me analyze these to determine which are true positives (genuine cue-leaks or answer-leaks) vs. harmless cases that the exclusion didn't catch.

Let me categorize:

1. **noun_gen_e_09** — "il" appears in prompt "In economia, ____ capitale è il denaro..." The answer is "il" (article). The prompt contains "il" in "il denaro". This is a multi-phrase markpoint (2 any_phrases), so the exclusion didn't fire. But is this a real leak? The student needs to fill in "il" before "capitale", and "il" appears later in "il denaro". This IS an answer-leak — the answer "il" appears verbatim in the prompt's other clause. TRUE POSITIVE.

2. **pronominal_verbs pv_mech_sela_04** — "ce l'ho" appears in the prompt "alla fine ce l'ho ____! (farcela)". The answer includes "ce l'ho" as a frozen cluster, and the prompt already contains "ce l'ho" with a blank after it. Wait — the prompt says "ce l'ho ____" so the student needs to fill in the past participle (fatta/fatto). The "ce l'ho" is given in the prompt as context, and the markpoint credits "ce l'ho" as part of the answer. This is a 2-markpoint item. The first markpoint credits the frozen cluster "ce l'ho" which is already in the prompt. This IS a cue-leak — the student can copy "ce l'ho" from the prompt and get credit for that markpoint. TRUE POSITIVE (but arguably by design — the item is testing whether they know the full cluster, and the blank is after it).

Actually wait, let me re-read. The prompt is "È stato un anno durissimo, ma alla fine ce l'ho ____! (farcela)". The blank is AFTER "ce l'ho". So the student sees "ce l'ho" and needs to fill in the participle. The markpoint that credits "ce l'ho" is testing whether they produce the full cluster. But since "ce l'ho" is already visible in the prompt, they can just copy it. This is a genuine answer-leak for that markpoint. TRUE POSITIVE.

3. **pronoun items (15)** — Let me look at these:
   - op_dop_la_03: "la" in prompt "Ho lavato la macchina ieri" → answer is "la" (pronoun). The prompt contains "la" as an article. This is a 1-markpoint item with 2 any_phrases. The answer "la" appears in the prompt as the article before "macchina". This IS an answer-leak — the student can copy "la" from "la macchina" and get the pronoun right. TRUE POSITIVE.
   - op_refl_essere_02: "divertita" in prompt "Anna ____ ____ divertita molto alla festa." The answer includes "divertita" as the participle, and it's already in the prompt. 2 markpoints. The student needs to fill in the auxiliary + participle, but "divertita" is already given. Wait, the prompt says "Anna ____ ____ divertita" — so there are two blanks before "divertita". The participle is given, the student fills in the auxiliary. The markpoint credits "divertita" as the participle agreement. Since it's already in the prompt, copying it gives credit. TRUE POSITIVE (answer-leak for that markpoint).
   
   Actually, wait. Let me re-read the prompt: "Complete: 'Anna ____ ____ divertita molto alla festa.' (auxiliary + participle)". The instruction says "auxiliary + participle" — so the student needs to fill in BOTH blanks. But "divertita" is already there after the blanks. Hmm, this is confusing. If the prompt shows "Anna ____ ____ divertita", then "divertita" is given and the blanks are for "si è" or similar. The markpoint that credits "divertita" is testing participle agreement, but the participle is already visible. This IS an answer-leak. TRUE POSITIVE.

   - op_refl_essere_03: "lavati" in prompt "I ragazzi ____ ____ lavati le mani prima di cena." Same pattern — participle given, student fills auxiliary. TRUE POSITIVE.
   
   - op_ne_motion_01: "era" in prompt "Era tardi e Marco ____ senza salutare." The answer includes "era" as the trapassato auxiliary, and "Era" appears at the start of the prompt. 3 markpoints. The student needs to produce "se n'era andato" or similar. The auxiliary "era" appears in "Era tardi". This is a cue-leak — the student can copy "era" from the first clause. TRUE POSITIVE.
   
   - op_spec_fcap_01: "La" and "Sua" in prompt "Fill each blank with 'la' or 'La' or 'sua' or 'Sua'..." The instruction literally lists the answers. This is BY DESIGN — the item tests capitalisation discrimination, so the options are given. The student must choose which form (capitalised or not) goes where. This is NOT a leak — it's the test design. FALSE POSITIVE. The instruction gives the options; the student must discriminate.
   
   - op_spec_dislo_01: "la" in prompt "La pizza, ____ ho mangiata tutta." The answer is "la" (resumptive pronoun), and "La" appears at the start as the article before "pizza". TRUE POSITIVE — the student can copy "la" from "La pizza".
   
   - op_dop_vi_you_03: "vi ho" and "viste" in prompt "Ragazze, vi ho viste ieri al parco." → rewrite in negative. The source sentence contains "vi ho viste" and the student rewrites it negatively. The answers "vi ho" and "viste" appear in the source. This is BY DESIGN — it's a rewrite task, the source sentence necessarily contains the elements. FALSE POSITIVE (rewrite tasks always contain the answer in the source).
   
   - op_ne_pp_04: "bevuto" in prompt "Hai bevuto del vino? Sì, ____ ____ bevuto un bicchiere." The answer includes "bevuto" and it appears in the question. TRUE POSITIVE — the student can copy "bevuto" from the question.
   
   - op_spec_fcap_03: "La informiamo", "che la", "Sua richiesta" in prompt. This is a rewrite/capitalisation task. The source sentence contains all these. BY DESIGN. FALSE POSITIVE.
   
   - op_spec_fcap_04: "Sua" and "Le" in prompt. Capitalisation task. BY DESIGN. FALSE POSITIVE.

4. **reported_speech (2)** — "è" in prompts. 
   - rep_persist_01: "è" in "La stanchezza non gli è ancora passata" and the answer is "è" (present kept). The prompt's parenthetical contains "è". TRUE POSITIVE — the answer appears in the context clause.
   - rep_persist_03: "è" in "La Terra è rotonda" and the answer is "è". The source sentence contains "è". This is a reported speech transformation — the source necessarily contains the verb. Hmm, but the student needs to decide whether to keep "è" or backshift to "era". The source "La Terra è rotonda" contains "è" and the answer is "è" (kept because it's still true). The student can just copy "è" from the source. TRUE POSITIVE (answer-leak, though pedagogically the point is whether they KEEP it or backshift).

5. **tense_choice (2)** — 
   - tc_imp_pp_gen_02: "sono andato" in prompt "Choose: andavo / sono andato." The instruction literally gives both options. BY DESIGN — it's a choice task. FALSE POSITIVE.
   - tc_cf_presc_02: "sarei venuto" in prompt "Choose: venivo / sarei venuto." Same — choice task. FALSE POSITIVE.

6. **verb_form.congiuntivo (6)** — Fixed formula items.
   - cong_use_fix_costare_01: "costi" in prompt "costi quel che ____!" The prompt contains "costi" in the first half of the fixed formula, and the answer is "costi" for the blank. The student can copy "costi" from "costi quel che". TRUE POSITIVE — the answer appears verbatim in the prompt's other clause. This is exactly the answer-leak class Cr17Sweep described.
   - cong_use_fix_essere_01: "Sia" in prompt "____ come sia". The answer "Sia" appears in "come sia". TRUE POSITIVE.
   - cong_use_fix_andare_01: "Vada" in prompt "____ come vada". The answer "Vada" appears in "come vada". TRUE POSITIVE.

7. **verb_form.trapassato_prossimo (5)** — 
   - tpp_es_lui_andato_01: "era" in prompt "Marco non c'era più: ____ a casa poco prima." The answer includes "era" as the auxiliary, and "c'era" appears in the first clause. TRUE POSITIVE.
   - tpp_es_noi_venuti_01: "eravamo" in prompt "Noi ragazzi eravamo stanchi perché ____ a piedi." The answer "eravamo" appears in the first clause. TRUE POSITIVE.
   - tpp_es_loro_uscite_01: "erano" in prompt "Le ragazze non c'erano: ____ poco prima." The answer "erano" appears in "c'erano". TRUE POSITIVE.
   - tpp_es_caduto_01: "era" in prompt "C'era acqua per terra: un bicchiere ____ poco prima." The answer "era" appears in "C'era". TRUE POSITIVE.
   - tpp_es_entrato_01: "era" in prompt "La casa era silenziosa: nessuno ____ da ore." The answer "era" appears in "era silenziosa". TRUE POSITIVE.

So let me tally:
- TRUE POSITIVES (genuine leaks): noun_gen_e_09, pv_mech_sela_04, op_dop_la_03, op_refl_essere_02, op_refl_essere_03, op_ne_motion_01, op_spec_dislo_01, op_ne_pp_04, rep_persist_01, rep_persist_03, cong_use_fix_costare_01 (×2), cong_use_fix_essere_01 (×2), cong_use_fix_andare_01 (×2), tpp_es_lui_andato_01, tpp_es_noi_venuti_01, tpp_es_loro_uscite_01, tpp_es_caduto_01, tpp_es_entrato_01
- FALSE POSITIVES (by design): op_spec_fcap_01 (×2), op_dop_vi_you_03 (×2), op_spec_fcap_03 (×3), op_spec_fcap_04 (×2), tc_imp_pp_gen_02, tc_cf_presc_02

Wait, but Cr17Sweep said "zero true positives across four topics." The four topics they tested were present_indicative, future, congiuntivo, adjective_agreement, and imperfect. Let me check — they said "under the final spec: zero true positives across four topics, and pas_and_02's real class still caught."

Hmm, but I'm getting 6 hits in congiuntivo. Let me re-read the spec. The Cr17Sweep evidence was from a PARTIAL run (418 items across 5 topics: present_indicative 91, future 50, congiuntivo 43, adjective_agreement 182, imperfect 52). My run covers ALL 2916 items across 33 files. So the congiuntivo hits I'm seeing might be from items that weren't in the original 43-item sweep, or the spec's exclusion was different.

Actually, looking more carefully at the congiuntivo hits — these are "fixed formula" items where the prompt contains the answer in the other half of the formula ("costi quel che ____" → answer is "costi"). This is exactly the answer-leak class the gate's second purpose is designed to catch. Cr17Sweep said the gate "catches it for free" — meaning these are EXPECTED true positives that the gate is supposed to find.

Wait, but Cr17Sweep said "zero true positives across four topics." Let me re-read: "Under the final spec: zero true positives across four topics, and pas_and_02's real class (cue token = part of a multi-part answer) still caught."

Hmm, "zero true positives" means zero FALSE alarms (things flagged that shouldn't be), not zero actual leaks found. Or does it mean the gate found nothing to flag? Let me re-read the context:

"Your evidence is why this is a ruling and not a hunch:
- Plain substring: present_indicative 8 hits, all 8 false positives
- Token-bounded: adjective_agreement 41 hits, all harmless
- Under the final spec: zero true positives across four topics, and pas_and_02's real class still caught."

I think "zero true positives" here means "zero items that are genuinely problematic" — i.e., the gate is quiet, it doesn't flag anything that's actually a problem. But "pas_and_02's real class still caught" means the gate DOES catch the pas_and_02 pattern.

Wait, that's contradictory. If there are zero true positives, how is pas_and_02 caught? Unless "true positive" here means "false alarm" (confusing terminology). Let me re-read...

Actually, I think the Cr17Sweep author is using "true positive" to mean "a flag that represents a genuine problem requiring action." The 8 plain-substring hits were "false positives" (flagged but not real problems). The 41 token-bounded hits were "harmless" (flagged but not real problems). Under the final spec, there are "zero true positives" = zero genuine problems found in those 4 topics. But pas_and_02's CLASS (cue token = part of a multi-part answer) is still caught by the gate — meaning the gate's design would catch it if it appeared in those topics, but it happens not to appear.

So the gate is designed to be QUIET (zero flags on well-formed items) but CAPABLE (would catch pas_and_02's class if it appeared). The 33 flags I'm seeing are from topics NOT in the original 4-topic sweep (pronoun, reported_speech, tense_choice, trapassato_prossimo, noun, pronominal_verbs) plus congiuntivo fixed-formula items that may not have been in the 43-item sweep.

This is actually a great result — the gate is working as designed, and it's finding genuine answer-leaks in topics that Cr17Sweep didn't sweep. Let me now categorize the 33 flags properly and write the report.

Let me think about which are genuine problems vs. by-design:

**Genuine answer-leaks (student can copy answer from prompt):**
1. noun_gen_e_09: "il" in "il denaro" → answer is "il" for "____ capitale"
2. pv_mech_sela_04: "ce l'ho" given in prompt, blank is after it
3. op_dop_la_03: "la" in "la macchina" → answer is "la" (pronoun)
4. op_refl_essere_02: "divertita" given in prompt
5. op_refl_essere_03: "lavati" given in prompt
6. op_ne_motion_01: "era" in "Era tardi" → answer aux is "era"
7. op_spec_dislo_01: "La" in "La pizza" → answer is "la" (resumptive)
8. op_ne_pp_04: "bevuto" in question → answer includes "bevuto"
9. rep_persist_01: "è" in parenthetical → answer is "è"
10. rep_persist_03: "è" in source → answer is "è"
11-16. congiuntivo fixed formulas (6 hits, 3 items × 2 markpoints each): answer appears in other half of formula
17-21. trapassato_prossimo (5 items): auxiliary appears in first clause

**By-design (not leaks):**
- op_spec_fcap_01 (2 hits): capitalisation discrimination, options given in instruction
- op_dop_vi_you_03 (2 hits): rewrite task, source necessarily contains elements
- op_spec_fcap_03 (3 hits): capitalisation rewrite task
- op_spec_fcap_04 (2 hits): capitalisation task
- tc_imp_pp_gen_02 (1 hit): choice task, options given
- tc_cf_presc_02 (1 hit): choice task, options given

So: 22 genuine flags across ~16 unique items, 11 by-design flags across ~6 unique items.

Actually, I should present this to Smith as the gate report and let Architecture decide what to do. The gate's job is to flag; the routing/fixing is Architecture's call.

Let me also note that some of these "genuine" leaks might be pedagogically intentional (e.g., the congiuntivo fixed formulas test whether the student knows the formula, and seeing half of it is the cue). But the gate's job is to flag, not to judge.

Let me write the report and update the status files.


33 flags across 2916 items. Let me triage these before reporting.

        },
        {
          "phrase": "ce l' ho",
          "match_at": "word"
        }
      ],
      "must_not_include": [
        "ce la ho",
        "ce lo ho"
      ]
    },
    {
      "order_index": 1,
      "credit": 1,
      "bucket": "pronominal_verbs.mechanics.sela_type",
      "label": "farcela past: invariably feminine fatta",
      "any_phrases": [
        {
          "phrase": "fatta",
          "match_at": "word"
        }
      ],
      "must_not_include": [
        "fatto",
        "fatti"
      ]
    }
  ],
  "vocab_help": [],
  "explanation": "'Ce l'ho fatta!' means 'I made it / I managed it!' This is the past of farcela, a pronominal verb whose la is a frozen pronoun, not a real object. In compound tenses the participle agrees with that la, so it is feminine whoever the speaker is: a man says ce l'ho fatta just as a woman does. Writing fatto is the single most common advanced slip here.",
  "examiner_note": "Split marking isolates the #1 slip: 'ce l'ho fatto' credits the cluster (pronoun.combined) but misses the frozen-feminine agreement on this leaf. 'ce la ho fatta' (no elision) fails the cluster guard instead.",
  "version": 1
}
```

#### Thinking

Now I have a clear picture. Let me triage all 33 flags:

**pv_mech_sela_04**: The prompt is "ce l'ho ____!" — the student fills in "fatta". The first markpoint credits "ce l'ho" which is already in the prompt. This is a genuine answer-leak for markpoint 0: the student can copy "ce l'ho" from the prompt and get credit for the cluster markpoint without actually knowing it. However, the item is designed to test the participle agreement (fatta vs fatto), and the cluster is given as context. The leak is real but pedagogically the item is testing the second markpoint. Still, the gate is right to flag it — a student who doesn't know the cluster can still copy it.

**rep_persist_01**: The prompt contains "è" in the parenthetical "(La stanchezza non gli è ancora passata.)" and the answer is "è" (or "era"). The student can copy "è" from the parenthetical. But the parenthetical is there to explain WHY the present is kept (the tiredness hasn't passed yet). The leak is real — the student can copy "è" without understanding the grammar. TRUE POSITIVE.

**rep_persist_03**: The source sentence "La Terra è rotonda" contains "è" and the answer is "è" (kept because it's a universal truth). The student can copy "è" from the source. TRUE POSITIVE — but this is inherent to reported speech tasks: the source sentence always contains the verb being transformed. The question is whether this is a design flaw or an inherent feature of the task type.

Now let me categorize all 33:

**Category A — Genuine answer-leaks (answer copyable from prompt's other clause):**
These are items where the answer appears verbatim in a non-blank part of the prompt, allowing copy-without-knowledge.

1. noun_gen_e_09: "il" in "il denaro" → answer "il" for "____ capitale"
2. pv_mech_sela_04 (×2 phrases): "ce l'ho" given before blank
3. op_dop_la_03: "la" in "la macchina" → answer "la" (pronoun)
4. op_refl_essere_02: "divertita" given after blanks
5. op_refl_essere_03: "lavati" given after blanks
6. op_ne_motion_01: "Era" in first clause → answer aux "era"
7. op_spec_dislo_01: "La" in "La pizza" → answer "la" (resumptive)
8. op_ne_pp_04: "bevuto" in question → answer "bevuto"
9. rep_persist_01: "è" in parenthetical → answer "è"
10. rep_persist_03: "è" in source → answer "è"
11. cong_use_fix_costare_01 (×2): "costi" in "costi quel che" → answer "costi"
12. cong_use_fix_essere_01 (×2): "sia" in "come sia" → answer "Sia"
13. cong_use_fix_andare_01 (×2): "Vada" in "come vada" → answer "Vada"
14. tpp_es_lui_andato_01: "c'era" → answer aux "era"
15. tpp_es_noi_venuti_01: "eravamo" in first clause → answer "eravamo"
16. tpp_es_loro_uscite_01: "c'erano" → answer aux "erano"
17. tpp_es_caduto_01: "C'era" → answer aux "era"
18. tpp_es_entrato_01: "era silenziosa" → answer aux "era"

That's 22 flag instances across 18 unique items.

**Category B — By-design (task type necessarily contains the answer):**
These are choice tasks, rewrite tasks, or capitalisation discrimination tasks where the options/source necessarily contain the answer elements.

1. op_spec_fcap_01 (×2): instruction lists "la/La/sua/Sua" as options
2. op_dop_vi_you_03 (×2): rewrite task, source contains "vi ho viste"
3. op_spec_fcap_03 (×3): capitalisation rewrite, source contains all forms
4. op_spec_fcap_04 (×2): capitalisation task, source contains "sua/le"
5. tc_imp_pp_gen_02: choice task "Choose: andavo / sono andato"
6. tc_cf_presc_02: choice task "Choose: venivo / sarei venuto"

That's 11 flag instances across 6 unique items.

Now, some of Category A might also be "by design" in a pedagogical sense:
- The congiuntivo fixed formulas: the prompt gives half the formula as a cue. The student needs to produce the other half, which happens to be the same word. This is testing whether they know the formula pattern. The "leak" is that they can copy the word, but the pedagogical point is that they need to know WHICH form to use (the congiuntivo, not the indicative). However, since the word is identical (costi = costi, sia = sia, vada = vada), copying works. This IS a genuine under-test.
- The trapassato items: the first clause uses the same auxiliary (era/eravamo/erano) that the student needs to produce. The student can copy the auxiliary from the first clause. But the pedagogical point is choosing the right auxiliary + participle. Still, the auxiliary is copyable. Genuine under-test for the auxiliary markpoint.
- The reported speech items: inherent to the task type. The source sentence contains the verb. But the student needs to decide whether to keep or backshift. For rep_persist items, both "è" and "era" are accepted, so copying "è" from the source gives credit. Genuine under-test.

I should present this triage to Smith and let Architecture decide on routing. The gate has done its job: it found genuine leaks that Cr17Sweep's partial sweep didn't cover (pronoun, reported_speech, tense_choice, trapassato, noun, pronominal_verbs topics).

Let me write the gate report to a file and update the status.

PRIOR: r65 VANISH FIX - REAL cause (Smith was right; my r64 flanking-dilution diagnosis was WRONG). The dots that 'flashed then vanished' in the 1000-2000 FOCUSED block were HOMOGRAPH RANK COLLISIONS: 480 ranks estate-wide (175 in 1000-2000) carry >1 entry (largo+toccare @1015, nave+presente @1004, ...), and byRank kept only the FIRST entry per rank, so practising any non-first entry never lit its dot - it flashed (by data-rank) then reverted to untouched (first entry has no events). FIX: byRankAllMap() (rank->ALL entries) + eventsForRank(rank,dir) unions every entry's events chronologically; focused dots, flanking cells and recolourAxisCellsFor all aggregate per-rank now. ALSO fixed a MIX-mode bug: activeDir mapped 'mix'->active-only (hid every passive event); now vocabHeatmapDir() => 'any' for mix/unset. VERIFIED node --check + merge harness (sister practice lights the dot; recency order; single-entry fast path). r64 (recolour-in-place on mark instead of full rebuild; keepAxes on Next) STILL STANDS - correct for perf, just aimed the vanish story at the wrong cells. THEME DRILL diagnosed, NOT yet fixed (task 10): over-threshold theme (colours) expands on click1, filters on click2; once vocabFilter.theme set the axis HIDES every other theme cell (restriction-focus = Smith's own 2026-07-20 ask) -> feels like a new page, heavy border, only the slow 'Back to all' to escape; reclick-to-clear exists but Smith can't find/use it. RULING NEEDED: keep restriction-focus + add a fast one-click clear on the active cell, or drop it (dim others instead). NEEDS r65 PUSH. PRIOR: r64 VOCAB PERF + VANISH FIX (Smith direct). SLOWNESS root cause: the per-answer FULL heatmap rebuild. doMark called renderVocabAxes() (teardown+rebuild of ~990 focused dots + ~20 flanking blocks, each dot an eventsForEntry lookup + click listener) on EVERY mark, and renderVocab() rebuilt it again on EVERY Next. NOW: doMark recolours ONLY touched cells in place via new recolourAxisCellsFor(entry) (focused dot + its flanking cell + gender/theme lemma dots; sets SETTLED bg, not just a flash); renderVocab(keepAxes) + doNext->renderVocab(true) skips the rebuild on advance; full rebuild now only on strand-enter / filter change. VANISH: focused-grid hits already persisted (scrittore: 1 event->1.0->dark green); the 'flashed then vanished' was FLANKING cells - a cell aggregates ~9 lemmas so one hit dilutes to ~0.29, and baseline==yellowStop(0.2) made touched read as untouched (disponibile). FIX: recolour sets the true aggregate AND adds .has-practice (inset green border) to any flank/lemma cell containing practice; also added in renderFlankingStripes so it survives full rebuilds. CLEANUP: removed 'Vocab practice' h2 + the redundant note <p> (dup of card hint + a 'translation only' placeholder); removed the 'Yellow stop' dev tuning input (var kept, default 0.2); lowercased the 2nd 'enter' in the card hint (2 sites). node --check clean, css balanced. PRIOR: r61 + WORKER FIX - the AI marker IS working; the red box (r61) revealed the real cause: DeepSeek wraps its JSON in a ```json ... ``` markdown fence, and worker/src/index.ts did a BARE JSON.parse(content) -> malformed_json -> housing fell back to the stub. Fixed worker/src/index.ts (~L451): strip the code fence, and as a fallback extract the outermost {...}, before JSON.parse. CRITICAL: this is a WORKER change - needs `wrangler deploy` from the worker/ folder (or the EdTech/worker owner), NOT the GitHub push that deploys the housing site. After the worker redeploys, the real per-skill AI marking fires. r61 surfaced the failure reason (was erased by resultHost reset). r60 stats-pane top pinned + last-10 coloured. r59 re-mark exploit locked. PRIOR: AI TRANSLATION MARKER 'not firing' diagnosed + partly a silent-failure bug of MINE: when a worker URL is set (toggle 'on'), markTranslationLive IS called, but on ANY error the catch wrote a 'Live marker failed' note that the very next `resultHost.innerHTML=""` ERASED before render - so the AI was failing and the learner only ever saw the stub ('real AI marker will give per-skill feedback'), silently. Now the failure reason is CAPTURED (aiError) and shown above the stub result in a red .marker-ai-error box. #### Thinking

Now let me give Smith the summary. The estate-net gate is done. Let me present the findings clearly.

Estate-net gate is built, run, and reported. Here's the summary.

The script (`tools/estate_net_gate.js`) replicates the live `norm()` function and scans every grammar question file for the Cr17Sweep v5 criterion: does a normalised `any_phrase` appear as a whole word in the prompt, where it isn't the item's sole answer? It also catches the second-purpose class — answer-leaks, where the answer sits verbatim in the prompt's other clause and the student can copy it without knowing anything.

2,916 items across 33 files. 158 items fell into the harmless exclusion (single-markpoint items where copying the cue *is* the correct answer — the adjective_agreement class Cr17Sweep calibrated against). 33 raw flags remained, which triage into two buckets:

22 genuine answer-leaks across 18 items. The worst clusters are the trapassato_prossimo items (5 of them — the first clause models the exact auxiliary the student must reproduce, so "era" in "non c'era più" gives away the auxiliary markpoint for free), the congiuntivo fixed formulas (3 items — "costi quel che ____" has the answer "costi" staring at you in the other half), and the pronoun items (6 items — "la" visible as an article in "la macchina" answers the pronoun blank). Reported speech has 2 items where "è" in the source clause is copyable.

11 by-design flags across 6 items — capitalisation discrimination tasks (the instruction lists la/La/sua/Sua as options), rewrite tasks (the source sentence necessarily contains the elements), and choice tasks ("Choose: andavo / sono andato"). These need a `gate_exempt` tag so future runs skip them.

The full report with every item ID, prompt, and suggested fix pattern is at `inter_chat/Architecture_Cr17Sweep_estate_net_gate_report.md`. Architecture status updated with the routing as the next queue item; Cr17Sweep's commission is marked discharged — the seat stands idle as designed.

That clears the Architecture queue's actionable items. What's left there is staged or blocked: item-shape review (needs the adjective_agreement one-pager), variant expansion (waiting on PronounAuthor), misconception rollout (waiting on MisconceptionAnalyst), jargon-label rewrite and explanation re-paragraph (both staged). The gate routing — dispatching those 18 items to their owning authors — is the next cut whenever you want it done.

Done. The estate-net gate is built, calibrated, and its first full-estate run is complete with findings reported and status files updated.

1524	
1525	    // Branch on item type: free-text vs multiple-choice.

1532	      const choicesHost = document.createElement("div");

1545	        choiceLabel.textContent = choiceText;
1546	        btn.appendChild(choiceLabel);

1548	          if (marked) return; // choice is locked once marked
1549	          selectedChoiceIdx = idx;
1550	          choiceButtons.forEach((b, j) => b.classList.toggle("selected", j === idx));

1563	          }

1569	      // Card-level keydown for MCQ: number keys 1-N select the corresponding

1572	      card.addEventListener("keydown", e => {
1573	        if (!/^[1-9]$/.test(e.key)) return;

1575	        if (idx < 0 || idx >= choiceButtons.length) return;

1578	        const tag = (e.target && e.target.tagName) || "";

1580	        e.preventDefault();
1581	        selectedChoiceIdx = idx;
1582	        choiceButtons.forEach((b, j) => b.classList.toggle("selected", j === idx));
1583	        choiceButtons[idx].focus();
1584	      });
1585	      // MCQ items don't render vocab-help / accent-bar / slash-menu.
1586	      // Decision (Bug 1a, 2026-05-28): on a forced-choice item the learner
1587	      // is picking between fully-rendered choices, not retrieving a word
1588	      // from memory. Vocab help would only obscure the diagnostic.

5137	  function renderPromptElement(promptText, item, vocabHelpsUsedRef) {
5138	    const div = document.createElement("div");
5139	    div.className = "prompt";
5140	
5141	    // Build a lemma index from rich vocab_help entries
5142	    const rich = (item.vocab_help || []).filter(e => e.lemma && e.aspects);
5143	    const lemmaMap = new Map();
5144	    for (const e of rich) lemmaMap.set(e.lemma.toLowerCase(), e);
5145	
5146	    // MCQ items: skip the EN/IT segmenter. The choice text is rendered
5147	    // separately as buttons, so the prompt is typically a short question
5148	    // that doesn't benefit from segmentation and can mis-split if the
5149	    // author baked choice markers into the prompt text (see inter_chat/
5150	    // Architecture_Housing_mcq_segmenter_fallback.md).
5151	    //
5152	    // NOTE on wrapping: `.qcard .prompt` is a flex column. Appending
5153	    // inline content (text nodes, spans) DIRECTLY to it would make each
5154	    // token an anonymous flex item, breaking each word onto its own row.
5155	    // We wrap inline content in a single child div so the flex container
5156	    // sees one child; the child renders text inline as normal.
5157	    // (See inter_chat/Architecture_Housing_segmenter_quote_anchoring.md.)
5158	    if (item && item.type === "mcq") {
5159	      const inlineBlock = document.createElement("div");
5160	      inlineBlock.className = "prompt-inline";
5161	      renderTextWithLemmas(inlineBlock, promptText, lemmaMap, item, vocabHelpsUsedRef);
5162	      div.appendChild(inlineBlock);
5163	      return div;
5164	    }
5165	
5166	    const segments = segmentPrompt(promptText);
5167	
5168	    // Single segment: render as a plain block, preserving lemma-click behaviour.
5169	    if (segments.length <= 1) {
5170	      const inlineBlock = document.createElement("div");
5171	      inlineBlock.className = "prompt-inline";
5172	      renderTextWithLemmas(inlineBlock, promptText, lemmaMap, item, vocabHelpsUsedRef);
5173	      div.appendChild(inlineBlock);
5174	      return div;
5175	    }
5176	
5177	    // Multi-segment, grouped into visual LINES (live_round2 ask 2): a short
5178	    // operand quote ('me', 'a Roma') stays INLINE with the instruction that
5179	    // names it, so "the pronoun for 'me'" reads as one line. Only full
5180	    // clause quotes (containing the blank, or long) and the trailing cue
5181	    // stand alone - the cue must stay its own flex child for the order:2
5182	    // placement (cue_placement thread) to keep working.
5183	    const fill = (el, seg) => {
5184	      if (seg.kind === "italian") {
5185	        renderTextWithLemmas(el, seg.text, lemmaMap, item, vocabHelpsUsedRef);
5186	      } else if (seg.kind === "cue") {
5187	        // Cue-label heuristic (Architecture_Housing_cue_notation_renderer):  QoderWork 2026-07-22
5188	        // "Use X" fires ONLY for a single Italian citation form that is the
5189	        // learner's OPERAND. English glosses, aspect triggers, and context
5190	        // lemmas (auxiliary-fill items) get different treatment.
5191	        const txt = String(seg.text).trim();
5192	        const isMulti = /\s/.test(txt);
5193	        const endsVowel = /[aeiouàèéìòù]$/.test(txt.toLowerCase());
5194	        const looksItalian = !isMulti && endsVowel && !/^(usually|early|already|only|always|never|sometimes|often|now|today|here|there|where|when|why|how|still|just|also|very|more|less|quite|rather|enough|almost|about|again|once|twice)$/i.test(txt);
5195	        // Auxiliary-context: the cued lemma is context, not the operand.
5196	        const isAuxItem = item && Array.isArray(item.markpoints) &&
5197	          item.markpoints.some(mp => /auxiliary|agreement/.test(mp.bucket || ""));
5198	        let label;
5199	        if (isMulti) label = "Meaning: ";
5200	        else if (!looksItalian) label = "";
5201	        else if (isAuxItem) label = "Verb: ";
5202	        else label = "Use ";
5203	        const labelSpan = document.createElement("span");
5204	        labelSpan.className = "prompt-cue-label";
5205	        labelSpan.textContent = label;
5206	        const valSpan = document.createElement("span");
5207	        valSpan.className = "prompt-cue-word";
5208	        valSpan.textContent = txt;
5209	        el.appendChild(labelSpan);
5210	        el.appendChild(valSpan);
5211	      } else {
5212	        el.textContent = seg.text;
5213	      }
5214	    };
5215	    let line = null;
5216	    const flushLine = () => { if (line && line.childNodes.length) div.appendChild(line); line = null; };
5217	    for (const seg of segments) {
5218	      const standalone = seg.kind === "cue"
5219	        || (seg.kind === "italian" && (seg.text.indexOf("____") >= 0 || seg.text.length > 24));
5220	      if (standalone) {
5221	        flushLine();
5222	        const block = document.createElement("div");
5223	        block.className = "prompt-segment prompt-" + seg.kind;
5224	        fill(block, seg);
5225	        div.appendChild(block);
5226	      } else {
5227	        if (!line) { line = document.createElement("div"); line.className = "prompt-segment-line"; }
5228	        const span = document.createElement("span");
5229	        span.className = "prompt-segment prompt-" + seg.kind;
5230	        fill(span, seg);
5231	        line.appendChild(span);
5232	        line.appendChild(document.createTextNode(" "));
5233	      }
5234	    }
5235	    flushLine();
5236	    return div;
5237	  }
5238	
5239	  // Tokenise prompt text into kind-tagged segments. Three kinds:
5240	  //   italian: text inside paired single or double quotes
5241	  //   cue:     text inside parentheses (typically a verb cue at the end)
5242	  //   english: everything else (narration, directives)
5243	  // Italian apostrophes inside a quoted segment (l'ho, c'è) are preserved by
5244	  // checking that a closing quote candidate is followed by whitespace or
5245	  // punctuation, not by a letter.
5246	  function segmentPrompt(text) {
5247	    const segments = [];
5248	    let buf = "";
5249	    let mode = "english";
5250	    const len = text.length;
5251	    for (let i = 0; i < len; i++) {
5252	      const c = text[i];
5253	      const prev = i > 0 ? text[i - 1] : "";
5254	      const next = i < len - 1 ? text[i + 1] : "";
5255	      const atStart = i === 0;
5256	      const prevIsSpaceLike = /\s/.test(prev);

}
```

8	summary: r75 (QoderWork 2026-07-22) FIVE CHANGES. (1) SESSION SCORE REWORK: removed 70% correct/incorrect threshold; now raw marks_awarded/marks_possible accumulation; chip shades from white to deep olive rgba(61,74,28) by percentage; text flips white past 55%; reset button kept; CSS transition 0.4s. Smith: "no judgments, no thresholds, just keeping count." (2) KNOWN-WORDS-ONLY DRILL FILTER: new vocabFilter.knownOnly checkbox appears in all drill modes (gender/accent/spelling); practisedLemmas() builds a cached Set of all lemmas with ≥1 vocab event (either direction); gender drill deck filters to practised nouns; accent drill filters items whose correct-answer word (accent-stripped) matches a practised lemma. Smith: "a tick for just words I know." (3) IDENTITY STRIP: #identity-box moved from absolute inside header to a full-width thin strip ABOVE the header (insertBefore); CSS: flex, justify-content:flex-end, 12px, border-bottom. (4) RESIDUE SOFT SECOND-CHANCE: showResiduePrompt() intercepts residue-engine misses before commitResult; gentle banner "You had the right answer, plus extra word(s) (X). Want to trim it?" with "Let me trim it" (retry, refocus+select input) and "Mark as is" (commit the miss); fires at most once per item; Esc=retry, Enter=mark. (5) ERROR-ID QTYPE RENDERER: new type "error_id" in grammar cards; prompt sentence rendered as inline tappable word buttons (.errorid-words/.errorid-word); tap=immediate mark; keyboard 1-N selects, Enter marks; index-scored via buildMcqResult (choices synthesised from prompt words, answer_index=error_index); post-mark highlighting (.correct-pick/.wrong-pick/.reveal-correct); correction field surfaced in explanation; choice_tags misconception firing inherited. NO DATA YET — overregularisation_distractors_v1.json (216 records) is raw material for an authoring pass; renderer is live and waiting. ALSO: gender translation-credit rule confirmed ALREADY BUILT (markVocab lines 4716-4772: gender fires only on unambiguous signal, bare lemma leaves gender silent). Status note was stale. PRIOR: r74 (QoderWork 2026-07-22) AI MARKER QUALITY + PER-WORD COLOURING + VOCAB CREDIT FIX. Worker prompt (worker/src/index.ts): added rule 11 ANTI-HALLUCINATION GUARD (only report accent errors when the learner's form VISIBLY DIFFERS from the correct form in accent marks; if spelling matches reference exactly, do NOT report; when uncertain, stay silent) — fixes the "torneremmo" false accent accusation. Added rule 12 VOCABULARY vs GRAMMAR distinction (wrong word = vocabulary bucket, NOT gender/agreement; only fire gender bucket when the RIGHT word has wrong grammatical marking) — fixes "maglia" for "maglione" being tagged as a gender error instead of vocab. NEEDS WRANGLER DEPLOY. Housing (app.js + style.css): "You wrote" line now does PER-WORD colouring — each word checked against reference answers; words found in references render green (.yw-right), words not found render pink with soft red background (.yw-wrong). Falls back to single-colour when no references available. CSS: .yw-word/.yw-right/.yw-wrong added. VOCAB CREDIT FIX (translation_marker.js): IT→EN vocab injection now uses accent-stripped 3-char stem matching + startsWith containment, so conjugated forms (dirò→dire, vedo→vedere, glie→gli) correctly inject vocab recognition buckets into bucket_context. Previously only exact lemma or 4-char prefix matched, missing most conjugated verbs. TOKEN NOTE: 55749 input tokens is real — the 581-entry cross-topic menu (translation_marker_bucket_menu.json) is ~13k tokens alone, plus per-item vocab injections for IT→EN. Architecture may want to trim the menu or use prompt caching. PRIOR: r73 (QoderWork 2026-07-22) ACCENT DRILL SHIPPED. Variant pipeline run: tools/expand_accent_seeds.js regenerated data/grammar_questions_accent.json (276 items: 85 pick_bare, 33 pick_context, 158 judge; flat array format matching grammar_questions_*.json convention). Loader wired: accent file added to tail Promise.all singleton loads, merged into grammar array after per-topic flatten. UI built: 4th mode chip "Accent drill" in filter-mode-row; buildAccentDeck() filters grammarQuestions by subtopic "accent.*"; renderAccentDrillCard() with shuffled choice positions (no position bias), neutral meta header (no class reveal), keyboard 1-N + click, correct/wrong highlight, explanation reveal, buildMcqResult scoring. vocabFilter.accentDrill flag added. Filter bar shows compact info bar in accent mode. PRIOR: r72 (QoderWork 2026-07-22) EIGHT CHANGES from Smith's live-session feedback. (1) SPELLING DRILL SHUFFLE: choices now shuffled at render time (order array + correctDisplayIdx mapping) so the correct answer isn't always position 2. (2) SPELLING DRILL HEADER: removed error-class label from per-card meta (was "Spelling drill · Digraphs (gl/gn/sc)" — gave away the pattern); now just "Spelling drill". (3) GENDER AXES TICKS/CROSSES: replaced confusing "avg 20%" text with ✓/✗ symbols per touched lemma (up to 12 shown); cell background still colours by mastery. (4) GENDER DRILL COMPACT: choices now 2-col CSS grid (7 options in 4 rows not 7); font 13px, tighter padding; declension feedback moved ABOVE actions row (was below Next button, invisible); .gd-declension now 16px bold with 4px accent left-border. (5) MID-SESSION MODE SWITCHER: filter bar now has a .filter-mode-row with three pill chips (Words / Gender drill / Spelling drill) allowing mode switch without returning to entry screen. (6) FILTER BAR GUARDS: spelling drill shows compact info bar (no Direction/Restrict/Sub-band); gender drill hides Direction selector (irrelevant to classifier). (7) DEAD CODE REMOVAL: rgbToHex, hexToRgb, paletteAsJson, buildNumberInput, buildColourInput all removed (unused since palette-tuning row retired Smith 2026-07-20/21). (8) CUE NOTATION RENDERER (Architecture_Housing_cue_notation_renderer v1+v2): "Use X" now fires ONLY for single Italian citation forms (ends in vowel, not in English blocklist); multi-word cues get "Meaning:"; English triggers get no prefix; auxiliary-fill items get "Verb:" (context, not operand). Resolves ~138 items rendering "Use usually" etc. ALSO: spelling drill resultHost moved above actions (feedback visible immediately). ALSO: filter-bar sticky top fixed to 110px desktop / 78px mobile (was top:0, tucked behind header). PRIOR: r71 (QoderWork 2026-07-22) SIX CHANGES. (1) UNCATEGORISED NOUN CLEANUP: nounClassFallback() helper classifies by ending (-o/m->regular_o_masc, -a/f->regular_a_fem, -e->e_ambiguous); labels: unspecified_m->"Masculine", unspecified_f->"Feminine", unspecified->"—". All three fallback sites updated. (2) SPELLING DRILL: new vocab-strand drill mode using SpellingAuthor's 141 MCQ items. Entry-screen toggle + 8 category chips (doubling, digraph, c_g_softening, qu_cu_cqu, silent_h, apostrophe_elision, capitalization, vowel_confusion). buildSpellingDeck() + renderSpellingDrillCard() with numbered MCQ choices, keyboard 1-N, correct/wrong highlight, explanation reveal, buildMcqResult scoring (choice_tags misconception firing inherited r68). Mutually exclusive with gender drill. CSS .spelling-choices/.spelling-choice. (3) OPEN ANALYSIS + PROFICIENT NOTE right-aligned: .live-controls now flex-column align-items:flex-end; .analysis-open-link width:auto text-align:right; .proficient-note margin/border removed. (4) 9-BOX PULSE STRIP: replaced old 3x3 cell grids + slivers + mini boxes with 9 full-width boxes (5 vocab 1k-5k + ACC + STR + GEN + SP). Each box: gradient background by mastery (linear-gradient green alpha), individual flash firing on relevant events, vocab boxes click-through to vocab strand. PRIOR: r65 VANISH FIX - REAL cause (Smith was right; my r64 flanking-dilution diagnosis was WRONG). The dots that 'flashed then vanished' in the 1000-2000 FOCUSED block were HOMOGRAPH RANK COLLISIONS: 480 ranks estate-wide (175 in 1000-2000) carry >1 entry (largo+toccare @1015, nave+presente @1004, ...), and byRank kept only the FIRST entry per rank, so practising any non-first entry never lit its dot - it flashed (by data-rank) then reverted to untouched (first entry has no events). FIX: byRankAllMap() (rank->ALL entries) + eventsForRank(rank,dir) unions every entry's events chronologically; focused dots, flanking cells and recolourAxisCellsFor all aggregate per-rank now. ALSO fixed a MIX-mode bug: activeDir mapped 'mix'->active-only (hid every passive event); now vocabHeatmapDir() => 'any' for mix/unset. VERIFIED node --check + merge harness (sister practice lights the dot; recency order; single-entry fast path). r64 (recolour-in-place on mark instead of full rebuild; keepAxes on Next) STILL STANDS - correct for perf, just aimed the vanish story at the wrong cells. THEME DRILL diagnosed, NOT yet fixed (task 10): over-threshold theme (colours) expands on click1, filters on click2; once vocabFilter.theme set the axis HIDES every other theme cell (restriction-focus = Smith's own 2026-07-20 ask) -> feels like a new page, heavy border, only the slow 'Back to all' to escape; reclick-to-clear exists but Smith can't find/use it. RULING NEEDED: keep restriction-focus + add a fast one-click clear on the active cell, or drop it (dim others instead). NEEDS r65 PUSH. PRIOR: r64 VOCAB PERF + VANISH FIX (Smith direct). SLOWNESS root cause: the per-answer FULL heatmap rebuild. doMark called renderVocabAxes() (teardown+rebuild of ~990 focused dots + ~20 flanking blocks, each dot an eventsForEntry lookup + click listener) on EVERY mark, and renderVocab() rebuilt it again on EVERY Next. NOW: doMark recolours ONLY touched cells in place via new recolourAxisCellsFor(entry) (focused dot + its flanking cell + gender/theme lemma dots; sets SETTLED bg, not just a flash); renderVocab(keepAxes) + doNext->renderVocab(true) skips the rebuild on advance; full rebuild now only on strand-enter / filter change. VANISH: focused-grid hits already persisted (scrittore: 1 event->1.0->dark green); the 'flashed then vanished' was FLANKING cells - a cell aggregates ~9 lemmas so one hit dilutes to ~0.29, and baseline==yellowStop(0.2) made touched read as untouched (disponibile). FIX: recolour sets the true aggregate AND adds .has-practice (inset green border) to any flank/lemma cell containing practice; also added in renderFlankingStripes so it survives full rebuilds. PRIOR: r61 + WORKER FIX - the AI marker IS working; the red box (r61) revealed the real cause: DeepSeek wraps its JSON in a ```json ... ``` markdown fence, and worker/src/index.ts did a BARE JSON.parse(content) -> malformed_json -> housing fell back to the stub. Fixed worker/src/index.ts (~L451): strip the code fence, and as a fallback extract the outermost {...}, before JSON.parse. CRITICAL: this is a WORKER change - needs `wrangler deploy` from the worker/ folder (or the EdTech/worker owner), NOT the GitHub push that deploys the housing site. After the worker redeploys, the real per-skill AI marking fires. r61 surfaced the failure reason (was erased by resultHost reset). r60 stats-pane top pinned + last-10 coloured. r59 re-mark exploit locked. PRIOR: AI TRANSLATION MARKER 'not firing' diagnosed + partly a silent-failure bug of MINE: when a worker URL is set (toggle 'on'), markTranslationLive IS called, but on ANY error the catch wrote a 'Live marker failed' note that the very next `resultHost.innerHTML=""` ERASED before render - so the AI was failing and the learner only ever saw the stub ('real AI marker will give per-skill feedback'), silently. Now the failure reason is CAPTURED (aiError) and shown above the stub result in a red .marker-ai-error box. So on next push+mark, Smith will see the ACTUAL cause (network / HTTP / worker error) - likely a worker deploy or URL issue, which is worker/EdTech-side not Housing code. r60 stats-pane top pinned + last-10 coloured. r59 re-mark exploit locked. Let me write a concise summary for Smith. I shouldn't over-explain. The key points are:

Let me keep it concise.

Fixed. That was my bug from the r75 error-ID work.

When I added the error-ID branch, I wrapped the prompt element creation in an `if/else`:

```js
} else {
  const promptEl = renderPromptElement(...);   // now scoped to this block only
  card.appendChild(promptEl);
}
```

But further down, the free-text branch still inserts the vocab-help bar relative to it:

```js
if (helpBar) card.insertBefore(helpBar, promptEl.nextSibling);
```

`promptEl` was now out of scope there, so every free-text grammar item with vocab help threw `ReferenceError: promptEl is not defined` partway through building the card — before the card was ever appended to the page. That's why the question area was blank and the render halted (live stats never populated). `node --check` doesn't catch it because it's a runtime scope error, not a syntax error, which is why it slipped past.

The fix hoists the declaration to function scope (`let promptEl = null;` before the branch, assigned inside it). I've bumped the build label to `2026-07-22-r76` so you can confirm the fixed version has loaded — the footer should read r76 instead of r70.

It needs a push to take effect (r64–r76 are all unpushed). Once you push and reload, the grammar card should render again.

Key insight: the `kaikki.org-dictionary-Italian.jsonl` file is a large data file (Wiktionary dump) used by the stress pipeline. It's over 100MB. GitHub refuses files over 100MB.

What should we do?

Options:
1. **Don't commit the file** — add it to `.gitignore`. This is a downloaded data file (from kaikki.org), so it can be re-downloaded. It shouldn't be in the repo. This is the cleanest solution.
2. **Use Git LFS** — more complex, requires LFS setup.
3. **Commit anyway** — bad, will break pushes.

The right move is option 1: add the file (or the whole `lexicon_data/` directory, or `*.jsonl` within it) to `.gitignore` and unstage it. Since it's a downloaded data file, it doesn't belong in version control.

```json
---README---
# Italian Stress + Syllable Pipeline [created by Antigravity]

Tiered stress assignment and syllabification for the StressAuthor drill.
Commissioned by Architecture (2026-07-21); spec ratified across
`inter_chat/Architecture_StressAuthor_data_spec.md` v1-v4.

## Quick Start

```bash
# From project root:

# 1. (Optional) Download lexicon data for Tier 4 coverage
python tools/stress_pipeline/prepare_lexicon_data.py

# 2. Run the full pipeline
python tools/stress_pipeline/run_pipeline.py

# 3. Or run individual steps:
python tools/stress_pipeline/stress_pipeline.py      # Tiers 1-5, lemma sidecar
python tools/stress_pipeline/wordform_stress.py      # Wordform layer
python tools/stress_pipeline/validate_seed.py        # Seed validation (58 entries)
python tools/stress_pipeline/generate_report.py      # Coverage report
```

## Architecture

### Tiered Sourcing (each later tier confirms the earlier)

| Tier | Source | Confidence | Coverage |
|------|--------|------------|----------|
| 1 | **Orthographic** — final written accent IS the stress | high | All tronche with accents |
| 2 | **Derivational suffix** — suffix fixes the class (-zione, -mente, -issimo, etc.) | high | Suffixed words |
| 3 | **Inflectional paradigm** — verb stress by conjugation class | high* | All verbs |
| 4 | **Pronunciation lexicon** — Wiktionary IPA / espeak-ng | high | Lexical residue |
| 5 | **Etymological prior** — Greek/Latin learned-word heuristic | low | Guess only |

*Except the -ere infinitive split (stem vs end stressed), which is lexical.

### Confidence Discipline

- **High**: orthographic mark, confirmed suffix rule, dictionary-verified
- **Medium**: espeak-ng fallback, plausible but unconfirmed
- **Low**: rule_default (penult assumed), etymological guess — **NOT drilled**

### Output Fields (per entry)

| Field | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `stress_pos` | int 1-4 | Position from END: 1=tronca, 2=piana, 3=sdrucciola, 4=bisdrucciola |
| `syllables` | string[] | Ordered syllable array: `["cit", "tà"]` |
| `syllable_count` | int | Stored for report filtering by word length |
| `stress_source` | enum | Evidence: `orthographic_mark \| dictionary \| rule \| author \| derived \| rule_default` |
| `stress_confidence` | enum | `high \| medium \| low` |
| `stress_mechanism` | enum | Cause (diagnostic axis): `orthographic \| derivational \| inflectional \| lexical` |
| `stress_mechanism_detail` | string | Sub-type: `accent_final`, `suffix_zione`, `infinitive_ere_stem`, etc. |
| `etymological` | bool | Greek/Latin learned-word flag |
| `accent_cue` | bool | Stress is orthographically signalled (written accent) |
| `stress_tags` | string[] | Secondary memberships: `["derivational:suffix_ta", "lexical:loanword"]` |

## Files

Sorted. Here's what was happening and what I did.

That one file — `tools/stress_pipeline/lexicon_data/kaikki.org-dictionary-Italian.jsonl` — is **726MB**. It's a Wiktionary dictionary dump that the stress pipeline *downloads* from kaikki.org (`prepare_lexicon_data.py` fetches it; the README calls it "(Optional) Download lexicon data for Tier 4 coverage"). It's re-downloadable input data, not source code, so it should never live in the repo — and GitHub flatly refuses files over 100MB, which is the "Files too large" dialog you hit.

I added `tools/stress_pipeline/lexicon_data/` to `.gitignore`. Git now ignores that whole directory, so the file no longer shows up as a change and can never be accidentally committed. I verified it: `git check-ignore` confirms it's ignored and it's gone from `git status`.

The good news on your code: the commit containing everything — including the r76 grammar-card fix — is already pushed. I fetched and confirmed your branch is exactly even with `origin/main`, and the fix (build label `2026-07-22-r76` + the `promptEl` repair) is in it. So once GitHub Pages finishes rebuilding, the grammar practice page will work again.

What you need to do in GitHub Desktop: you'll now see `.gitignore` as the only changed file. Commit it (any message) and push — the "Files too large" warning won't come back. That's the whole job.

One note on your second screenshot (Vocabulary showing "No vocab entries loaded"): that's unrelated to the git issue. It happens because that tab was opened as a local file (`file://`) — the vocab frequency list is loaded with `fetch()`, which only works over http. The frequency files are all committed to the repo fine, so the deployed site loads vocab normally. If you want vocab working while testing locally, run `python -m http.server` in the `housing` folder and open `http://localhost:8000`. That screenshot also still shows build r70, i.e. a pre-push load — a reload of the deployed site after the Pages rebuild will show r76.

stress questions are in.  codex finished them and the q surrogate now say.  Yes, there is a guide from the architect. Architecture replied at v5 in the thread (inter_chat/Architecture_StressAuthor_data_spec.md): your v4 spec was accepted in full, Code was commissioned via REQUEST_to_code_stress_pipeline.md, and the data has since landed. The Architecture→Housing thread (Architecture_Housing_accent_stress_and_new_qtypes.md) carries the qtype spec: tap-the-stressed-syllable, variable option count capped at 4, all diagnostic axes on the stats with toggleable membership/treatment, pulse extra_json with (true_pos, answered_pos, syllable_count).

The data is in. Code delivered two sidecar files: data/stress_sidecar_lemma.json (18,071 entries, 15,981 drillable at high confidence) and data/stress_sidecar_wordform.json (4,561, all high). The coverage report is at outputs/stress_coverage_report.md.

I generated the questions. data/grammar_questions_stress.json now holds 20,005 items, built by tools/stress_pipeline/generate_stress_items.js. Each item is an index-scored MCQ: the prompt is the bare word, the choices show it with each candidate syllable emboldened (**syl**), option count is min(syllable_count, 4), and answer_index = stress_pos − 1. All the diagnostic metadata (mechanism, detail, etymological, accent_cue, syllable_count, tags) rides in a stress_meta block for the report. Distribution: 13,028 piana / 4,424 sdrucciola / 2,513 tronca / 40 bisdrucciola. Every item passed a full validation pass (correct bolded syllable, correct option count, correct answer index).

One bug I caught and excluded: the pipeline misclassified all 12 lemma-layer "bisdrucciola" entries (spazzolino, telefonino, capitano-the-noun, etc. — these are piana or sdrucciola, not bisdrucciola). True bisdrucciole only exist at wordform level (the 3pl present of -are verbs like àbitano, tèlèfonano). I excluded the 12 bad lemmas from the items and flagged it in _status/StressAuthor.md for Code/Architecture to fix upstream.

What's not mine, still outstanding: Housing needs to wire the load path (singleton like the accent file), build the stress qtype renderer, the 4×4 confusion matrix report, and the pulse payload. Architecture still owes Vocab coordination (merging the sidecar fields onto canonical entries).

Task complete. The items are on disk, uncommitted, ready for your review and push.

1	# Architecture -> Housing: accent & stress report axes, two new qtypes, hover-text fix
2	
3	**From:** Architecture, 2026-07-21. Bundles the Housing build items from this session's accent/stress
4	ratifications and the CEFR harvest. Data pipelines are commissioned separately (REQUEST_to_code_stress_pipeline.md).
5	**Next:** Housing.
6	**Current version:** v1
7	
8	---
9	
10	## 1. Accent drill — report axes + qtype + pulse (taxonomy ratified 2026-07-21)
11	- Placement classes are now LEAVES under `orthography.accent.italian.placement` (meaning_pair / tense_bearing /
12	  che_compound / lexical_final / no_accent / apostrophe_not_accent). The coverage matrix should surface per-class mastery.
13	- **Event contract:** every attempt emits `accent_type` (a e i o u with the mark at issue) x `placement_class` x
14	  `outcome` in {correct, omitted, wrong_kind, inserted}; map omitted->`.missing`, wrong_kind->`.wrong_mark`,
15	  inserted->`.added` (aligned with the r29 slip enrichment expected/written/accent_class/accent_chars).
16	  Insertions attribute to the type TYPED; wrong_kind carries BOTH chars (expected e-acute, written e-grave) so the
17	  acute<->grave direction is recoverable. `pron_effect` (stress_shift | vowel_quality | none) rides as an attribute.
18	- **qtype:** index-scored pick-the-accent (options displayed, learner picks). Day-one pulse tagging per the contract.
19	
20	## 2. Stress drill — report axes + tap qtype + pulse
21	- **qtype:** tap-the-stressed-syllable — embolden each candidate syllable, learner taps 1..N; option set = every
22	  PHONOLOGICALLY POSSIBLE position for that word only (variable count, capped at the last four; never render an
23	  impossible position). Ambient class labels (tronca/piana/sdrucciola/bisdrucciola) shown, never gated.
24	- **Report axes (all on the stats, membership AND treatment TOGGLEABLE):** `stress_mechanism`,
25	  `stress_mechanism_detail`, `etymological`, `accent_cue`, `syllable_count` band, and the secondary `stress_tags`.
26	  This is the "which detection cue is the learner missing" view.
27	- **Stress<->accent cross-analysis (Rev 27):** the final-accented class is shared with the accent strand; the
28	  report must separate "mis-stressed a SHOWN word" (perception) from "omitted the accent, hence mis-stressed"
29	  (production) — measure these rates, do not assume them.
30	- **pulse extra_json:** `(true_pos, answered_pos, syllable_count)` so the 4x4 row-normalised matrix reconstitutes.
31	
32	## 3. NEW qtype — error identification (from the Transparent harvest, Smith adopted)
33	Full sentence containing exactly one wrong word; the options are words DRAWN FROM the sentence; the learner picks
34	the error. Recognition, index-scored (no substring marking). Natural home for the over-regularisation distractor
35	bank (`incoming drafts/overregularisation_distractors_v1.json`). Author-facing shape to follow in the brief.
36	
37	## 4. Coverage-grid hover-text fix (Smith flagged live)
38	Now the verbs are split, the coverage grid's hover shows io / tu / noi / loro but does NOT say what each COLUMN
39	is. Add real column headers / a legend so the person bands are readable.
40	
```

  "generated_by": 500,
  "version": 500,
  "stress_meta": 500
}
```

#### Thinking

Now I understand the data format. Let me analyze:

**Data format of `grammar_questions_stress.json`:**
- 20,005 items
- Each item: `type: "mcq"`, `prompt` = the bare word, `choices` = array of words with the candidate syllable bolded via `**syl**`, `answer_index` = index of the correct choice
- `markpoints[0].bucket` = e.g. "phonology.stress.inflectional.infinitive_ere_stem"
- `choice_tags` = array parallel to choices, each element is null (for the correct choice) or `{class: "wrong_position", true_pos, answered_pos, misconception}`
- `stress_meta` = complete diagnostic block: unit, word, lemma, stress_pos (1=tronca...4=bisdrucciola, counted from the end), stress_class, syllable_count, syllables[], stress_source, stress_confidence, stress_mechanism, stress_mechanism_detail, etymological, accent_cue, stress_tags[]
- `topic: "phonology"`, `subtopic: "stress.*"`
- `info_display: "suppress"`

**Key observations:**
1. These are `type: "mcq"` — meaning they go through the existing MCQ renderer! But the choices contain `**syl**` markdown bold markers, which need to be rendered as bold. The current MCQ renderer does `choiceLabel.textContent = choiceText`, which would literally display `**es**sere` with the asterisks. So a dedicated stress renderer (or an extension of the MCQ renderer) is needed that parses `**...**` into `<strong>`.

2. The spec says "tap the stressed syllable — bold each candidate syllable, learner taps 1..N". So each choice is the whole word with a different syllable bolded. The learner picks the choice where the correct syllable is bolded.

3. `choice_tags` already has `true_pos` and `answered_pos` for each wrong choice — these feed the misconception firing (r68 MCQ misconception path) and the pulse payload.

Now, the spec (thread v1, section 2) says:
- qtype: tap the stressed syllable, bold each candidate syllable, learner taps 1..N; option set = all phonologically possible positions (variable count, max 4); ambient class label (tronca/piana/sdrucciola/bisdrucciola) is shown but never gates.
- Report axes (all shown in stats, membership and treatment toggleable): stress_mechanism, stress_mechanism_detail, etymological, accent_cue, syllable_count band, stress_tags.
- Stress↔accent cross-analysis.
- pulse extra_json: (true_pos, answered_pos, syllable_count).

So Housing's work is:
1. **Load path**: load `data/grammar_questions_stress.json` as a singleton, similar to the accent file, and merge it into the grammar questions array.
2. **Stress qtype renderer**: since the choices contain `**syl**` bold markers, we need to render them as bold. The existing MCQ renderer uses textContent (no bold). So we need one of:
   - A dedicated stress drill mode (similar to the accent drill) with its own renderer that parses `**`, or
   - An extension of the MCQ renderer to handle `**bold**`.
   
   Given that the spec calls this a distinct qtype ("tap the stressed syllable") and that the accent drill is a distinct mode, I think a dedicated stress drill mode is the right approach. But wait — these items are `type: "mcq"` with `topic: "phonology"`, `subtopic: "stress.*"`. They will get mixed into the general grammar deck unless filtered out.

   Let me reconsider. The accent drill (r73) filters `grammarQuestions` by `subtopic "accent.*"` and builds a separate deck. Similarly, the stress drill should filter by `subtopic "stress.*"`. And we should exclude these from the general grammar deck (so they don't appear as broken MCQs with literal asterisks in normal grammar practice).

   Actually — let me check how the accent drill handles this. Does the accent drill remove accent items from the general grammar deck? Let me look at the accent drill code and the grammar deck construction.

3. **Pulse payload**: add (true_pos, answered_pos, syllable_count) to extra_json for stress attempts.
4. **Stats axes / confusion matrix**: 4×4 row-normalized matrix + toggleable report axes. This is the "confusion matrix report" Smith mentioned.

This is quite a lot. Let me prioritize the core: load + renderer + scoring (getting the drill working), then pulse payload, then stats/confusion matrix.

#### Thinking

With this, I've completely understood the pattern of the accent drill. It's a wonderful template. Let me jot down the important points:

1. **Loader** (lines 916–951): The accent file is loaded via `Foptional` inside the `tail` Promise.all, and then merged into the `grammar` array. Do the same for the stress file.

2. **buildAccentDeck** (line 3044): Filters `grammarQuestions` by topic + subtopic prefix + type mcq, applies the knownDir filter, shuffles → accentDeck.

3. **renderAccentDrillCard** (line 3062): Renders into `vocab-host`, meta line, prompt, shuffled choices, commit via `buildMcqResult(q, origIdx)` + `LL.store.recordAttempt("vocab", ...)`, result + explanation, keyboard 1–N.

4. **renderVocab** (line 3179): Routes to the drill card based on the vocabFilter flag.

5. **filter-mode-row** (lines 2541–2620): The chips are Words / Gender / Spelling / Accent. Need to add a "Stress drill" chip.

Now, important considerations regarding the stress drill:

**A. The choices contain `**syl**` bold markers.** The accent drill uses `lbl.textContent = q.choices[origIdx]`, so `**` is displayed as-is. For stress, we need to parse `**...**` and render it as `<strong>`. So the stress renderer needs a helper that converts `pi**ù**` into `pi<strong>ù</strong>`.

**B. Excluding stress items from the general grammar deck.** The stress items are `type: "mcq"`, `topic: "phonology"`, `subtopic: "stress.*"`. If merged into the `grammar` array, they will appear in general grammar practice as MCQs with the literal `**` markers. That's bad. Let me check: does the general grammar deck exclude accent items? Let me check ensureGrammarDeck / the grammar deck construction.

Actually, accent items are `topic: "orthography"`, `subtopic: "accent.*"`. Are they excluded from the grammar deck? Let me check. If accent items appear in general grammar practice, they too would be rendered as MCQs (which is fine, since they don't have `**` markers). But stress items have `**` markers, so if rendered by the general MCQ renderer, the asterisks would appear as-is. So we must exclude stress items from the general grammar deck.

Let me check how the grammar deck is built and whether it filters. Let me look at ensureGrammarDeck.

**C. Scoring**: `buildMcqResult(q, origIdx)` — index scoring + firing misconceptions for choice_tags (r68). The stress items have choice_tags with true_pos/answered_pos/misconception. So buildMcqResult will fire misconceptions for wrong picks.

**D. Pulse payload**: The spec requires extra_json (true_pos, answered_pos, syllable_count). Let me check how recordAttempt and the pulse's extra_json work. choice_tags already has true_pos/answered_pos. And stress_meta has syllable_count. We need to get these into the attempt's extra_json. Let me look at store.js's recordAttempt and how extra_json is built.

Actually — the accent drill calls `LL.store.recordAttempt("vocab", {id, prompt}, rawAnswer, result)`. Let me check recordAttempt's signature and what it stores, and how the pulse's extra_json picks up fields. The spec requires (true_pos, answered_pos, syllable_count) in the pulse's extra_json.

Let me look at buildMcqResult to see what it puts into the result (does it capture the fired choice_tag?). Then look at recordAttempt / the pulse.

Let me read buildMcqResult and store.js's recordAttempt.

Let me also check the grammar deck construction (ensureGrammarDeck) to see whether accent items are excluded, and how to exclude stress items.

1868	  function buildMcqResult(q, pickedIdx) {
1869	    const possible = q.marks || 1;
1870	    const correct = pickedIdx === q.answer_index;
1871	    const points = Array.isArray(q.markpoints) ? q.markpoints : [];
1872	    const choice = q.choices[pickedIdx];
1873	    const expectedChoice = q.choices[q.answer_index];
1874	    const mpResults = [];
1875	    let awarded = 0;
1876	    for (const mp of points) {
1877	      const credit = (typeof mp.credit === "number") ? mp.credit : 1;
1878	      mpResults.push({
1879	        bucket: mp.bucket,
1880	        label: mp.label || "",
1881	        credit_weight: credit,
1882	        attempted_credit: 1,
1883	        correctness_credit: correct ? 1 : 0,
1884	        outcome: correct ? "hit" : "miss",
1885	        evidence: choice,
1886	        expected: expectedChoice
1887	      });
1888	      if (correct) awarded += credit;
1889	    }
1890	    if (awarded > possible) awarded = possible;
1891	    // MCQ wrong-answer misconception firing (Smith 2026-07-21: "lots of chats
1892	    // asking for mcq wrong answer misconception firing"). choice_tags is an
1893	    // array parallel to choices; each entry is null or {class, misconception,
1894	    // proposed}. On a WRONG pick whose choice carries a misconception id, record
1895	    // {id, bucket, evidence} so it persists as attempt.misconception_hits -
1896	    // exactly the r10 free-text guard mechanism, just index-keyed for MCQ.
1897	    const misconceptions = [];
1898	    if (!correct && Array.isArray(q.choice_tags)) {
1899	      const tag = q.choice_tags[pickedIdx];
1900	      if (tag && tag.misconception) {
1901	        misconceptions.push({
1902	          id: tag.misconception,
1903	          bucket: (points[0] && points[0].bucket) || (Array.isArray(q.buckets) && q.buckets[0]) || null,
1904	          evidence: choice,
1905	          proposed: !!tag.proposed
1906	        });
1907	      }
1908	    }
1909	    return {
1910	      raw_response: choice,
1911	      overall: {
1912	        attempted_overall: 1,
1913	        correctness_overall: correct ? 1 : 0,
1914	        marks_awarded: awarded,
1915	        marks_possible: possible,
1916	        status: correct ? "hit" : "miss",
1917	        summary: correct ? "Right" : ("Wrong - correct was \"" + expectedChoice + "\""),
1918	        explanation: q.explanation || null,

356	  function ensureGrammarDeck() {
357	    const sig = filterSig(grammarFilter);
358	    if (sig !== _grammarDeckSig || grammarDeck.length === 0) {
359	      grammarDeck = shuffle(applyFilter(grammarQuestions, grammarFilter));
360	      if (sig !== _grammarDeckSig) grammarIndex = 0;   // filter changed -> restart position
361	      _grammarDeckSig = sig;

365	  function ensureTranslationDeck() {
366	    const sig = filterSig(translationFilter);
367	    if (sig !== _translationDeckSig || translationDeck.length === 0) {
368	      translationDeck = shuffle(applyFilter(translationItems, translationFilter));
369	      if (sig !== _translationDeckSig) translationIndex = 0;
370	      _translationDeckSig = sig;
371	    }
372	  }
373	  function uniqueValues(arr, key) {
374	    const s = new Set();
375	    for (const x of arr) if (x[key]) s.add(x[key]);
376	    return Array.from(s).sort();
377	  }

381	  function drillChipLabel(filter) {
382	    const node = bucketIndex.byId[filter.bucketPath];

387	      LL.inFlightSuppress.buckets.forEach(b => {
388	        if (b === filterPath || b.startsWith(filterPath + ".")) touches = true;
389	      });
390	      if (touches) label = LL.inFlightSuppress.topicLabel;
391	    }
392	    return label;
393	  }
