```json
126	  function applyFilter(arr, filter) {
127	    const hasClauses = Array.isArray(filter.clauses) && filter.clauses.length > 0;
128	    const range = (Array.isArray(filter.cefrRange) && filter.cefrRange.length === 2)
129	      ? filter.cefrRange : null;
130	    const lvls = (Array.isArray(filter.cefrLevels) && filter.cefrLevels.length)
131	      ? filter.cefrLevels : null;
132	    return arr.filter(q => {
133	      if (lvls) {
134	        // Independent multi-select: membership in the OR-ed level set.
135	        if (!lvls.includes(cefrIndex(q.cefr_level_target))) return false;
136	      } else if (range) {
137	        const ci = cefrIndex(q.cefr_level_target);
138	        if (ci < range[0] || ci > range[1]) return false;
139	      } else if (filter.cefr && q.cefr_level_target !== filter.cefr) {
140	        return false;
141	      }
142	      // Translation which-way: keep only items translating INTO the chosen
143	      // language (target_lang). Grammar items carry no target_lang and grammar
144	      // never sets this, so it is a no-op there.
145	      if (filter.targetLang && q.target_lang !== filter.targetLang) return false;
146	      if (hasClauses) {
147	        if (!filter.clauses.some(c => itemMatchesClause(q, c))) return false;
148	      } else if (filter.topic && q.topic !== filter.topic) {
149	        return false;
150	      }
151	      // Drill bucketPath is an ADDITIONAL constraint applied in both branches, so
152	      // drilling into a bucket narrows a composed (clauses) session too, not only
153	      // a scalar one. See inter_chat/Architecture_Housing_drill_filter_not_applied.md.
154	      if (filter.bucketPath) {
155	        const buckets = getItemBuckets(q);
156	        if (!buckets.some(b => bucketUnder(b, filter.bucketPath))) return false;
157	      }
158	      return true;
159	    });
160	  }
161	
162	  // What the topic scope currently is, in learner language.
163	  function scopeLabelFor(filter) {
164	    if (Array.isArray(filter.clauses) && filter.clauses.length) return "Custom mix";
165	    if (filter.topic) return friendlyTopicLabel(filter.topic);
166	    return "";
167	  }
168	  function cefrLabelFor(filter) {
169	    if (Array.isArray(filter.cefrLevels) && filter.cefrLevels.length) {
170	      // Collapse to runs: [A1,A2,B1] -> "A1-B1"; [A1,B1] -> "A1, B1".
171	      const s = filter.cefrLevels.slice().sort((a, b) => a - b);
172	      const parts = [];
173	      let a = s[0], b = s[0];
174	      for (let k = 1; k <= s.length; k++) {
175	        if (k < s.length && s[k] === b + 1) { b = s[k]; continue; }
176	        parts.push(a === b ? CEFR_ORDER[a] : CEFR_ORDER[a] + "\u2013" + CEFR_ORDER[b]);
177	        if (k < s.length) { a = s[k]; b = s[k]; }
178	      }
179	      return parts.join(", ");
180	    }
181	    if (Array.isArray(filter.cefrRange) && filter.cefrRange.length === 2) {
182	      const a = CEFR_ORDER[filter.cefrRange[0]], b = CEFR_ORDER[filter.cefrRange[1]];
183	      return a === b ? a : a + "\u2013" + b;
184	    }
185	    return filter.cefr || "";
```

In other words, `applyFilter` does not exclude accent or stress items from the general grammar deck. That is, when "all topics" is selected, accent items (topic=orthography, subtopic=accent.*) and stress items (topic=phonology, subtopic=stress.*) end up included in the general grammar deck.

If the user selects "all topics" (filter.topic is empty), applyFilter returns all items including accent items. So accent items would show up in general grammar practice as MCQs.

Hmm, but is that actually the case? Let me reconsider. Actually, this might be existing behavior that Smith has accepted (accent items appearing in general grammar practice as regular MCQs). Since accent items don't have `**` markers, they render fine as MCQs ("which spelling is correct?"). So it's not a bug for accent.

But for stress items, the `**` markers would render literally in the general MCQ renderer. That's a problem. So stress items must be excluded from the general grammar deck.

Options:
1. Don't merge stress items into the `grammar` array at all — keep them in a separate `stressQuestions` array. Then they'd never appear in general grammar practice. The stress drill would use `stressQuestions` directly.
2. Merge into grammar but exclude in applyFilter / ensureGrammarDeck.

Option 1 is cleaner and safer. The accent items were merged into grammar (option 2-ish, merged but not excluded), but for stress, keeping them separate avoids the literal `**` problem entirely.

**Output:**

```json
1	/* ============================================================================
2	   Store: bucket / question / item registry plus attempt persistence.
3	   In-memory + localStorage. No backend.
4	   ============================================================================ */
5	
6	(function () {
7	  "use strict";
8	  const LL = window.LL || (window.LL = {});
9	
10	  const LS_KEY = "ll_state_v1";
11	
12	  function loadState() {
13	    try {
14	      const raw = localStorage.getItem(LS_KEY);
15	      if (!raw) return defaultState();
16	      const parsed = JSON.parse(raw);
17	      const merged = Object.assign(defaultState(), parsed);
18	      // State hygiene: drop events whose bucket field isn't a non-empty string.
19	      // Bad events accumulated from older code revisions or partial migrations
20	      // cause downstream renderers to throw TypeError. Log the count so we can
21	      // spot recurrence. See inter_chat/
22	      // Architecture_Housing_eventsForNode_TypeError_and_state_hygiene.md.
23	      let droppedEventCount = 0;
24	      for (const att of (merged.attempts || [])) {
25	        if (!Array.isArray(att.events)) { att.events = []; continue; }
26	        const before = att.events.length;
27	        att.events = att.events.filter(ev =>
28	          ev && typeof ev.bucket === "string" && ev.bucket.length > 0
29	        );
30	        droppedEventCount += before - att.events.length;
31	      }
32	      if (droppedEventCount > 0) {
33	        console.warn("[Linguics] State hygiene: dropped " + droppedEventCount +
34	          " malformed event(s) from loaded state (events without a valid bucket field).");
35	      }
36	      return merged;
37	    } catch (e) {
38	      console.warn("Could not parse localStorage state", e);
39	      return defaultState();
40	    }
41	  }
42	
43	  function defaultState() {
44	    return {
45	      user_cefr: "B1",
46	      attempts: [],            // { id, strand, question_id|item_id, raw_response, intent?, overall, markpoint_events, timestamp }
47	      drafts: [],              // bucket-proposal drafts from AI marker
48	      preferences: { show_arcane: false, show_decay: true }
49	    };
50	  }
51	
52	  function saveState(state) {
53	    localStorage.setItem(LS_KEY, JSON.stringify(state));
54	  }
55	
56	  // --- bucket tree ---
57	  function indexBuckets(arr) {
58	    const byId = {};
59	    for (const b of arr) byId[b.id] = Object.assign({ children: [] }, b);
60	    const roots = [];
61	    for (const b of Object.values(byId)) {
62	      if (b.parent_id && byId[b.parent_id]) byId[b.parent_id].children.push(b);
63	      else roots.push(b);
64	    }
65	    return { byId, roots };
66	  }
67	
68	  // --- attempts ---
69	  function recordAttempt(strand, item_or_question, raw, result, intent) {
70	    const state = LL.state;
71	    // Build events from markpoints, then filter out any that lack a valid
72	    // bucket string. Prevents bad events from accumulating in localStorage
73	    // and surfaces the upstream cause (a markpoint missing .bucket) via
74	    // console.warn. See inter_chat/
75	    // Architecture_Housing_eventsForNode_TypeError_and_state_hygiene.md.
76	    // Person tag (verb_formation_person_split v2): formation items carry a
77	    // structured `person` (1sg..3pl; explicit null on non-finite). Every
78	    // event of a person-carrying item is tagged so per-person mastery can
79	    // aggregate within a leaf. Items without the field produce untagged
80	    // events, exactly as before - the render side treats absent as unsplit.
81	    // Generalised for leaf-declared paradigms (paradigm_bands v1): "person"
82	    // for verb formation, "slot" for form-paradigm leaves (articles,
83	    // articulated prepositions, demonstratives, possessives). The leaf
84	    // DECLARES which field matters; the store just carries what the item has.
85	    const PARADIGM_FIELDS = ["person", "slot"];
86	    const rawEvents = (result.markpoints || []).map(mp => {
87	      const ev = {
88	        bucket: mp.bucket,
89	        attempted_credit: mp.attempted_credit,
90	        correctness_credit: mp.correctness_credit,
91	        outcome: mp.outcome,
92	        evidence: mp.evidence || mp.evidence_in_attempt,
93	        source: strand === "grammar" ? "engine" : "ai_stub",
94	        bucket_proposed: !!mp.bucket_proposed
95	      };
96	      for (const pf of PARADIGM_FIELDS) {
97	        if (item_or_question && item_or_question[pf] !== undefined) ev[pf] = item_or_question[pf];
98	      }
99	      return ev;
100	    });
101	    const events = rawEvents.filter(ev => {
102	      if (typeof ev.bucket !== "string" || ev.bucket.length === 0) {
103	        console.warn("[Linguics] recordAttempt: skipping event with bad bucket field", ev);
104	        return false;
105	      }
106	      return true;
107	    });
108	    // accent slips (grammar only)
109	    if (result.orthography) {
110	      for (const o of result.orthography) {
111	        const oe = {
112	          bucket: o.bucket,
113	          attempted_credit: 1,
114	          correctness_credit: 0,
115	          outcome: "miss",
116	          evidence: o.evidence,
117	          source: "engine_orthography"
118	        };
119	        // Classed slip payload (live_round2 ask 3 / pulse maximal payload):
120	        // which word, what they wrote, which accent characters, what class.
121	        for (const k of ["expected", "written", "accent_class", "accent_chars"]) {
122	          if (o[k] !== undefined) oe[k] = o[k];
123	        }
124	        events.push(oe);
125	      }
126	    }
127	    const attempt = {
128	      id: "att_" + Date.now() + "_" + Math.random().toString(36).slice(2, 8),
129	      strand,
130	      question_id: strand === "grammar" ? item_or_question.external_id : null,
131	      item_id: strand === "translation" ? item_or_question.external_id : null,
132	      raw_response: raw,
133	      intent: intent || null,
134	      overall: result.overall,
135	      events,
136	      timestamp: new Date().toISOString()
137	    };
138	    // candidate_tenses metadata (AUTHOR_BRIEF criterion 16, Phase 1; see
139	    // inter_chat/Architecture_Housing_candidate_tenses_tick.md). Recorded when
140	    // the item declares its candidate set, to feed per-context discrimination
141	    // stats. Phase 1 stores correct_tense + whether the learner got the item
142	    // right; chosen-wrong-tense attribution is Phase 2 (needs tense-tagged
143	    // must_not_include entries).
144	    if (Array.isArray(item_or_question.candidate_tenses)
145	        && item_or_question.candidate_tenses.length >= 2
146	        && item_or_question.correct_tense) {
147	      attempt.tense_meta = {
148	        candidate_tenses: item_or_question.candidate_tenses.slice(),
149	        correct_tense: item_or_question.correct_tense,
150	        was_right: result.overall.marks_possible > 0
151	          && result.overall.marks_awarded >= result.overall.marks_possible
152	      };
153	    }
154	    // candidate_forms metadata: same semantics as tense_meta above, for
155	    // non-tense discriminations (suo his/her/your-formal, questo/quello). See
156	    // inter_chat/Architecture_Housing_breadcrumb_defaults_and_candidate_forms.md.
157	    if (Array.isArray(item_or_question.candidate_forms)
158	        && item_or_question.candidate_forms.length >= 2
159	        && item_or_question.correct_form) {
160	      attempt.form_meta = {
161	        candidate_forms: item_or_question.candidate_forms.slice(),
162	        correct_form: item_or_question.correct_form,
163	        was_right: result.overall.marks_possible > 0
164	          && result.overall.marks_awarded >= result.overall.marks_possible
165	      };
166	    }
167	    // Item-level misconception tags from the firing guard (attribution only;
168	    // the stats axis is a later phase). See inter_chat/
169	    // Architecture_Housing_misconception_item_tags.md.
170	    if (Array.isArray(result.misconceptions) && result.misconceptions.length) {
171	      attempt.misconception_hits = result.misconceptions.slice();
172	    }
173	    // Residue review flag: marks this attempt for the question-review queue.  QoderWork 2026-07-22
174	    if (result.residue_review) {
175	      attempt.residue_review = result.residue_review;
176	    }
177	    state.attempts.push(attempt);
178	    LL.lastAttempt = attempt;   // read by the grammar pulse strip (live flashes)
179	    // Attempt pulse to the estate workbook (shared_login_and_pulse v1).
180	    // Fail-soft by contract: a pulse must never break marking or storage.
181	    try { if (LL.pulse) LL.pulse.reportAttempt(strand, item_or_question, result, attempt); } catch (e) {}
182	
183	    // Track any bucket proposals that came through
184	    for (const mp of (result.markpoints || [])) {
185	      if (mp.bucket_proposed) {
186	        state.drafts.push({
187	          proposed_id: mp.bucket,
188	          proposed_parent_id: mp.proposed_parent_id,
189	          proposed_label: mp.proposed_label,
190	          proposed_rationale: mp.proposed_rationale,
191	          first_seen_attempt: attempt.id,
192	          status: "pending",
193	          occurrences: 1
194	        });
195	      }
196	    }
197	    saveState(state);
198	    return attempt;
199	  }
200	
201	  // --- bucket stats ---
202	  function bucketStats(bucketId) {
203	    const events = [];
204	    for (const att of LL.state.attempts) {
205	      for (const ev of att.events) {
206	        if (ev.bucket === bucketId) events.push({ event: ev, attempt: att });
207	      }
208	    }
209	    if (events.length === 0) return null;
210	    // most recent K
211	    const K = 5;
212	    const recent = events.slice(-K);
213	    let attemptedSum = 0, correctSum = 0, respondedCount = 0;
214	    for (const r of recent) {
215	      attemptedSum += r.event.attempted_credit;
216	      if (r.event.correctness_credit !== null) { correctSum += r.event.correctness_credit; respondedCount++; }
217	    }
218	    return {
219	      bucket: bucketId,
220	      n_events: events.length,
221	      attempted_rate: attemptedSum / recent.length,
222	      correctness_given_attempt: respondedCount > 0 ? correctSum / respondedCount : 0,
223	      combined: (attemptedSum / recent.length) * (respondedCount > 0 ? correctSum / respondedCount : 0),
224	      last_seen: events[events.length - 1].attempt.timestamp
225	    };
226	  }
227	
228	  function allBucketStats() {
229	    const out = {};
230	    for (const att of LL.state.attempts) {
231	      for (const ev of att.events) {
232	        if (!out[ev.bucket]) {
233	          out[ev.bucket] = { bucket: ev.bucket, attempted: 0, correct: 0, n: 0 };
234	        }
235	        out[ev.bucket].n += 1;
236	        out[ev.bucket].attempted += ev.attempted_credit;
237	        if (ev.correctness_credit !== null) out[ev.bucket].correct += ev.correctness_credit;
238	      }
239	    }
240	    for (const k of Object.keys(out)) {
241	      const r = out[k];
242	      r.attempted_rate = r.n > 0 ? r.attempted / r.n : 0;
243	      r.correctness = r.attempted > 0 ? r.correct / r.attempted : 0;
244	      r.combined = r.attempted_rate * r.correctness;
245	    }
246	    return out;
247	  }
248	
249	  LL.store = {
250	    indexBuckets,
251	    recordAttempt,
252	    bucketStats,
253	    allBucketStats,
254	    saveState,
255	    loadState,
256	    LS_KEY
257	  };
258	})();
259	
```

#### Thinking

Now I understand store.js's recordAttempt. Key points:
- `recordAttempt(strand, item_or_question, raw, result, intent)` builds events from `result.markpoints`, and persists `attempt.overall`, `attempt.events`, `attempt.misconception_hits` (from result.misconceptions), `attempt.tense_meta`, `attempt.form_meta`, `attempt.residue_review`.
- Pulse: `LL.pulse.reportAttempt(strand, item_or_question, result, attempt)`.

Regarding the stress pulse payload (true_pos, answered_pos, syllable_count), there are two options:
1. Add `stress_meta` to the attempt record (like tense_meta/form_meta), and have the pulse's extra_json pick it up.
2. Pass the stress fields via result and have recordAttempt capture them.

The cleanest approach: add a `stress_meta` block to the attempt (similar to tense_meta). In recordAttempt, check `item_or_question.stress_meta` and persist the relevant fields. Then the pulse's extra_json can read from either attempt or item.

But wait — the pulse's extra_json. Let me check how pulse.js builds extra_json. The spec says "pulse extra_json: (true_pos, answered_pos, syllable_count)". Let me look at pulse.js's reportAttempt and check how extra_json is assembled and whether item_or_question's fields are automatically swept in.

From the Housing status: "extra_json was EMPTY in the live sheet... estate script only sweeps top-level keys -> payload was flattened". And "maximal extra_json at the store funnel". Let me look at pulse.js and understand how extra_json is built.

Actually, the accent drill passes `{ id: q.external_id, prompt: q.prompt }` as item_or_question — a minimal object. For stress, if I pass the full `q` object (which includes stress_meta and choice_tags), the pulse might sweep those into extra_json. But that could get large. Let me check pulse.js.

Let me read pulse.js's reportAttempt and extra_json assembly.

Actually, let me reconsider. The key fields for the confusion matrix are true_pos, answered_pos, syllable_count. These need to be persisted somewhere the report can read them. Two places:
1. **attempt record** (localStorage) — for the in-app confusion matrix report.
2. **pulse extra_json** — for the estate workbook.

For the in-app confusion matrix, I need to persist stress attempt data in localStorage. The best approach: add a `stress_meta` field to the attempt (similar to tense_meta) containing {true_pos, answered_pos, syllable_count, stress_class, mechanism, ...}. Then the confusion matrix report reads from attempts that have stress_meta.

Regarding true_pos/answered_pos:
- true_pos = q.stress_meta.stress_pos (the actual stress position, 1=final syllable...4).
- answered_pos = the position the learner chose. How do I compute it? The choices are ordered such that choice[0] has the first syllable bolded... no, wait. Let me look at the data.

Looking at the first item:
```
"prompt": "essere",
"choices": ["esse**re**", "es**se**re", "**es**sere"],
"answer_index": 2,
stress_meta: { stress_pos: 3, syllables: ["es","se","re"], stress_class: "sdrucciola" }
```

So choices[0] = "esse**re**" (last syllable "re" bolded = position 1 from the end = tronca position).
choices[1] = "es**se**re" (middle syllable "se" bolded = position 2 from the end = piana position).
choices[2] = "**es**sere" (first syllable "es" bolded = position 3 from the end = sdrucciola position).
answer_index = 2 → choices[2] = "**es**sere" (stress on "es", 3rd from the end). stress_pos = 3. ✓

So the choices are ordered such that choices[i] has the syllable at position (i+1) from the end bolded? Let me verify:
- choices[0]: "re" bolded. "re" is the last syllable = position 1 from the end. So choices[0] = position 1.
- choices[1]: "se" bolded. "se" is 2nd from the end = position 2. So choices[1] = position 2.
- choices[2]: "es" bolded. "es" is 3rd from the end = position 3. So choices[2] = position 3.

Yes! choices[i] has the syllable at position (i+1) from the end bolded. And answer_index = stress_pos − 1. Confirmed: answer_index=2, stress_pos=3, 3−1=2. ✓

So **answered_pos = pickedOrigIdx + 1** (the original, pre-shuffle choice index + 1). And **true_pos = stress_pos = answer_index + 1**.

Wait, but I need to double-check that the choice ordering is consistently position-based. Let me verify with the tronca item:
```
"prompt": "più",
"choices": ["pi**ù**", "**pi**ù"],
"answer_index": 0,
stress_meta: { stress_pos: 1, syllables: ["pi","ù"], stress_class: "tronca" }
```
- choices[0] = "pi**ù**" ("ù" bolded = last syllable = position 1). answer_index=0, stress_pos=1, 1−1=0. ✓
- choices[1] = "**pi**ù" ("pi" bolded = position 2).

So choices[i] = position (i+1) from the end. Consistent.

Therefore, for a learner who chose display index `displayIdx` (which maps to original index `origIdx = order[displayIdx]`):
- answered_pos = origIdx + 1
- true_pos = q.stress_meta.stress_pos (= q.answer_index + 1)
- syllable_count = q.stress_meta.syllable_count

Now, choice_tags already encodes true_pos and answered_pos for each wrong choice:
```
"choice_tags": [
  {"class":"wrong_position","true_pos":3,"answered_pos":1,"misconception":"stress.confusion.sdrucciola_as_tronca"},
  {"class":"wrong_position","true_pos":3,"answered_pos":2,"misconception":"stress.confusion.sdrucciola_as_piana"},
  null   // correct choice
]
```
So choice_tags[i] (for wrong choice i) has true_pos and answered_pos. The correct choice's tag is null.

So when the learner picks origIdx, if wrong, choice_tags[origIdx].true_pos and .answered_pos give the values. If correct, answered_pos = true_pos = stress_pos.

For the pulse payload and confusion matrix, I can compute:
- true_pos = q.stress_meta.stress_pos
- answered_pos = origIdx + 1 (works for both correct and wrong)
- syllable_count = q.stress_meta.syllable_count

This is robust. Let me use this.

Now, how to persist this. Let me add to recordAttempt: if item_or_question has stress_meta, persist a `stress_attempt` block on the attempt:
```js
if (item_or_question && item_or_question.stress_meta) {
  attempt.stress_attempt = {
    true_pos: item_or_question.stress_meta.stress_pos,
    answered_pos: <picked position>,
    syllable_count: item_or_question.stress_meta.syllable_count,
    stress_class: item_or_question.stress_meta.stress_class,
    mechanism: item_or_question.stress_meta.stress_mechanism,
    ...
  };
}
```

But recordAttempt doesn't know the picked index. It only receives `raw` (the raw response = the choice text) and `result`. So I need to pass answered_pos somehow.

Options:
1. Compute answered_pos in the renderer and pass it via `result` (e.g., result.stress = {true_pos, answered_pos, syllable_count}). Then recordAttempt reads result.stress.
2. Pass the full item (with stress_meta) and the picked index, and let recordAttempt compute it.

The cleanest approach: have the renderer build a `result.stress_meta` or attach the stress fields to the result, and have recordAttempt persist them. But buildMcqResult builds the result. Either add the stress fields after buildMcqResult returns, or pass them separately.

Actually, the simplest approach: in the stress renderer, after `const result = buildMcqResult(q, origIdx)`, attach `result.stress = {true_pos, answered_pos, syllable_count, ...}`. Then in recordAttempt, check `if (result.stress) attempt.stress_attempt = result.stress;`. This mirrors how residue_review works (result.residue_review → attempt.residue_review).

This is clean and follows existing patterns. Let me do this.

Then the confusion matrix report reads `attempt.stress_attempt` from LL.state.attempts.

Regarding the pulse extra_json: pulse.reportAttempt(strand, item_or_question, result, attempt) is called. Let me check how pulse builds extra_json — does it sweep attempt's fields, or item's fields? Let me read pulse.js.

Let me read pulse.js's reportAttempt.

Actually, from the Housing status history: "maximal extra_json at the store funnel" and "estate script only sweeps top-level keys -> payload was flattened (answer/expected at the front, jsons pre-stringified)". This suggests the pulse flattens the attempt/item into extra_json. Let me read pulse.js to be sure about how to get stress fields into extra_json.

Let me read pulse.js.

`recordAttempt` persists `result`-derived blocks (`misconception_hits`, `residue_review`) onto the attempt — I'll follow that pattern with a `stress` block. The choice ordering is position-based (`choices[i]` bolds the syllable at position `i+1` from the end, `answer_index = stress_pos − 1`), so `answered_pos = pickedOrigIdx + 1`. #### Thinking

Now I understand how the pulse payload works. `buildAttemptPayload` constructs the payload with top-level keys. To add (true_pos, answered_pos, syllable_count) to the pulse, I'll add them as top-level keys in buildAttemptPayload, reading from either `attempt.stress_attempt` (which recordAttempt persists) or from item/result.

The pulse receives `(strand, item, result, attempt)`. If I attach `result.stress = {true_pos, answered_pos, syllable_count}` in the renderer and have recordAttempt persist `attempt.stress_attempt = result.stress`, then in buildAttemptPayload I can read from either `attempt.stress_attempt` or `result.stress`. Let me read from `attempt.stress_attempt` (the persisted one), with a fallback to `result.stress`.

Let me add the following to buildAttemptPayload:
```js
// Stress drill diagnostics (Architecture_Housing_accent_stress_and_new_qtypes.md v1 §2):
// (true_pos, answered_pos, syllable_count) so the 4x4 row-normalised matrix reconstitutes.

payload.stress_answered_pos = sa ? sa.answered_pos : "";
payload.stress_syllable_count = sa ? sa.syllable_count : "";
```

### Piece 3: buildStressDeck() (near buildAccentDeck, line 3044)
```js
function buildStressDeck() {
  const stripAcc = s => s.replace(...)...;  // reuse
  let items = stressQuestions.filter(q => q.type === "mcq" && Array.isArray(q.choices) && q.choices.length);
  if (vocabFilter.knownDir) {
    const known = practisedLemmas(vocabFilter.knownDir);
    items = items.filter(q => {
      const word = String(q.prompt || "").toLowerCase().trim();
      return known.has(word) || known.has(stripAcc(word));
    });
  }
  stressDeck = shuffle(items);
  stressIndex = 0;
}
```

- Bold rendering helper:
```js
function renderBoldedText(container, text) {
  // Split on ** and alternate text/strong.
  const parts = String(text).split("**");
  parts.forEach((part, i) => {
    if (i % 2 === 1) {
      const strong = document.createElement("strong");
      strong.textContent = part;
      container.appendChild(strong);
    } else if (part) {
      container.appendChild(document.createTextNode(part));
    }
  });
}
```

- Post-mark: highlight correct/wrong (gc-correct/gc-wrong), show result + explanation.

Important design decisions for the stress renderer:
- The spec says "tap the stressed syllable — bold each candidate syllable, and the learner taps 1..N". The data already provides choices with each candidate syllable bolded via **syl**. So the renderer converts `**syl**` → `<strong>syl</` (via innerHTML or DOM construction).
- Shuffle the display order of choices (same as accent). answered_pos = origIdx + 1 (choices[i] bolds the syllable at position i+1 from the end). true_pos = q.stress_meta.stress_pos = q.answer_index + 1.
- result.stress = { true_pos, answered_pos, syllable_count, stress_class, ... } — attach it to result so that store.js can persist it as attempt.stress_attempt and pulse can read it.

```json
1877	  // MCQ scorer: builds a result with the same shape as markGrammar so the
1878	  // renderResult pipeline doesn't care which marker produced it.
1879	  function buildMcqResult(q, pickedIdx) {
1880	    const possible = q.marks || 1;
1881	    const correct = pickedIdx === q.answer_index;
1882	    const points = Array.isArray(q.markpoints) ? q.markpoints : [];
1883	    const choice = q.choices[pickedIdx];
1884	    const expectedChoice = q.choices[q.answer_index];
1885	    const mpResults = [];
1886	    let awarded = 0;
1887	    for (const mp of points) {
1888	      const credit = (typeof mp.credit === "number") ? mp.credit : 1;
1889	      mpResults.push({
1890	        bucket: mp.bucket,
1891	        label: mp.label || "",
1892	        credit_weight: credit,
1893	        attempted_credit: 1,
1894	        correctness_credit: correct ? 1 : 0,
1895	        outcome: correct ? "hit" : "miss",
1896	        evidence: choice,
1897	        expected: expectedChoice
1898	      });
1899	      if (correct) awarded += credit;
1900	    }
1901	    if (awarded > possible) awarded = possible;
1902	    // MCQ wrong-answer misconception firing (Smith 2026-07-21: "lots of chats
1903	    // asking for mcq wrong answer misconception firing"). choice_tags is an
1904	    // array parallel to choices; each entry is null or {class, misconception,
1905	    // proposed}. On a WRONG pick whose choice carries a misconception id, record
1906	    // {id, bucket, evidence} so it persists as attempt.misconception_hits -
1907	    // exactly the r10 free-text guard mechanism, just index-keyed for MCQ.
1908	    const misconceptions = [];
1909	    if (!correct && Array.isArray(q.choice_tags)) {
1910	      const tag = q.choice_tags[pickedIdx];
1911	      if (tag && tag.misconception) {
1912	        misconceptions.push({
1913	          id: tag.misconception,
1914	          bucket: (points[0] && points[0].bucket) || (Array.isArray(q.buckets) && q.buckets[0]) || null,
1915	          evidence: choice,
1916	          proposed: !!tag.proposed
1917	        });
1918	      }
1919	    }
1920	    return {
1921	      raw_response: choice,
1922	      overall: {
1923	        attempted_overall: 1,
1924	        correctness_overall: correct ? 1 : 0,
1925	        marks_awarded: awarded,
1926	        marks_possible: possible,
1927	        status: correct ? "hit" : "miss",
1928	        summary: correct ? "Right" : ("Wrong - correct was \"" + expectedChoice + "\""),
1929	        explanation: q.explanation || null,
1930	        examiner_note: q.examiner_note || null
1931	      },
1932	      markpoints: mpResults,
1933	      orthography: [],
1934	      misconceptions: misconceptions
1935	    };
1936	  }
```

#### Thinking

buildMcqResult returns a result object. In the stress renderer, after calling buildMcqResult, attach `result.stress = { true_pos, answered_pos, syllable_count, stress_class, word }`. Then store.js persists `result.stress` → `attempt.stress_attempt`, and pulse.js reads `attempt.stress_attempt` (or result) and puts it into top-level keys.

Wait — pulse's buildAttemptPayload takes (strand, item, result, attempt). It can read from attempt.stress_attempt. Good.

Load-path TODOs are done. Mark #1 as complete, #2 as in-progress. Next, write buildStressDeck + renderStressDrillCard. Insert after renderAccentDrillCard (ends around line 3189, before `function renderVocab` around line 3191).

Design based on spec (Architecture_Housing_accent_stress_and_new_qtypes.md §2):
- Tap the stressed syllable: each choice displays a word with candidate syllables bolded (**syl** → <strong>).
- Ambient class labels (tronca/piana/sdrucciola/bisdrucciola) are shown but never gated. That is, display the stress class as ambient info — but wait, wouldn't showing the class give away the answer? "Ambient class labels are shown but never gated" — my reading is: the UI can display what the classes mean (legend: 1=tronca (last), 2=piana, 3=sdrucciola, 4=bisdrucciola), but never gates access to items by class. Hmm, but showing "this word is piana" would give away the answer. Rereading: "Ambient class labels (tronca/piana/sdrucciola/bisdrucciola) are shown but never gated."

I think this means the position numbers correspond to class labels — that is, the legend shows what each position is called (last syllable = tronca, etc.) as ambient educational labeling, but the drill doesn't filter/gate by class. The accent drill's meta line says "Accent drill" neutrally — "don't reveal the placement class." For stress, showing a legend of position names is pedagogically useful and doesn't give away the answer (the learner still has to know which syllable of this word is stressed). Show a small legend: "1 = last (tronca) · 2 = second-to-last (piana) · 3 = third-to-last (sdrucciola) · 4 = fourth-to-last (bisdrucciola)". That's ambient class labeling, never gated.

- Bold rendering: choice text like "es**se**re" → need to render "es<strong>se</strong>re". Build DOM safely: split on ** markers. Write a helper `renderBoldedText(el, text)` that parses **...** segments into <strong> elements and puts the rest into text nodes. Safe (no innerHTML with raw data).

- Shuffle display order like accent. answered_pos = origIdx + 1. true_pos = q.stress_meta.stress_pos (== answer_index + 1).

- After commit: attach result.stress = { true_pos, answered_pos, syllable_count, stress_class, word }. recordAttempt("vocab", {id, prompt}, raw, result). store.js persists attempt.stress_attempt = result.stress.

- Pulse: top-level keys stress_true_pos, stress_answered_pos, stress_syllable_count (per spec: pulse extra_json (true_pos, answered_pos, syllable_count)).

- Meta line: "Stress drill" + ambient legend. Prompt: bare word (q.prompt).

- Explanation shown after commit (q.explanation).

- Keyboard 1-N.

Also need a `stressDeck`/`stressIndex` module state, and knownDir filtering like accent (practisedLemmas). The word for known filtering: q.stress_meta.word or q.prompt.

Next, the mode chip. Read the filter-mode-row area (2540-2617) to wire up the chip, inDrill, and info bar.

```json
  function renderVocabFilterBar() {
    const host = document.getElementById("vocab-filter-bar");
    if (!host) return;
    host.innerHTML = "";

    // Mode switcher: allows mid-session toggling between vocab / gender / spelling / accent / stress.  QoderWork 2026-07-22 (stress 2026-07-23)
    const modeRow = document.createElement("div");
    modeRow.className = "filter-mode-row";
    const modes = [
      { key: "vocab", label: "Words" },
      { key: "gender", label: "Gender drill" },
      { key: "spelling", label: "Spelling drill" },
      { key: "accent", label: "Accent drill" },
      { key: "stress", label: "Stress drill" }
    ];
    const activeMode = vocabFilter.stressDrill ? "stress" : vocabFilter.accentDrill ? "accent" : vocabFilter.spellingDrill ? "spelling" : vocabFilter.genderDrill ? "gender" : "vocab";
    modes.forEach(m => {
      const chip = document.createElement("button");
      chip.type = "button";
      chip.className = "filter-mode-chip" + (m.key === activeMode ? " active" : "");
      chip.textContent = m.label;
      chip.addEventListener("click", () => {
        if (m.key === activeMode) return;
        vocabFilter.genderDrill = m.key === "gender";
        vocabFilter.spellingDrill = m.key === "spelling";
        vocabFilter.accentDrill = m.key === "accent";
        vocabFilter.stressDrill = m.key === "stress";
        if (m.key === "spelling") buildSpellingDeck();
        if (m.key === "accent") buildAccentDeck();
        if (m.key === "stress") buildStressDeck();
        vocabDeck = []; vocabIndex = 0;
        renderVocab();
      });
      modeRow.appendChild(chip);
    });
...
```

```json
    // Stress drill: uses its own MCQ deck, not vocab entries.  QoderWork 2026-07-23
    if (vocabFilter.stressDrill) { renderStressDrillCard(keepAxes); return; }
    if (!vocabEntries.length) {
      const msg = document.createElement("p");
      msg.className = "muted";
      msg.style.cssText = "padding:18px;font-style:italic";
      msg.textContent = "No vocab entries loaded. Serve the project over http (e.g. python -m http.server) and reload to fetch the frequency list.";
...
```

#### Thinking

Next, the core part: buildStressDeck + renderStressDrillCard. Insert it after renderAccentDrillCard (which ends with `if (!keepAxes) renderVocabAxes(); }` right before `function renderVocab(keepAxes) {`).

Design:
- `let stressDeck = []; let stressIndex = 0;`
- buildStressDeck(): filter stressQuestions by type==="mcq", apply knownDir filtering via practisedLemmas against the word (q.stress_meta.word or q.prompt), then shuffle.
- renderStressDrillCard(keepAxes): model it on the accent card, but with the following differences:
  - Meta line: "Stress drill" + ambient legend of position names (tronca/piana/sdrucciola/bisdrucciola) — displayed but never used as a gate. Spec: "ambient class labels (tronca/piana/sdrucciola/bisdrucciola) are displayed, never gated."
  - Prompt: bare word (q.prompt).
  - Choices: render `**syl**` as <strong>. Helper appendBoldedText(el, text).
  - Shuffle display order; commit(displayIdx) → origIdx = order[displayIdx]; result = buildMcqResult(q, origIdx); then attach result.stress = { true_pos, answered_pos, syllable_count, stress_class, word }.
    - answered_pos = origIdx + 1 (choices[i] bolds the syllable at position i+1 from the end).
    - true_pos = (q.stress_meta && q.stress_meta.stress_pos) || (q.answer_index + 1).
    - syllable_count = q.stress_meta.syllable_count || q.choices.length... note: the number of choices is min(syllable_count, 4), so syllable_count must be taken from stress_meta.
  - recordAttempt("vocab", {id: q.external_id, prompt: q.prompt}, q.choices[origIdx], result).
  - After commit: show correct/incorrect styling, renderResult, explanation, renderLiveStats, Next button.
  - Keyboard 1..N.

Wait — one design question: should the legend reveal the class name per position? Position 1 = last syllable = tronca, 2 = piana, 3 = sdrucciola, 4 = bisdrucciola. The spec says the ambient class labels are "displayed, never gated." My interpretation: the learner can see a legend mapping positions to class names (educational), but the drill does not filter by class. This doesn't give away the answer — the learner still has to know which syllable of this word is stressed. The accent drill hides the placement class in the meta line ("neutral — don't reveal the placement class"), but that's because in the accent drill the class itself is close to the answer axis. In the stress drill, the class labels are the position names themselves — showing "2 = piana (second from last)" is just explaining what the positions mean. Hmm, but actually, showing "this word is a piana" would give away the answer. I won't show the word's class, only the legend of what each position number is called. That's the safe interpretation of "ambient class labels displayed, never gated" — the labels exist in the environment (as a legend), but nothing is gated by them.

Actually, let me reconsider once more. "Ambient class labels (tronca/piana/sdrucciola/bisdrucciola) are displayed, never gated." Another reading: after answering, display which class the word belongs to (ambient feedback), but don't gate progression on it. The explanation already contains the class ("essere is sdrucciola: ..."). A legend mapping positions to class names is pedagogically useful and consistent with either reading. I'll include a small legend line. Keep it understated (faint).

Also need a helper for rendering bolded text. Write it like this:

```js
function appendBoldedText(el, text) {
  // Render **syl** markers as <strong>; everything else as plain text nodes.
  // Data-driven but DOM-safe (no innerHTML).  QoderWork 2026-07-23
  const parts = String(text == null ? "" : text).split("**");
  for (let i = 0; i < parts.length; i++) {
    if (parts[i] === "") continue;
    if (i % 2 === 1) {
      const s = document.createElement("strong");
      s.textContent = parts[i];
      el.appendChild(s);
    } else {
      el.appendChild(document.createTextNode(parts[i]));
    }
  }
}
```

```js
result.stress = {

  answered_pos: origIdx + 1,
  syllable_count: (typeof meta.syllable_count === "number") ? meta.syllable_count : (q.choices || []).length,
  stress_class: meta.stress_class || "",
  word: meta.word || q.prompt || ""
};
```

knownDir filtering: use practisedLemmas(vocabFilter.knownDir); word = (q.stress_meta && q.stress_meta.word) || q.prompt. The accent version also strips accents; for stress the words are bare (mostly no accents in the lemma, though tronche like "città" do have them). For robustness, apply the same stripAcc fallback. Actually, let me keep it simple and mirror the accent approach using the same strip helper. stripAcc is defined inside buildAccentDeck. Successfully modified file: C:\Claude (not on Gdrive, nor OneDrive)\Linguics\housing\js\app.js (1 replacements). Here is the updated code:
```json
...
    host.appendChild(card);
    setTimeout(() => { ensureCardVisible(card); card.focus(); }, 0);
    if (!keepAxes) renderVocabAxes();
  }

  // ---- Stress drill (StressAuthor pipeline; tap-the-stressed-syllable, index-scored)  QoderWork 2026-07-23 ----
  // Spec: inter_chat/Architecture_Housing_accent_stress_and_new_qtypes.md §2.
  // Each choice shows the word with ONE candidate syllable emboldened via
  // **syl** markers; choices[i] bolds the syllable at position (i+1) counted
  // from the END (1 = last). Option count = min(syllable_count, 4), so the
  // learner only ever sees phonologically possible positions.
  let stressDeck = [];
  let stressIndex = 0;

  // Render **bold** markers as <strong> via DOM nodes (data-driven but
  // innerHTML-free, so no injection surface).  QoderWork 2026-07-23
  function appendBoldedText(el, text) {
    const parts = String(text == null ? "" : text).split("**");
    for (let i = 0; i < parts.length; i++) {
      if (parts[i] === "") continue;
      if (i % 2 === 1) {
        const s = document.createElement("strong");
        s.textContent = parts[i];
        el.appendChild(s);
      } else {
        el.appendChild(document.createTextNode(parts[i]));
      }
    }
  }

  function buildStressDeck() {
    let items = stressQuestions.filter(q => q && q.type === "mcq" && Array.isArray(q.choices) && q.choices.length >= 2);
    if (vocabFilter.knownDir) {
      const stripAcc = s => s.replace(/[àáâä]/g, "a").replace(/[èéêë]/g, "e").replace(/[ìíîï]/g, "i").replace(/[òóôö]/g, "o").replace(/[ùúûü]/g, "u");
      const known = practisedLemmas(vocabFilter.knownDir);
      items = items.filter(q => {
        const word = String((q.stress_meta && q.stress_meta.word) || q.prompt || "").toLowerCase().trim();
        return known.has(word) || known.has(stripAcc(word));
      });
    }
    stressDeck = shuffle(items);
    stressIndex = 0;
  }

  function renderStressDrillCard(keepAxes) {
    const host = document.getElementById("vocab-host");
    if (!host) return;
    host.innerHTML = "";
    if (!stressDeck.length) {
      const msg = document.createElement("p");
      msg.className = "muted";
      msg.style.cssText = "padding:18px;font-style:italic";
      msg.textContent = "No stress items loaded. Expected at data/grammar_questions_stress.json (tools/stress_pipeline/generate_stress_items.js).";
      host.appendChild(msg);
      return;
    }
    if (stressIndex >= stressDeck.length) { stressDeck = shuffle(stressDeck); stressIndex = 0; }
    const q = stressDeck[stressIndex];
    const sm = q.stress_meta || {};
    LL._cardShownAt = Date.now();

    const card = document.createElement("div");
    card.className = "qcard stress-drill-card";
    card.tabIndex = -1;

    // Meta line: neutral — never reveal THIS word's class (that's the answer).
    const meta = document.createElement("div");
    meta.className = "meta faint";
    meta.textContent = "Stress drill";
    card.appendChild(meta);

    // Prompt: the bare word.
    const prompt = document.createElement("div");
    prompt.className = "prompt";
    prompt.textContent = q.prompt || (sm.word || "");
    card.appendChild(prompt);

    // Ambient class labels (spec §2: shown, never gated). A legend naming each
    // position — it does NOT say which one this word is.  QoderWork 2026-07-23
    const legend = document.createElement("div");
    legend.className = "meta faint stress-legend";
    legend.textContent = "1 = last (tronca) \u00b7 2 = 2nd-last (piana) \u00b7 3 = 3rd-last (sdrucciola) \u00b7 4 = 4th-last (bisdrucciola)";
    card.appendChild(legend);

    // Choices
    const choicesWrap = document.createElement("div");
    choicesWrap.className = "mcq-choices stress-choices";
    const resultHost = document.createElement("div");
    const buttons = [];
    let marked = false;

    const doNext = () => {
      stressIndex++;
      if (stressIndex >= stressDeck.length) { stressDeck = shuffle(stressDeck); stressIndex = 0; }
      renderVocab(true);
    };
    const next = document.createElement("button");
    next.type = "button"; next.className = "btn"; next.textContent = "Skip"; next.title = "Next item";
    next.addEventListener("click", doNext);
    next.addEventListener("keydown", (e) => { if (e.key === "Enter") { e.preventDefault(); doNext(); } });

    // Shuffle choice positions so the correct one isn't always in the same slot.
    const choiceCount = (q.choices || []).length;
    const order = shuffle([...Array(choiceCount).keys()]);
    const correctDisplayIdx = order.indexOf(q.answer_index);

    const commit = (displayIdx) => {
      if (marked) return;
      marked = true;
      const origIdx = order[displayIdx];
      const result = buildMcqResult(q, origIdx);
      // Stress attempt block: feeds attempt persistence (store.js) and the
      // pulse top-level keys, and reconstitutes the 4x4 row-normalised
      // true_pos x answered_pos confusion matrix (spec §2). answered_pos is
      // counted from the END: choices[i] bolds position (i+1).  QoderWork 2026-07-23
      result.stress = {
        true_pos: (typeof sm.stress_pos === "number") ? sm.stress_pos : (q.answer_index + 1),
        answered_pos: origIdx + 1,
        syllable_count: (typeof sm.syllable_count === "number") ? sm.syllable_count : choiceCount,
        stress_class: sm.stress_class || "",
        word: sm.word || q.prompt || ""
      };
      const attempt = LL.store.recordAttempt("vocab",
        { id: q.external_id || ("stress_" + stressIndex), prompt: q.prompt },
        q.choices[origIdx], result);
      recentlyChangedBuckets = new Set(attempt.events.map(e => e.bucket));
      buttons.forEach((b, i) => {
        if (i === correctDisplayIdx) b.classList.add("gc-correct");
        if (i === displayIdx && displayIdx !== correctDisplayIdx) b.classList.add("gc-wrong");
        b.disabled = true;
      });
      resultHost.innerHTML = "";
      resultHost.appendChild(renderResult(result));
      // Show explanation if present (carries the class name + rule).
      if (q.explanation) {
        const expl = document.createElement("div");
        expl.className = "gd-declension";
        expl.textContent = q.explanation;
        resultHost.appendChild(expl);
      }
      renderLiveStats();
      next.textContent = "Next";
      setTimeout(() => next.focus({ preventScroll: true }), 0);
    };

    order.forEach((origIdx, displayIdx) => {
      const b = document.createElement("button");
      b.type = "button";
      b.className = "mcq-choice stress-choice";
      const num = document.createElement("span"); num.className = "gc-num"; num.textContent = displayIdx + 1;
      const lbl = document.createElement("span"); lbl.className = "gc-label";
      appendBoldedText(lbl, q.choices[origIdx]);
      b.appendChild(num); b.appendChild(lbl);
      b.addEventListener("click", () => commit(displayIdx));
      choicesWrap.appendChild(b);
      buttons.push(b);
    });
    card.appendChild(choicesWrap);

    card.appendChild(resultHost);

    const actions = document.createElement("div");
    actions.className = "actions";
    actions.appendChild(next);
    const hint = document.createElement("span");
    hint.className = "kbd-hint";
    hint.textContent = "Tap the stressed syllable \u2014 press 1-" + choiceCount + " or click. Enter to advance.";
    actions.appendChild(hint);
    card.appendChild(actions);

    card.addEventListener("keydown", (e) => {
      if (marked) { if (e.key === "Enter") { e.preventDefault(); doNext(); } return; }
      const n = parseInt(e.key, 10);
      if (n >= 1 && n <= choiceCount) { e.preventDefault(); commit(n - 1); }
    });

    host.appendChild(card);
    setTimeout(() => { ensureCardVisible(card); card.focus(); }, 0);
    if (!keepAxes) renderVocabAxes();
  }

  function renderVocab(keepAxes) {
    renderVocabFilterBar();
    const host = document.getElementById("vocab-host");
    if (!host) return;
    host.innerHTML = "";
...
```

```json
592	    { id: "vocab3", label: "3k",    kind: "vocab", lo: 2001, hi: 3000 },
593	    { id: "vocab4", label: "4k",    kind: "vocab", lo: 3001, hi: 4000 },
594	    { id: "vocab5", label: "5k",    kind: "vocab", lo: 4001, hi: 5000 },
595	    { id: "acc",    label: "ACC",   kind: "accent" },
596	    { id: "str",    label: "STR",   kind: "stress" },
597	    { id: "gen",    label: "GEN",   kind: "gender" },
598	    { id: "sp",     label: "SP",    kind: "spelling" }
599	  ];
600	  function buildPulseStrip(strandName) {
601	    const host = document.querySelector("#live-stats .live-header");
602	    if (!host) return null;
603	    let strip = host.querySelector(".pulse-strip");
604	    if (strip) return strip;
605	    strip = document.createElement("div");
606	    strip.className = "pulse-strip";
607	    for (const def of STRIP_BOX_DEFS) {
608	      const box = document.createElement("div");
609	      box.className = "strip-box9 strip-" + def.id;
610	      box.dataset.kind = def.kind;
611	      box.dataset.id = def.id;
612	      if (def.kind === "vocab") { box.dataset.lo = def.lo; box.dataset.hi = def.hi; }
613	      box.title = def.kind === "vocab"
614	        ? "Words " + def.lo + "\u2013" + def.hi + ": click for vocab coverage"
615	        : def.label + " mastery";
616	      if (def.kind === "vocab") box.addEventListener("click", () => showStrand("vocab"));
617	      const lbl = document.createElement("span");
618	      lbl.className = "strip-box9-label";
619	      lbl.textContent = def.label;
620	      box.appendChild(lbl);
621	      strip.appendChild(box);
622	    }
623	    host.appendChild(strip);   // after h3 + live-controls so "Live stats" sits above  QoderWork 2026-07-22
624	    return strip;
625	  }
626	  function eachStrip(fn) {
627	    for (const s of STRIP_STRANDS) {
628	      const strip = buildPulseStrip(s);
629	      if (strip) fn(strip);
630	    }
631	  }
632	  // Mastery ratio for a set of events: correctness of attempted.
633	  function stripMastery(events) {
634	    let att = 0, cor = 0;
635	    for (const ev of events) {
636	      att += (ev.attempted_credit || 1);
637	      cor += (ev.correctness_credit || 0);
638	    }
639	    return att > 0 ? cor / att : 0;
640	  }
641	  function paintStripBase() {
642	    const allEvents = [];
643	    for (const a of ((LL.state && LL.state.attempts) || [])) {
644	      for (const ev of (a.events || [])) allEvents.push(ev);
645	    }
646	    // Bucket events by box kind.
647	    const byBox = {};
648	    for (const def of STRIP_BOX_DEFS) byBox[def.id] = [];
649	    for (const ev of allEvents) {
650	      const b = String(ev.bucket || "");
651	      if (b.indexOf("vocabulary.it.") === 0) {
652	        const lemma = b.split(".")[2];
653	        const rank = lemmaMinRank(lemma);
654	        if (rank != null) {
655	          for (const def of STRIP_BOX_DEFS) {
656	            if (def.kind === "vocab" && rank >= def.lo && rank <= def.hi) { byBox[def.id].push(ev); break; }
657	          }
658	        }
659	        if (b.indexOf(".gender") >= 0) byBox.gen.push(ev);
660	      } else if (b.indexOf("orthography.accent") === 0) {
661	        byBox.acc.push(ev);
662	      } else if (b.indexOf("orthography.spelling") === 0) {
663	        byBox.sp.push(ev);
664	      }
665	      // STR: no events yet (stress drill not built)
666	    }
667	    eachStrip(strip => {
668	      for (const def of STRIP_BOX_DEFS) {
669	        const el = strip.querySelector('.strip-box9[data-id="' + def.id + '"]');
670	        if (!el) continue;
671	        const m = stripMastery(byBox[def.id]);
672	        const n = byBox[def.id].length;
673	        // Gradient: base cream -> green by mastery; opacity scales with attempts.
674	        const alpha = n === 0 ? 0 : Math.min(0.15 + m * 0.55, 0.7);
675	        el.style.background = n === 0
676	          ? ""
677	          : "linear-gradient(to top, rgba(47,143,78," + alpha.toFixed(2) + ") 0%, rgba(47,143,78," + (alpha * 0.3).toFixed(2) + ") 100%)";
678	        el.title = (def.kind === "vocab"
679	          ? "Words " + def.lo + "\u2013" + def.hi
680	          : def.label) + ": " + n + " events, " + Math.round(m * 100) + "% correct";
681	      }
682	    });
683	  }
684	  function pulseStripFlash() {
685	    const a = LL.lastAttempt;
686	    if (!a) return;
687	    const doFlash = (el, cls) => {
688	      if (!el) return;
689	      el.classList.remove("flash-good", "flash-bad", "flash-warm");
690	      void el.offsetWidth;
691	      el.classList.add(cls);
692	    };
693	    // Collect which boxes fire.
694	    const vocabFires = {};   // boxId -> good|bad
695	    let accFire = null, genFire = null, spFire = null;
696	    for (const ev of (a.events || [])) {
697	      const b = String(ev.bucket || "");
698	      const good = (ev.correctness_credit || 0) > 0;
699	      if (b.indexOf("vocabulary.it.") === 0) {
700	        const lemma = b.split(".")[2];
701	        const rank = lemmaMinRank(lemma);
702	        if (rank != null) {
703	          for (const def of STRIP_BOX_DEFS) {
704	            if (def.kind === "vocab" && rank >= def.lo && rank <= def.hi) {
705	              vocabFires[def.id] = (vocabFires[def.id] === "bad") ? "bad" : (good ? "good" : "bad");
706	              break;
707	            }
708	          }
709	        }
710	        if (b.indexOf(".gender") >= 0) genFire = (genFire === "bad") ? "bad" : (good ? "good" : "bad");
711	      } else if (b.indexOf("orthography.accent") === 0) {
712	        accFire = good ? "good" : "warm";
713	      } else if (b.indexOf("orthography.spelling") === 0) {
714	        spFire = good ? "good" : "bad";
715	      }
716	    }
717	    eachStrip(strip => {
718	      for (const [id, verdict] of Object.entries(vocabFires)) {
719	        doFlash(strip.querySelector('.strip-box9[data-id="' + id + '"]'), verdict === "good" ? "flash-good" : "flash-bad");
720	      }
721	      if (accFire) doFlash(strip.querySelector('.strip-box9[data-id="acc"]'), accFire === "good" ? "flash-good" : "flash-warm");
722	      if (genFire) doFlash(strip.querySelector('.strip-box9[data-id="gen"]'), genFire === "good" ? "flash-good" : "flash-bad");
723	      if (spFire) doFlash(strip.querySelector('.strip-box9[data-id="sp"]'), spFire === "good" ? "flash-good" : "flash-bad");
724	    });
725	  }
726	  function renderPulseStrip() {
727	    paintStripBase();
728	    pulseStripFlash();
729	  }
730	
731	  function renderGrammarFilterBar() {
732	    const host = document.getElementById("grammar-filter-bar");
733	    host.innerHTML = "";
734	    if (!grammarQuestions.length) return;
```

Now I understand the strip. Stress events have buckets like `phonology.stress.inflectional.infinitive_ere_stem` (from the item's markpoints). So in paintStripBase, I add the following: `else if (b.indexOf("phonology.stress") === 0) { byBox.str.push(ev); }`. And in pulseStripFlash: `else if (b.indexOf("phonology.stress") === 0) { strFire = good ? "good" : "bad"; }`.

Let me check the bucket prefix in the data: "phonology.stress.inflectional.infinitive_ere_stem". Yep, the prefix is "phonology.stress".

#### Thinking

With this, all the patterns are in place. Write `buildStressConfusionMatrix()`:

- Rows = true_pos (1..4), columns = answered_pos (1..4).
- Aggregate from attempts that have stress_attempt: count[true_pos][answered_pos]++.
- Row normalization: each cell displays the proportion within its row (what proportion of words whose true position is X were answered as Y). The diagonal = correct.
- Smith's preferences (important): the heatmap scale is normalized to a single global maximum (the darkest cell = true maximum); font color is constant across all cells, and text does not switch between dark and light. Also, his scoring philosophy: raw counts + continuous shading, no threshold.

Therefore: display raw count + percentage in each cell. Background shading: for off-diagonal cells, shade by value/global_max (the darker, the more that confusion is happening). Diagonal (correct) — should it be shaded green, or use the same scale? Smith said "scale normalized to a single global maximum (the darkest cell = true maximum); constant font color across all cells." Use a single olive shading scale across all cells based on count/global_max, and keep the font color constant. Show the diagonal as correct.

Actually, to keep it simple and align with his preferences: single global maximum across all 16 cells, shade all cells with olive rgba(61,74,28, pct) (translation scoring uses olive rgba(61,74,28,pct/100), and above 55% becomes white — but that's for translation scoring; the heatmap preference says constant font color, no switching). Use a moderate maximum alpha so text stays readable, and keep font color constant (inherit). To guarantee readability without switching, cap alpha at about 0.5. Hmm, but "the darkest cell = true maximum" — the darkest cell should correspond to the true maximum. If I use alpha = 0.6 * (count/global_max), the maximum cell will have alpha 0.6. Text stays dark. This satisfies both conditions.

Let me reconsider: the recorded heatmap preference: "scale normalized to a single global maximum (the darkest cell = true maximum); constant font color across all cells, never switch text between dark and light." So: alpha proportional to count, with the global maximum as the darkest. Constant font color. Cap alpha at 0.65 and keep text as the default dark color. Good.

Row normalization: the spec says "4x4 row-normalized matrix." So the displayed percentages are row-normalized (within true_pos, the proportion that answered as pos Y). But for the shading, use raw counts with the global maximum (Smith's preference). Display both: percentage (row-normalized, prominent) + raw count (smaller). Actually, to keep the cell compact: display "33%" and put "n=3" in the title. Hmm, let me display the percentage as text and put count + percentage in the title.

Wait — Smith's philosophy is "raw counts + continuous shading, no threshold." So emphasize raw counts. Display the raw count as the main text, and put the row-normalized percentage in the title, or display it small. Let me display count as the main text and add row % as secondary text. Actually, the cleanest is: main = count, sub = (row %). Let me do this: td.textContent = count; title = "true X → answered Y: n=count (rowPct% of true X)". And maybe add a small row % label. To respect "raw counts," display count prominently and show row % in parentheses smaller.

Let me structure each cell like this: `<td><b>count</b> <span class=sub>pct%</span></td>`. Keep it simple.

Also add row/column headers: rows labeled "true: last/2nd-last/3rd-last/4th-last" (tronca/piana/sdrucciola/bisdrucciola), columns labeled "tapped: ...". And a note that the diagonal = correct.

Also, if there's no data, handle it with a note like the person-tense grid does.

#### Thinking

Insert `buildStressConfusionMatrix()` after buildPersonClassGrid (line 8575), before misconceptionFamilyCounts (line 8576).

Design (following Smith's heatmap policy):
- 4×4: rows = true_pos (1–4), columns = answered_pos (1–4).
- Aggregate attempt.stress_attempt.
- Row-normalized percentages (spec) + raw counts (Smith).
- Shading: single global maximum across all cells → darkest cell = true maximum; olive scale; constant font color (no inversion). Use rgba(61,74,28, alpha), alpha = 0.65 * count/globalMax. Text stays default (dark) — since the max alpha is 0.65 over cream, dark text remains readable.
- Diagonal cells = correct responses. Mark with a subtle class, but keep the same shading scale. Add a legend note: "rows = actual stress position · columns = tapped position · diagonal = correct. Shading is by raw count (darkest = most frequent cell); % is within-row."
- Position labels: 1=last (tronca), 2=2nd-last (piana), 3=3rd-last (sdrucciola), 4=4th-last (bisdrucciola).

Alright, let's write it.


Now I'll insert the confusion matrix builder after `buildPersonClassGrid`:



#### Tool: Edit

> Modified: `C:\Claude (not on Gdrive, nor OneDrive)\Linguics\housing\js\app.js`

**Output:**

```json
Successfully modified file: C:\Claude (not on Gdrive, nor OneDrive)\Linguics\housing\js\app.js (1 replacements). Here is the updated code:
...
      table.appendChild(tbody);
      const scroll = document.createElement("div"); scroll.className = "pt-scroll"; scroll.appendChild(table); wrap.appendChild(scroll);
    } catch (e) { wrap.textContent = "(person × class unavailable)"; }
    return wrap;
  }

  // Stress confusion matrix (Architecture_Housing_accent_stress_and_new_qtypes.md
  // §2): 4x4 true_pos x answered_pos, reconstituted from attempt.stress_attempt.
  // Rows = where the stress REALLY is; columns = where the learner TAPPED.
  // Diagonal = correct. Raw counts are primary (Smith: raw counts, no
  // thresholds); the row-normalised % rides second (the spec's matrix).
  // Shading: ONE global max across all 16 cells (darkest cell = true max),
  // olive scale, constant font colour — never flips dark/light (Smith prefs).
  // QoderWork 2026-07-23
  function buildStressConfusionMatrix() {
    const wrap = document.createElement("div"); wrap.className = "pt-grid-wrap";
    try {
      const POS_LABELS = { 1: "last (tronca)", 2: "2nd-last (piana)", 3: "3rd-last (sdrucciola)", 4: "4th-last (bisdrucciola)" };
      // counts[true_pos][answered_pos] = n
      const counts = {}; let total = 0; let globalMax = 0;
      for (let t = 1; t <= 4; t++) { counts[t] = {}; for (let p = 1; p <= 4; p++) counts[t][p] = 0; }
      for (const a of ((LL.state && LL.state.attempts) || [])) {
        const sa = a.stress_attempt;
        if (!sa || typeof sa.true_pos !== "number" || typeof sa.answered_pos !== "number") continue;
        const t = sa.true_pos, p = sa.answered_pos;
        if (t < 1 || t > 4 || p < 1 || p > 4) continue;
        counts[t][p]++; total++;
        if (counts[t][p] > globalMax) globalMax = counts[t][p];
      }
      if (!total) {
        const n = document.createElement("p"); n.className = "analysis-note";
        n.textContent = "No stress answers yet — the matrix fills in as you tap stressed syllables.";
        wrap.appendChild(n); return wrap;
      }
      const table = document.createElement("table"); table.className = "pt-grid stress-confusion";
      const thead = document.createElement("thead"); const htr = document.createElement("tr");
      const corner = document.createElement("th"); corner.textContent = "true \u2193 / tapped \u2192"; htr.appendChild(corner);
      for (let p = 1; p <= 4; p++) { const th = document.createElement("th"); th.textContent = POS_LABELS[p]; htr.appendChild(th); }
      thead.appendChild(htr); table.appendChild(thead);
      const tbody = document.createElement("tbody");
      for (let t = 1; t <= 4; t++) {
        const rowTotal = counts[t][1] + counts[t][2] + counts[t][3] + counts[t][4];
        const tr = document.createElement("tr");
        const rh = document.createElement("th"); rh.textContent = POS_LABELS[t]; tr.appendChild(rh);
        for (let p = 1; p <= 4; p++) {
          const c = counts[t][p]; const td = document.createElement("td");
          if (t === p) td.classList.add("stress-confusion-diag");
          if (c > 0) {
            // Single global-max scale: darkest cell = most frequent cell overall.
            const alpha = 0.65 * (c / globalMax);
            td.style.background = "rgba(61,74,28," + alpha.toFixed(2) + ")";
            const pct = rowTotal > 0 ? Math.round(100 * c / rowTotal) : 0;
            const strong = document.createElement("strong"); strong.textContent = String(c);
            const sub = document.createElement("span"); sub.className = "stress-confusion-pct"; sub.textContent = " " + pct + "%";
            td.appendChild(strong); td.appendChild(sub);
            td.title = "True " + POS_LABELS[t] + " \u2192 tapped " + POS_LABELS[p] + ": " + c + " answer" + (c === 1 ? "" : "s") + " (" + pct + "% of this row)" + (t === p ? " — correct" : "");
          } else {
            td.className = "pt-empty" + (t === p ? " stress-confusion-diag" : "");
            td.title = "True " + POS_LABELS[t] + " \u2192 tapped " + POS_LABELS[p] + ": no answers";
          }
          tr.appendChild(td);
        }
        tbody.appendChild(tr);
      }
      table.appendChild(tbody);
      const scroll = document.createElement("div"); scroll.className = "pt-scroll"; scroll.appendChild(table); wrap.appendChild(scroll);
      const note = document.createElement("p"); note.className = "analysis-note";
      note.textContent = "Rows = where the stress really is; columns = where you tapped. The diagonal is correct. Number = raw count; % = share of that row. Shade is by count across the whole table (darkest = most frequent cell).";
      wrap.appendChild(note);
    } catch (e) { wrap.textContent = "(stress confusion matrix unavailable)"; }
    return wrap;
  }
  function misconceptionFamilyCounts() {
    const counts = new Map();
    for (const a of ((LL.state && LL.state.attempts) || [])) for (const h of (a.misconception_hits || [])) { const id = (h && (h.id || h)) || ""; if (!id) continue; const fam = String(id).split(".")[0]; counts.set(fam, (counts.get(fam) || 0) + 1); }
    return counts;
  }
...
```

Let's do one more targeted verification: simulate the store.js + pulse.js integration — that is, confirm that a result with a .stress block produces attempt.stress_attempt, and that the pulse payload picks up the three keys. Let's write a small harness that stubs the environment and requires store.js and pulse.js. Actually, store.js and pulse.js are IIFEs attached to window.LL. We can stub window/localStorage, load them, and drive recordAttempt and buildAttemptPayload.

Let's write a second harness that does the following:
1. Create a fake window, LL, and localStorage.
2. Load store.js and pulse.js via eval or require.
3. Call LL.store.recordAttempt("vocab", item, raw, resultWithStress).
4. Verify attempt.stress_attempt is set.

```json
1	/* =============================================================================
2	 * pulse.js — estate shared sign-in + attempt pulse (Linguics adopter)
3	 *
4	 * Mechanism verbatim from the EdTech packet (inter_chat/
5	 * EdTechOverview_Architecture_shared_login_and_pulse_adoption.md) under the
6	 * rulings of Architecture_Housing_shared_login_and_pulse v1:
7	 *   - one shared Apps Script endpoint, one workbook; project: "linguics"
8	 *     grows its own tab on first POST, no deploy step;
9	 *   - identity SHARED with the physics drillers via localStorage key
10	 *     smithics_fields_identity_v1 (anonymous_id + display_name common);
11	 *     the LINGUICS cohort is SCOPED: held under our own key, never written
12	 *     into the shared object, sent as `cohort` on every POST;
13	 *   - payload MAXIMAL from day one (Smith: "absolutely as much information
14	 *     as possible so you don't have to retro-add anything");
15	 *   - fail-soft everywhere: a pulse must never break marking.
16	 * ========================================================================== */
17	(function () {
18	  "use strict";
19	  const LL = window.LL || (window.LL = {});
20	
21	  const REPORT_URL = "https://script.google.com/macros/s/AKfycbwQ2NxNi-AWGCpBVDa6nT9DDYsS66F53ENZvtZozDwuWkKaivqgLaGUyjSFd_InJ4Kt/exec";
22	  const PROJECT = "linguics";
23	  const IDENTITY_KEY = "smithics_fields_identity_v1";   // shared estate identity
24	  const COHORT_KEY = "linguics_cohort_v1";              // scoped Italian class
25	  // Interim hardcoded list (Smith, 2026-07-20). Swap to the TeacherViewer
26	  // doGet single-source when milestone M2 announces it.
27	  const INTERIM_CLASSES = ["Test", "Y12 Italian", "Y13 Italian"];
28	
29	  function uuid() {
30	    try { return crypto.randomUUID(); } catch (e) {
31	      return "xxxxxxxx-xxxx-4xxx-yxxx-xxxxxxxxxxxx".replace(/[xy]/g, c => {
32	        const r = Math.random() * 16 | 0; return (c === "x" ? r : (r & 0x3 | 0x8)).toString(16);
33	      });
34	    }
35	  }
36	
37	  function readShared() {
38	    try {
39	      const raw = localStorage.getItem(IDENTITY_KEY);
40	      const obj = raw ? JSON.parse(raw) : null;
41	      return (obj && typeof obj === "object") ? obj : {};
42	    } catch (e) { return {}; }
43	  }
44	  function writeShared(obj) {
45	    try { localStorage.setItem(IDENTITY_KEY, JSON.stringify(obj)); } catch (e) {}
46	  }
47	
48	  function identity() {
49	    const shared = readShared();
50	    let cohort = "";
51	    try { cohort = localStorage.getItem(COHORT_KEY) || ""; } catch (e) {}
52	    return {
53	      anonymous_id: shared.anonymous_id || "",
54	      display_name: shared.display_name || "",
55	      cohort
56	    };
57	  }
58	  function signedIn() {
59	    const id = identity();
60	    return !!(id.display_name && id.cohort);
61	  }
62	  // Sign in: PRESERVE every unknown key in the shared object (the packet's
63	  // caution about the physics drillers); never touch shared.cohort (that is
64	  // the physics class); the Linguics class lives only under COHORT_KEY.
65	  function signIn(displayName, cohort) {
66	    const shared = readShared();
67	    if (!shared.anonymous_id) shared.anonymous_id = uuid();
68	    shared.display_name = String(displayName || "").trim();
69	    if (shared.google_email === undefined) shared.google_email = "";
70	    writeShared(shared);
```

PRIOR: r65 VANISH FIX - REAL cause (Smith was right; my r64 flanking-dilution diagnosis was WRONG). The dots that 'flashed then vanished' in the 1000-2000 FOCUSED block were HOMOGRAPH RANK COLLISIONS: 480 ranks estate-wide (175 in 1000-2000) carry >1 entry (largo+toccare @1015, nave+presente @1004, ...), and byRank kept only the FIRST entry per rank, so practising any non-first entry never lit its dot - it flashed (by data-rank) then reverted to untouched (first entry has no events). FIX: byRankAllMap() (rank->ALL entries) + eventsForRank(rank,dir) unions every entry's events chronologically; focused dots, flanking cells and recolourAxisCellsFor all aggregate per-rank now. ALSO fixed a MIX-mode bug: activeDir mapped 'mix'->active-only (hid every passive event); now vocabHeatmapDir() => 'any' for mix/unset. VERIFIED node --check + merge harness (sister practice lights the dot; recency order; single-entry fast path). r64 (recolour-in-place on mark instead of full rebuild; keepAxes on Next) STILL STANDS - correct for perf, just aimed the vanish story at the wrong cells. SLOWNESS root cause: the per-answer FULL heatmap rebuild. doMark called renderVocabAxes() (teardown+rebuild of ~990 focused dots + ~20 flanking blocks, each dot an eventsForEntry lookup + click listener) on EVERY mark, and renderVocab() rebuilt it again on EVERY Next. NOW: doMark recolours ONLY touched cells in place via new recolourAxisCellsFor(entry) (focused dot + its flanking cell + gender/theme lemma dots; sets SETTLED bg, not just a flash); renderVocab(keepAxes) + doNext->renderVocab(true) skips the rebuild on advance; full rebuild now only on strand-enter / filter change. VANISH: focused-grid hits already persisted (scrittore: 1 event->1.0->dark green); the 'flashed then vanished' was FLANKING cells - a cell aggregates ~9 lemmas so one hit dilutes to ~0.29, and baseline==yellowStop(0.2) made touched read as untouched (disponibile). PRIOR: r61 + WORKER FIX - the AI marker IS working; the red box (r61) revealed the real cause: DeepSeek wraps its JSON in a ```json ... ``` markdown fence, and worker/src/index.ts did a BARE JSON.parse(content) -> malformed_json -> housing fell back to the stub. Fixed worker/src/index.ts (~L451): strip the code fence, and as a fallback extract the outermost {...}, before JSON.parse. Now the failure reason is CAPTURED (aiError) and shown above the stub result in a red .marker-ai-error box. PRIOR: r65 VANISH FIX - REAL cause (Smith was right; my r64 flanking-dilution diagnosis was WRONG). The dots that 'flashed then vanished' in the 1000-2000 FOCUSED block were HOMOGRAPH RANK COLLISIONS: 480 ranks estate-wide (175 in 1000-2000) carry >1 entry (largo+toccare @1015, nave+presente @1004, ...), and byRank kept only the FIRST entry per rank, so practising any non-first entry never lit its dot - it flashed (by data-rank) then reverted to untouched (first entry has no events). FIX: byRankAllMap() (rank->ALL entries) + eventsForRank(rank,dir) unions every entry's events chronologically; focused dots, flanking cells and recolourAxisCellsFor all aggregate per-rank now. ALSO fixed a MIX-mode bug: activeDir mapped 'mix'->active-only (hid every passive event); now vocabHeatmapDir() => 'any' for mix/unset. SLOWNESS root cause: the per-answer FULL heatmap rebuild. doMark called renderVocabAxes() (teardown+rebuild of ~990 focused dots + ~20 flanking blocks, each dot an eventsForEntry lookup + click listener) on EVERY mark, and renderVocab() rebuilt it again on EVERY Next. NOW: doMark recolours ONLY touched cells in place via new recolourAxisCellsFor(entry) (focused dot + its flanking cell + gender/theme lemma dots; sets SETTLED bg, not just a flash); renderVocab(keepAxes) + doNext->renderVocab(true) skips the rebuild on advance; full rebuild now only on strand-enter / filter change. VANISH: focused-grid hits already persisted (scrittore: 1 event->1.0->dark green); the 'flashed then vanished' was FLANKING cells - a cell aggregates ~9 lemmas so one hit dilutes to ~0.29, and baseline==yellowStop(0.2) made touched read as untouched (disponibile). FIX: recolour sets the true aggregate AND adds .has-practice (inset green border) to any flank/lemma cell containing practice; also added in renderFlankingStripes so it survives full rebuilds. Fixed worker/src/index.ts (~L451): strip the code fence, and as a fallback extract the outermost {...}, before JSON.parse. CRITICAL: this is a WORKER change - needs `wrangler deploy` from the worker/ folder (or the EdTech/worker owner), NOT the GitHub push that deploys the housing site. PRIOR: AI TRANSLATION MARKER 'not firing' diagnosed + partly a silent-failure bug of MINE: when a worker URL is set (toggle 'on'), markTranslationLive IS called, but on ANY error the catch wrote a 'Live marker failed' note that the very next `resultHost.innerHTML=""` ERASED before render - so the AI was failing and the learner only ever saw the stub ('real AI marker will give per-skill feedback'), silently. Now the failure reason is CAPTURED (aiError) and shown above the stub result in a red .marker-ai-error box. 1	# Architecture -> Housing: accent & stress report axes, two new qtypes, hover-text fix
```json
2	
3	**From:** Architecture, 2026-07-21. Bundles the Housing build items from this session's accent/stress
4	ratifications and the CEFR harvest. Data pipelines are commissioned separately (REQUEST_to_code_stress_pipeline.md).

10	## 1. Accent drill — report axes + qtype + pulse (taxonomy ratified 2026-07-21)
11	- Placement classes are now LEAVES under `orthography.accent.italian.placement` (meaning_pair / tense_bearing /
12	  che_compound / lexical_final / no_accent / apostrophe_not_accent). The coverage matrix should surface per-class mastery.
13	- **Event contract:** every attempt emits `accent_type` (a e i o u with the mark at issue) x `placement_class` x
14	  `outcome` in {correct, omitted, wrong_kind, inserted}; map omitted->`.missing`, wrong_kind->`.wrong_mark`,
15	  inserted->`.added` (aligned with the r29 slip enrichment expected/written/accent_class/accent_chars).
16	  Insertions attribute to the type TYPED; wrong_kind carries BOTH chars (expected e-acute, written e-grave) so the
17	  acute<->grave direction is recoverable. `pron_effect` (stress_shift | vowel_quality | none) rides as an attribute.
18	- **qtype:** index-scored pick-the-accent (options displayed, learner picks). Day-one pulse tagging per the contract.
19	
20	## 2. Stress drill — report axes + tap qtype + pulse
21	- **qtype:** tap-the-stressed-syllable — embolden each candidate syllable, learner taps 1..N; option set = every
22	  PHONOLOGICALLY POSSIBLE position for that word only (variable count, capped at the last four; never render an
23	  impossible position). Ambient class labels (tronca/piana/sdrucciola/bisdrucciola) shown, never gated.
24	- **Report axes (all on the stats, membership AND treatment TOGGLEABLE):** `stress_mechanism`,
25	  `stress_mechanism_detail`, `etymological`, `accent_cue`, `syllable_count` band, and the secondary `stress_tags`.
26	  This is the "which detection cue is the learner missing" view.
27	- **Stress<->accent cross-analysis (Rev 27):** the final-accented class is shared with the accent strand; the
28	  report must separate "mis-stressed a SHOWN word" (perception) from "omitted the accent, hence mis-stressed"
29	  (production) — measure these rates, do not assume them.
30	- **pulse extra_json:** `(true_pos, answered_pos, syllable_count)` so the 4x4 row-normalised matrix reconstitutes.
31	
32	## 3. NEW qtype — error identification (from the Transparent harvest, Smith adopted)
33	Full sentence containing exactly one wrong word; the options are words DRAWN FROM the sentence; the learner picks
34	the error. Recognition, index-scored (no substring marking). Natural home for the over-regularisation distractor
35	bank (`incoming drafts/overregularisation_distractors_v1.json`). Author-facing shape to follow in the brief.
36	
37	## 4. Coverage-grid hover-text fix (Smith flagged live)
38	Now the verbs are split, the coverage grid's hover shows io / tu / noi / loro but does NOT say what each COLUMN
39	is. Add real column headers / a legend so the person bands are readable.
40
```
