```json
---

## Housing reply — §2 stress drill BUILT (r77, QoderWork 2026-07-23)

The four items Smith handed off are done and verified: load path, tap-qtype renderer, 4x4 confusion-matrix
report, and pulse payload.

- **Load path:** `data/grammar_questions_stress.json` (20,005 items) joins the tail singleton `Promise.all`, but
  rides its OWN `stressQuestions` array — NOT merged into `grammarQuestions`. Reason: stress choices embed
  `**bold**` syllable markers that the general grammar MCQ path would render literally. Loader returns a separate
  `stress` key.
- **qtype:** tap-the-stressed-syllable. Each choice shows the word with one candidate syllable emboldened
  (`**syl**` -> `<strong>`, DOM-safe, no innerHTML). Display positions shuffled (no position bias). Ambient class
  legend shown (`1=last(tronca) · 2=2nd-last(piana) · 3=3rd-last(sdrucciola) · 4=4th-last(bisdrucciola)`), never
  reveals THIS word's class (that is the answer). Index-scored via `buildMcqResult`; choice_tags misconception
  firing inherited (r68). Option count = `min(syllable_count, 4)` so no impossible position is ever rendered.
- **Position mapping (the load-bearing fact):** `choices[i]` bolds the syllable at position `(i+1)` from the END,
  so `answered_pos = originalIndex + 1` and `true_pos = stress_meta.stress_pos`. Verified against all 20,005 items
  (0 failures).
- **pulse extra_json:** `stress_true_pos` / `stress_answered_pos` / `stress_syllable_count` lifted as top-level
  keys (estate script sweeps them into extra_json), reconstituting the 4x4 row-normalised matrix.
- **4x4 confusion matrix:** `buildStressConfusionMatrix()` on the Analysis view — rows = true position, cols =
  tapped position, diagonal = correct. Raw counts primary + row-normalised % secondary (Smith: raw counts, no
  thresholds); single-global-max olive shade, constant font colour (Smith heatmap prefs).
- **STR strip box:** wired to `phonology.stress.*` buckets (was an explicit placeholder).

**Data note (not a defect):** 2,405 `accent_final` wordform items (`accent_cue=true`, `mechanism=orthographic`)
show the written accent in the PROMPT (that is the cue under test) and strip it in the choices. By design.

**Still outstanding from §2 (later phase, not in Smith's four-item handoff):** the toggleable report axes
(`stress_mechanism` / `stress_mechanism_detail` / `etymological` / `accent_cue` / `syllable_count` band /
`stress_tags` — membership AND treatment toggleable) and the stress<->accent perception-vs-production
cross-analysis (Rev 27). The confusion matrix + pulse keys lay the data groundwork for both.

§1 (accent report axes), §3 (error-ID data authoring), §4 (coverage hover fix) unchanged / still open.

```

Done. The stress drill is fully wired and verified — build r77.

All four items from your handoff are built:

**Load path** — `grammar_questions_stress.json` (20,005 items) loads with the singleton wave but rides its own `stressQuestions` array rather than merging into `grammarQuestions`, because the stress choices embed `**bold**` syllable markers that the general grammar MCQ path would render literally.

**Renderer** — a fifth "Stress drill" mode chip. Each choice shows the word with one candidate syllable emboldened (rendered `**syl**`→`<strong>`, DOM-safe). Display positions are shuffled so there's no position bias, and an ambient legend names the positions (1=last/tronca … 4=4th-last/bisdrucciola) without ever revealing this word's class — that's the answer. Index-scored, keyboard 1–N, explanation reveal, known-words filter applies.

**4×4 confusion matrix** — on the Analysis view: rows = where the stress really is, columns = where they tapped, diagonal = correct. Raw counts primary with the row-normalised % secondary, shaded on a single global-max olive scale (darkest cell = most frequent, constant font colour) per your heatmap preferences.

**Pulse payload** — `stress_true_pos` / `stress_answered_pos` / `stress_syllable_count` go out as top-level keys so the workbook reconstitutes the matrix. The STR strip box is also live now (was a placeholder), firing on `phonology.stress.*` events.

The load-bearing mapping (`answered_pos = choice index + 1`, since `choices[i]` bolds the syllable *i+1* from the end) was checked against **all 20,005 items — zero failures**, and a separate harness confirmed the `stress_attempt` block persists, survives a localStorage round-trip, and lifts into the three pulse keys. `node --check` is clean on app/store/pulse.

One data note, not a defect: the 2,405 `accent_final` (tronca) items show the written accent in the prompt ("andò") and strip it in the choices — that's by design, since they're the `accent_cue`/orthographic items where reading the accent mark *is* the skill being tested.

I've updated `_status/Housing.md` (r77 entry) and logged completion on the Architecture thread, noting the two §2 items still outstanding for a later phase (the toggleable report axes and the stress↔accent perception/production cross-analysis).

Nothing is committed — the changes are ready for your review and push via GitHub Desktop. Once pushed, the footer should read **build 2026-07-23-r77**.

```json
2	 * feedback.js  -  the estate feedback widget (WEB_KIT standard, Sheets style)

4	 * v2, 2026-07-17: optional name/email fields, capture-disclosure caption, no
5	 * personal name in user-facing copy. Backend columns added in the same revision;
6	 * the backend must be redeployed as a NEW VERSION (same URL) to store them.
7	 * Deploy order does not matter: the old backend simply ignores the new fields.
8	 *
9	 * What it is: an in-page feedback panel that POSTs straight to a shared Google
10	 * Sheet via one Apps Script endpoint. No Google Form, no redirect, full styling
11	 * control. One shared sheet serves every tool; the `project` field is what
12	 * separates them.
13	 *
14	 * HOW TO USE (two lines in the host page, near the other scripts, NOT in <head>):
15	 *
16	 *   <script>
17	 *     window.FEEDBACK_CONFIG = {
18	 *       project: 'linguics-housing',            // required: which tool this is
19	 *       context: function () {                  // optional: app state to attach
20	 *         return { strand: currentStrand, build: 'LL_BUILD 2026-07-14-r4' };
21	 *       }
22	 *     };
23	 *   </script>
24	 *   <script src="js/feedback.js"></script>
25	 *
26	 * The context function is called at submit time and must never throw; anything
27	 * it returns is JSON-stringified into one Sheet column.
28	 *
29	 * Deliberate design points (from WEB_KIT):
30	 *  - Body script, not <head>, so automated data-only rewrites of <head> leave it alone.
31	 *  - POSTs as text/plain to dodge the Apps Script CORS preflight gotcha.
32	 *  - Fails soft: the user always gets a quiet thank-you, even if the POST is slow,
33	 *    blocked or the endpoint is not deployed yet. Never show the user a stack trace.
34	 *  - The trigger button carries an id, so the estate delegated click-tracker
35	 *    (site_click) picks it up with no extra instrumentation.
36	 *  - Copy never names the recipient personally: tools are used by pupils who know
37	 *    him by whatever they call him, not by the estate's name for him.
38	 *  - The caption under the form tells the user the page is included automatically,
39	 *    so they need not describe where they were. Name/email are optional fields.
40	 * ========================================================================== */

42	  'use strict';
43	
44	  if (window.__wkFeedbackLoaded) return;      // never double-mount
45	  window.__wkFeedbackLoaded = true;
46

49	   * If this URL ever changes, every copied tool orphans silently, so when editing

72	    '#wk-fb-name,#wk-fb-email{width:100%;box-sizing:border-box;margin-top:8px;',
73	    'padding:8px;border:1px solid rgba(0,0,0,.22);border-radius:8px;font:inherit}',
74	    '#wk-fb-panel p.wk-fb-note{margin:8px 0 0;font-size:11px;line-height:1.4;color:#6b7280}',
75	    '#wk-fb-row{display:flex;gap:8px;justify-content:flex-end;margin-top:10px}',
76	    '#wk-fb-panel button{font:600 13px/1 inherit;padding:8px 12px;border-radius:8px;cursor:pointer}',
77	    '#wk-fb-send{background:#111827;color:#fff;border:1px solid #111827}',
78	    '#wk-fb-cancel{background:#fff;color:#374151;border:1px solid rgba(0,0,0,.22)}',
79	    '#wk-fb-done{margin:0;padding:6px 0;font-size:13px;color:#065f46}'
80	  ].join('');
81	
82	  /* One source for the form so open/reset can never drift apart. */
83	  var FORM_HTML =
84	    '<h2>Send feedback</h2>' +
85	    '<p class="wk-fb-hint">What worked, what did not, what is missing.</p>' +
86	    '<textarea id="wk-fb-msg" aria-label="Your feedback"></textarea>' +
87	    '<input id="wk-fb-name" type="text" autocomplete="name" placeholder="Name (optional)" aria-label="Your name (optional)">' +
88	    '<input id="wk-fb-email" type="email" autocomplete="email" placeholder="Email (optional)" aria-label="Your email (optional)">' +
89	    '<p class="wk-fb-note">The page you were on is automatically included in the feedback, ' +
90	    'so you don\'t need to describe which page it is.</p>' +
91	    '<div id="wk-fb-row">' +
92	      '<button id="wk-fb-cancel" type="button">Cancel</button>' +
93	      '<button id="wk-fb-send" type="button">Send</button>' +
94	    '</div>';
95	
96	  function el(tag, attrs, html) {
97	    var n = document.createElement(tag);
98	    if (attrs) Object.keys(attrs).forEach(function (k) { n.setAttribute(k, attrs[k]); });
99	    if (html != null) n.innerHTML = html;
100	    return n;
101	  }
102	
103	  function safeContext() {
104	    try {
105	      return (typeof cfg.context === 'function') ? (cfg.context() || {}) : {};
106	    } catch (err) {
107	      return { context_error: String(err) };     // never let the host's code break submit
108	    }
109	  }
110	
111	  function send(message, name, email) {
112	    var payload = {
113	      project: PROJECT,
114	      message: message,
115	      name: name || '',
116	      email: email || '',
117	      url: location.href,
118	      ts: new Date().toISOString(),
119	      context: safeContext()
120	    };
121	    if (!ENDPOINT || ENDPOINT.indexOf('PASTE_') === 0) {
122	      // Not deployed yet. Do not bother the user; leave a trace for the developer.
123	      console.warn('[feedback] endpoint not configured; message not delivered:', payload);
124	      return Promise.resolve(false);
125	    }
126	    // text/plain avoids the CORS preflight that Apps Script does not answer.
127	    return fetch(ENDPOINT, {
128	      method: 'POST',
129	      mode: 'no-cors',
130	      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'text/plain;charset=utf-8' },
131	      body: JSON.stringify(payload)
132	    }).then(function () { return true; })
133	      .catch(function (err) { console.warn('[feedback] post failed:', err); return false; });
134	  }
135	
136	  function mount() {
137	    document.head.appendChild(el('style', null, CSS));
138	
139	    var btn = el('button', {
140	      id: 'wk-fb-btn',                       // id so the site_click tracker sees it
141	      type: 'button',
142	      'aria-haspopup': 'dialog',
143	      'aria-controls': 'wk-fb-panel'
144	    }, 'Feedback');
145	
146	    var panel = el('section', {
147	      id: 'wk-fb-panel',
148	      role: 'dialog',
149	      'aria-label': 'Send feedback',
150	      hidden: 'hidden'
151	    }, FORM_HTML);
152	
153	    document.body.appendChild(btn);
154	    document.body.appendChild(panel);
155	
156	    function val(id) {
157	      var n = panel.querySelector(id);
158	      return n ? n.value : '';
159	    }
160	
161	    function open() {
162	      panel.hidden = false;
163	      panel.innerHTML = FORM_HTML;           // fresh form every open
164	      wire();
165	      var msg = panel.querySelector('#wk-fb-msg');
166	      if (msg) msg.focus();
167	    }
168	    function close() { panel.hidden = true; btn.focus(); }
169	
170	    function thanks() {
171	      panel.innerHTML = '<h2>Thank you</h2><p id="wk-fb-done">Noted.</p>';
172	      setTimeout(function () {
173	        panel.hidden = true;
174	        panel.innerHTML = FORM_HTML;
175	        wire();
176	      }, 1400);
177	    }
178	
179	    function wire() {
180	      var c = panel.querySelector('#wk-fb-cancel');
181	      var s = panel.querySelector('#wk-fb-send');
182	      if (c) c.onclick = close;
183	      if (s) s.onclick = function () {
184	        var text = val('#wk-fb-msg').trim();
185	        if (!text) { close(); return; }
186	        // fire and forget: the user is thanked either way
187	        send(text, val('#wk-fb-name').trim(), val('#wk-fb-email').trim());
188	        thanks();
189	      };
190	    }
191	
192	    btn.addEventListener('click', function () {
193	      if (panel.hidden) open(); else close();
194	    });
195	    document.addEventListener('keydown', function (e) {
196	      if (e.key === 'Escape' && !panel.hidden) close();
197	    });
198	    wire();
199	
200	    window.Feedback = { open: open, close: close };   // minimal public API
201	  }
202	
203	  if (document.readyState === 'loading') {
204	    document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', mount);
205	  } else {
206	    mount();
207	  }
208	})();
209	
```

#### Thinking

The feedback widget is the estate's WEB_KIT standard file, and it says "copy as-is, do not fork." So I shouldn't directly modify feedback.js (that would be a fork). Instead, the correct approach is to use the existing configuration/hooks.

Looking at the code:
- `open()` resets the form with `panel.innerHTML = FORM_HTML;` every time it opens, and then calls `wire()`. In other words, even if you set the name field just once at mount time, it gets cleared every time it opens.
- There's a minimal public API: `window.Feedback = { open, close }`.

The identity (display_name) is stored in localStorage under the key `smithics_fields_identity_v1` (pulse.js's IDENTITY_KEY), as `display_name`.

How can I auto-fill the name without forking feedback.js?

Option A: Modify feedback.js to read the name from a config hook. But the file says "do not fork." That said, this is the estate's canonical file. Approach in the inline script:
```js
(function () {
  'use strict';
  function displayName() {
    try {
      var raw = localStorage.getItem('smithics_fields_identity_v1');
      var obj = raw ? JSON.parse(raw) : null;
      return (obj && obj.display_name) || '';
    } catch (e) { return ''; }
  }
  function fillIfEmpty(panel) {
    var name = panel.querySelector('#wk-fb-name');
    if (name && !name.value) {
      var dn = displayName();
      if (dn) name.value = dn;
    }
  }
  function watch(panel) {
    fillIfEmpty(panel);
    if (window.MutationObserver) {
      new MutationObserver(function () { fillIfEmpty(panel); }).observe(panel, { childList: true });
    }
  }
  function init() {
    var panel = document.getElementById('wk-fb-panel');
    if (panel) { watch(panel); return; }
    // Panel mounts on DOMContentLoaded; wait for it.
    if (window.MutationObserver) {
      var mo = new MutationObserver(function () {
        var p = document.getElementById('wk-fb-panel');
        if (p) { mo.disconnect(); watch(p); }
      });
      mo.observe(document.documentElement, { childList: true, subtree: true });
    } else {
      document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function () {
        var p = document.getElementById('wk-fb-panel');
        if (p) watch(p);
      });
    }
  }
  if (document.readyState === 'loading') {
    document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', init);
  } else {
    init();
  }
})();
```

Let me write the code.

```html
  <!-- Auto-populate the feedback name field with the signed-in display name.
       The widget (feedback.js) is WEB_KIT-standard and must not be forked, so
       this watches its panel and fills the (re)created name input from the
       shared estate identity (the name entered at the sign-in gate). Fails soft. -->
  <script>
    (function () {
      'use strict';
      function signedInName() {
        try {
          var raw = localStorage.getItem('smithics_fields_identity_v1');
          var obj = raw ? JSON.parse(raw) : null;
          return (obj && typeof obj.display_name === 'string') ? obj.display_name.trim() : '';
        } catch (e) { return ''; }
      }
      function fillIfEmpty(panel) {
        var nameInput = panel.querySelector('#wk-fb-name');
        if (nameInput && !nameInput.value) {
          var n = signedInName();
          if (n) nameInput.value = n;
        }
      }
      function watchPanel(panel) {
        fillIfEmpty(panel);                       // cover the already-mounted form
        if (window.MutationObserver) {
          // open() rebuilds the form via innerHTML on every open; fill each time.
          new MutationObserver(function () { fillIfEmpty(panel); })
            .observe(panel, { childList: true });
        }
      }
      function init() {
        var panel = document.getElementById('wk-fb-panel');
        if (panel) { watchPanel(panel); return; }
        // feedback.js mounts on DOMContentLoaded; wait for the panel to appear.
        if (window.MutationObserver) {
          var mo = new MutationObserver(function () {
            var p = document.getElementById('wk-fb-panel');
            if (p) { mo.disconnect(); watchPanel(p); }
          });
          mo.observe(document.documentElement, { childList: true, subtree: true });
        }
      }
      if (document.readyState === 'loading') {
        document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', init);
      } else {
        init();
      }
    })();
  </script>
```

Wait — there's a subtlety with the DOMContentLoaded ordering. feedback.js registers its mount on DOMContentLoaded when readyState is 'loading'. My script (after feedback.js) also sees readyState 'loading' and registers init on DOMContentLoaded. Since feedback.js registered first, mount() runs first, then init(). So by the time init runs, the panel exists → watchPanel directly. Good. The MutationObserver fallback is just insurance.

But actually, if both register on DOMContentLoaded and feedback.js's mount runs synchronously during DOMContentLoaded dispatch (before my init), then the panel exists. Good.

Now, one concern: the MutationObserver on the panel with childList:true. When open() does panel.innerHTML = FORM_HTML, that's a childList mutation (children removed and added). The observer fires → fillIfEmpty. Good. But the observer callback fires asynchronously (microtask). By that time, the form exists. Good.

Also, wire() is called synchronously in open() right after innerHTML. My fill happens in a microtask after that. No conflict.

```json
        } catch (e) { return ''; }
      }
      function fillIfEmpty(panel) {
        var nameInput = panel.querySelector('#wk-fb-name');
        if (nameInput && !nameInput.value) {
          var n = signedInName();
          if (n) nameInput.value = n;
        }
      }
      function watchPanel(panel) {
        fillIfEmpty(panel);                       // cover an already-mounted form
        if (window.MutationObserver) {
          // open() rebuilds the form via innerHTML on every open; refill each time.
          new MutationObserver(function () { fillIfEmpty(panel); })
            .observe(panel, { childList: true });
        }
      }
      function init() {
        var panel = document.getElementById('wk-fb-panel');
        if (panel) { watchPanel(panel); return; }
        // feedback.js mounts on DOMContentLoaded; wait for the panel to appear.
        if (window.MutationObserver) {
          var mo = new MutationObserver(function () {
            var p = document.getElementById('wk-fb-panel');
            if (p) { mo.disconnect(); watchPanel(p); }
          });
          mo.observe(document.documentElement, { childList: true, subtree: true });
        }
      }
      if (document.readyState === 'loading') {
        document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', init);
      } else {
        init();
      }
    })();
  </script>
</body>
</html>

```

FIX: byRankAllMap() (rank->ALL entries) + eventsForRank(rank,dir) unions every entry's events chronologically; focused dots, flanking cells and recolourAxisCellsFor all aggregate per-rank now. PRIOR: r76 (QoderWork 2026-07-22) HOTFIX — grammar card render was DEAD (Smith screenshot: empty question area + empty live-stats, footer label r70). Root cause: r75's error-ID branch wrapped `const promptEl = renderPromptElement(...)` inside the else block, but the free-text branch still referenced `promptEl` at the vocab-help insertion (`card.insertBefore(helpBar, promptEl.nextSibling)`), now out of scope -> ReferenceError on every free-text item with vocab_help, thrown before the card was appended, halting the whole render (live-stats never populated). FIX: hoisted `let promptEl = null;` to function scope. node --check clean. Build label bumped 2026-07-21-r70 -> 2026-07-22-r76 so Smith can confirm the fix loaded. PRIOR: r75 (QoderWork 2026-07-22) FIVE CHANGES. (1) SESSION SCORE REWORK: removed 70% correct/incorrect threshold; now raw marks_awarded/marks_possible accumulation; chip shades from white to deep olive rgba(61,74,28) by percentage; text flips white past 55%; reset button kept; CSS transition 0.4s. Smith: "no judgments, no thresholds, just keeping count." (2) KNOWN-WORDS-ONLY DRILL FILTER: new vocabFilter.knownOnly checkbox appears in all drill modes (gender/accent/spelling); practisedLemmas() builds a cached Set of all lemmas with ≥1 vocab event (either direction); gender drill deck filters to practised nouns; accent drill filters items whose correct-answer word (accent-stripped) matches a practised lemma. Smith: "a tick for just words I know." (3) IDENTITY STRIP: #identity-box moved from absolute inside header to a full-width thin strip ABOVE the header (insertBefore); CSS: flex, justify-content:flex-end, 12px, border-bottom. (4) RESIDUE SOFT SECOND-CHANCE: showResiduePrompt() intercepts residue-engine misses before commitResult; gentle banner "You had the right answer, plus extra word(s) (X). Want to trim it?" with "Let me trim it" (retry, refocus+select input) and "Mark as is" (commit the miss); fires at most once per item; Esc=retry, Enter=mark. (5) ERROR-ID QTYPE RENDERER: new type "error_id" in grammar cards; prompt sentence rendered as inline tappable word buttons (.errorid-words/.errorid-word); tap=immediate mark; keyboard 1-N selects, Enter marks; index-scored via buildMcqResult (choices synthesised from prompt words, answer_index=error_index); post-mark highlighting (.correct-pick/.wrong-pick/.reveal-correct); correction field surfaced in explanation; choice_tags misconception firing inherited. NO DATA YET — overregularisation_distractors_v1.json (216 records) is raw material for an authoring pass; renderer is live and waiting. ALSO: gender translation-credit rule confirmed ALREADY BUILT (markVocab lines 4716-4772: gender fires only on unambiguous signal, bare lemma leaves gender silent). Status note was stale. PRIOR: r74 (QoderWork 2026-07-22) AI MARKER QUALITY + PER-WORD COLOURING + VOCAB CREDIT FIX. Worker prompt (worker/src/index.ts): added rule 11 ANTI-HALLUCINATION GUARD (only report accent errors when the learner's form VISIBLY DIFFERS from the correct form in accent marks; if spelling matches reference exactly, do NOT report; when uncertain, stay silent) — fixes the "torneremmo" false accent accusation. Added rule 12 VOCABULARY vs GRAMMAR distinction (wrong word = vocabulary bucket, NOT gender/agreement; only fire gender bucket when the RIGHT word has wrong grammatical marking) — fixes "maglia" for "maglione" being tagged as a gender error instead of vocab. NEEDS WRANGLER DEPLOY. Housing (app.js + style.css): "You wrote" line now does PER-WORD colouring — each word checked against reference answers; words found in references render green (.yw-right), words not found render pink with soft red background (.yw-wrong). Falls back to single-colour when no references available. CSS: .yw-word/.yw-right/.yw-wrong added. VOCAB CREDIT FIX (translation_marker.js): IT→EN vocab injection now uses accent-stripped 3-char stem matching + startsWith containment, so conjugated forms (dirò→dire, vedo→vedere, glie→gli) correctly inject vocab recognition buckets into bucket_context. Previously only exact lemma or 4-char prefix matched, missing most conjugated verbs. TOKEN NOTE: 55749 input tokens is real — the 581-entry cross-topic menu (translation_marker_bucket_menu.json) is ~13k tokens alone, plus per-item vocab injections for IT→EN. Architecture may want to trim the menu or use prompt caching. PRIOR: r73 (QoderWork 2026-07-22) ACCENT DRILL SHIPPED. Loader wired: accent file added to tail Promise.all singleton loads, merged into grammar array after per-topic flatten. Filter bar shows compact info bar in accent mode. PRIOR: r72 (QoderWork 2026-07-22) EIGHT CHANGES from Smith's live-session feedback. Resolves ~138 items rendering "Use usually" etc. ALSO: spelling drill resultHost moved above actions (feedback visible immediately). PRIOR: r65 VANISH FIX - REAL cause (Smith was right; my r64 flanking-dilution diagnosis was WRONG). The dots that 'flashed then vanished' in the 1000-2000 FOCUSED block were HOMOGRAPH RANK COLLISIONS: 480 ranks estate-wide (175 in 1000-2000) carry >1 entry (largo+toccare @1015, nave+presente @1004, ...), and byRank kept only the FIRST entry per rank, so practising any non-first entry never lit its dot - it flashed (by data-rank) then reverted to untouched (first entry has no events). FIX: byRankAllMap() (rank->ALL entries) + eventsForRank(rank,dir) unions every entry's events chronologically; focused dots, flanking cells and recolourAxisCellsFor all aggregate per-rank now. ALSO fixed a MIX-mode bug: activeDir mapped 'mix'->active-only (hid every passive event); now vocabHeatmapDir() => 'any' for mix/unset. VERIFIED node --check + merge harness (sister practice lights the dot; recency order; single-entry fast path). r64 (recolour-in-place on mark instead of full rebuild; keepAxes on Next) STILL STANDS - correct for perf, just aimed the vanish story at the wrong cells. THEME DRILL diagnosed, NOT yet fixed (task 10): over-threshold theme (colours) expands on click1, filters on click2; once vocabFilter.theme set the axis HIDES every other theme cell (restriction-focus = Smith's own 2026-07-20 ask) -> feels like a new page, heavy border, only the slow 'Back to all' to escape; reclick-to-clear exists but Smith can't find/use it. RULING NEEDED: keep restriction-focus + add a fast one-click clear on the active cell, or drop it (dim others instead). NEEDS r65 PUSH. PRIOR: r64 VOCAB PERF + VANISH FIX (Smith direct). SLOWNESS root cause: the per-answer FULL heatmap rebuild. doMark called renderVocabAxes() (teardown+rebuild of ~990 focused dots + ~20 flanking blocks, each dot an eventsForEntry lookup + click listener) on EVERY mark, and renderVocab() rebuilt it again on EVERY Next. NOW: doMark recolours ONLY touched cells in place via new recolourAxisCellsFor(entry) (focused dot + its flanking cell + gender/theme lemma dots; sets SETTLED bg, not just a flash); renderVocab(keepAxes) + doNext->renderVocab(true) skips the rebuild on advance; full rebuild now only on strand-enter / filter change. VANISH: focused-grid hits already persisted (scrittore: 1 event->1.0->dark green); the 'flashed then vanished' was FLANKING cells - a cell aggregates ~9 lemmas so one hit dilutes to ~0.29, and baseline==yellowStop(0.2) made touched read as untouched (disponibile). FIX: recolour sets the true aggregate AND adds .has-practice (inset green border) to any flank/lemma cell containing practice; also added in renderFlankingStripes so it survives full rebuilds. #### Thinking

The changes have been reflected in index.html. I'll submit the completion report to Smith.

Done — the feedback form now auto-populates the name field with whatever they entered at the sign-in gate.

How it works: `feedback.js` is the WEB_KIT standard ("copy verbatim, do not fork"), and it rebuilds its form from scratch every time the panel opens (`panel.innerHTML = FORM_HTML`), so setting the value once at load wouldn't survive. Rather than fork the widget, I added a small self-contained watcher script in `index.html` right after the feedback.js tag. It reads `display_name` from the identity record (`smithics_fields_identity_v1`) fresh each time, and a MutationObserver fills `#wk-fb-name` whenever the form is rebuilt. It only fills when the field is empty (a typed name is never overwritten), trims whitespace, and fails soft — if the observer, the identity key, or the panel is missing, it just stays quiet.

Verified with a harness covering six scenarios: name fills on mount, refills after the open() reset, leaves a non-empty field untouched, and does nothing when signed out — all passed.

It rides in the same unpushed r77 batch as the stress drill (footer will read `2026-07-23-r77` once you push). `_status/Housing.md` is updated.

```json
159	  <script>
160	    window.FEEDBACK_CONFIG = {
161	      project: 'linguics-housing',
162	      context: function () {
163	        // Called at submit time; must not throw. Build id and strand are read
164	        // live so the values are always current, never baked.
165	        var strandBtn = document.querySelector('#strand-nav button.active');
166	        return {
167	          strand: (strandBtn && strandBtn.dataset && strandBtn.dataset.strand) || 'welcome',
168	          build: (window.LL && window.LL.build) || 'unknown'
169	        };
170	      }
171	    };
172	  </script>
173	  <script src="js/feedback.js"></script>
174	
175	  <!-- Auto-populate the feedback name field with the signed-in display name.
176	       feedback.js is the WEB_KIT standard widget (copy verbatim, do not fork),
177	       so instead of editing it we watch its panel: open() rebuilds the form via
178	       innerHTML on every open, so we fill the freshly-created name input from
179	       the shared estate identity (the name entered at the sign-in gate).
180	       Reads the name fresh each fill (survives sign-out/in), only fills when
181	       the field is empty, and fails soft.  QoderWork 2026-07-23 -->
182	  <script>
183	    (function () {
184	      'use strict';
185	      function signedInName() {
186	        try {
187	          var raw = localStorage.getItem('smithics_fields_identity_v1');
188	          var obj = raw ? JSON.parse(raw) : null;
189	          return (obj && typeof obj.display_name === 'string') ? obj.display_name.trim() : '';
190	        } catch (e) { return ''; }
191	      }
192	      function fillIfEmpty(panel) {
193	        var nameInput = panel.querySelector('#wk-fb-name');
194	        if (nameInput && !nameInput.value) {
195	          var n = signedInName();
196	          if (n) nameInput.value = n;
197	        }
198	      }
199	      function watchPanel(panel) {
200	        fillIfEmpty(panel);                       // cover an already-mounted form
201	        if (window.MutationObserver) {
202	          // open() rebuilds the form via innerHTML on every open; refill each time.
203	          new MutationObserver(function () { fillIfEmpty(panel); })
204	            .observe(panel, { childList: true });
205	        }
206	      }
207	      function init() {
208	        var panel = document.getElementById('wk-fb-panel');
209	        if (panel) { watchPanel(panel); return; }
210	        // feedback.js mounts on DOMContentLoaded; wait for the panel to appear.
211	        if (window.MutationObserver) {
212	          var mo = new MutationObserver(function () {
213	            var p = document.getElementById('wk-fb-panel');
214	            if (p) { mo.disconnect(); watchPanel(p); }
215	          });
216	          mo.observe(document.documentElement, { childList: true, subtree: true });
217	        }
218	      }
219	      if (document.readyState === 'loading') {
220	        document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', init);
221	      } else {
222	        init();
223	      }
224	    })();
225	  </script>
226	</body>
227	</html>
```

#### Thinking

Now I understand the pattern. The vocab config has "Gender drill" and "Spelling drill" sections, each with an `entry-special-drills` div, an h3 heading, a chip row, and a hint paragraph. I need to add a "Stress drill" section in the same style, with an explanation that makes it clear it's about pronunciation — where the stress falls — using the capitano example: *cap*itano / ca*pit*ano / capit*an*o / capitan*o*.

But wait — how does the stress drill get started? Looking at the current flow: the stress drill is a mode chip on the vocab strand's filter bar (inside the app, not on the entry screen). The user wants it on the intro page (entry screen). So I should add a stress drill section to `renderVocabConfig`, similar to gender/spelling drills, that sets `vocabFilter.stressDrill = true` and starts a vocab session (or starts the stress deck directly).

Let me check how the accent drill is handled on the entry screen — is there an accent drill section? Let me search for accentDrill in the entry config area.

#### Thinking

In other words, the accent drill isn't on the entry screen either—it's just a mode chip on the vocab strand's filter bar. Smith wants the stress drill placed on the intro page (entry screen), with an explanation.

The cleanest approach that fits the existing pattern: add a "Stress drill" section to `renderVocabConfig` (after the spelling drill section), with:
- an h3 "Stress drill"
- a chip to toggle it on
- an explanatory hint that conveys what it is: pronunciation—where the stress falls—with the capitano example rendered with bold syllables.

The example Smith gave: *cap*itano or ca*pit*ano or capit*an*o or capitan*o* — i.e. "capitano" with each of the 4 syllables bolded in turn. In the drill, the bolding is done with `**syl**` markers, but on the intro page we can render actual `<strong>` elements via the DOM.

Wait — "capitano" has 4 syllables: ca-pi-ta-no. Stress positions from the end: 1 (no → capitan*o*), 2 (ta → capit*an*o), 3 (pi → ca*pit*ano), 4 (ca → *cap*itano). Smith's example enumerates them in the order 4,3,2,1 from the beginning of the word. The actual word "capitano" (they captain) is a sdrucciola: CA-pi-ta-no, stress on the 3rd from the end. Good example.

Now, when the stress drill chip is toggled on in the entry screen and Start is pressed, `startVocabSession()` needs to set `vocabFilter.stressDrill = true` and build the stress deck. Let me check what startVocabSession currently does and how the stress drill is currently started via the mode chip.

Looking at the mode chip handler around line 2576: it sets vocabFilter.stressDrill and calls buildStressDeck(). Let me read that section to confirm the exact flow.


#### Tool: Read

> Read: `C:\Claude (not on Gdrive, nor OneDrive)\Linguics\housing\js\app.js`

**Output:**

```json

2577	        vocabFilter.stressDrill = m.key === "stress";

2583	      });

2585	    });

2609	        chip.addEventListener("click", () => {
2610	          vocabFilter.knownDir = o.key;
2611	          if (vocabFilter.accentDrill) buildAccentDeck();
2612	          if (vocabFilter.stressDrill) buildStressDeck();
2613	          vocabDeck = []; vocabIndex = 0;
2614	          renderVocab();
2615	        });
2616	        knownRow.appendChild(chip);
2617	      }
2618	      host.appendChild(knownRow);

2620	
2621	    // Spelling drill: vocab controls are irrelevant; show a compact info bar.  QoderWork 2026-07-22

2627	      lbl.style.fontWeight = "600";
```

```json
8242	  function saveLastSession(strand) {
8243	    if (!LL.state) return;
8244	    LL.state.last_session = { strand, sel: JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(entrySel)) };
8245	    try { LL.store.saveState(LL.state); } catch (e) { /* localStorage may be unavailable */ }
8246	  }
8247	  function resumeLastSession() {
8248	    const ls = LL.state && LL.state.last_session;
8249	    if (!ls) return;
8250	    entrySel = JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(ls.sel));
8251	    // Saved sessions from the range era carry cefrRange; migrate to the set.
8252	    if (Array.isArray(entrySel.cefrRange) && entrySel.cefrRange.length === 2 && !Array.isArray(entrySel.cefrLevels)) {
8253	      entrySel.cefrLevels = [];
8254	      for (let k = entrySel.cefrRange[0]; k <= entrySel.cefrRange[1]; k++) entrySel.cefrLevels.push(k);
8255	      entrySel.cefrRange = null;
8256	    }
8257	    if (!Array.isArray(entrySel.cefrLevels)) entrySel.cefrLevels = [];
8258	    if (ls.strand === "grammar") startGrammarSession();
8259	    else if (ls.strand === "vocab") startVocabSession();
8260	    else startTranslationSession();
8261	  }
8262	  function startMistakesSession() {
8263	    const stats = LL.store.allBucketStats();
8264	    const weak = Object.values(stats)
8265	      .filter(s => s.n > 0 && s.correctness < 1)
8266	      .sort((a, b) => a.combined - b.combined)
8267	      .map(s => s.bucket)
8268	      .slice(0, 10);
8269	    const clause = weak.length ? [{ bucketPaths: weak }] : null;
8270	    // Prefer grammar items touching the weak buckets; fall back to all grammar
8271	    // if none match (e.g. the misses are all vocab-side in this early build).
8272	    track("session_start", { strand: "grammar", source: "mistakes" });
8273	    const test = applyFilter(grammarQuestions, { clauses: clause });
8274	    grammarFilter.clauses = test.length ? clause : null;
8275	    grammarFilter.cefrRange = null;
8276	    grammarFilter.topic = ""; grammarFilter.cefr = ""; grammarFilter.bucketPath = "";
8277	    grammarDeck = []; grammarIndex = 0;
8278	    renderGrammarFilterBar();
8279	    renderGrammar();
8280	    showStrand("grammar");
8281	  }
8282	  function renderQuickStarts(col) {
8283	    const ls = LL.state && LL.state.last_session;
8284	    const ms = missStats();
8285	    const showMistakes = ms.n >= 12 && ms.distinct >= 3;
8286	    if (!ls && !showMistakes) return;
8287	    const wrap = document.createElement("div");
8288	    wrap.className = "entry-quickstarts";
8289	    if (showMistakes) {
8290	      const b = document.createElement("button");
8291	      b.type = "button";
8292	      b.className = "entry-quick entry-quick-mistakes";
8293	      b.innerHTML = '<span class="quick-title">Work on recent weaknesses</span>' +
8294	        '<span class="quick-sub">the words and rules you most often miss</span>';
8295	      b.addEventListener("click", () => startMistakesSession());
8296	      wrap.appendChild(b);
```

#### Thinking

Now I understand the overall picture. `entrySel` is persisted via `saveLastSession` and restored via `resumeLastSession`. `startVocabSession` reads `entrySel.vocab` and sets `vocabFilter`. Therefore, what's needed is:

1. Add `stressDrill: false` to `vocab` in `defaultEntrySel()`.
2. Add a "Stress drill" section to `renderVocabConfig`, after the spelling drill section. The contents are:
   - h3 "Stress drill"
   - A chip to toggle `v.stressDrill`
   - An explanatory hint that conveys the pronunciation aspect — where the stress falls — with the capitano example rendered with bold syllables.
3. In `startVocabSession`, set `vocabFilter.stressDrill = !!v.stressDrill;` and, if true, call `buildStressDeck()`. Also, make sure that selecting stress turns off gender/spelling (mutual exclusion, same as the others), and that gender/spelling turn off stress.
4. Add `stress_drill` to the track parameters.

Regarding the explanation: Smith wants "an explanation that makes people realize what this is — pronunciation — where does the stress fall? *cap*itano or ca*pit*ano or capit*an*o or capitan*o*".

So the hint should display the word "capitano" four times, each with a different syllable bolded. This needs to be rendered with actual `<strong>` elements (not textContent). The existing hint uses `textContent`. Either build the example with DOM elements, or use a small helper. The drill's choices use `**syl**` markers, and there's `appendBoldedText(el, text)` which converts `**bold**` → `<strong>` DOM-safely. I can reuse that for the example!

Let me check the signature of `appendBoldedText`.

Actually, I need to verify that it exists and its exact behavior. Let me grep.

```json
3219	  function appendBoldedText(el, text) {
3220	    const parts = String(text == null ? "" : text).split("**");
3221	    for (let i = 0; i < parts.length; i++) {
3222	      if (parts[i] === "") continue;
3223	      if (i % 2 === 1) {
3224	        const s = document.createElement("strong");
3225	        s.textContent = parts[i];
3226	        el.appendChild(s);
3227	      } else {
3228	        el.appendChild(document.createTextNode(parts[i]));
3229	      }
3230	    }
3231	  }
3232
```

Actually, let me first plan the UI. I'll add a "Stress drill" section after the spelling drill section in `renderVocabConfig`:

```
// ---- Stress drill: where does the stress fall? (Smith 2026-07-23) ----
const stDrills = document.createElement("div");
stDrills.className = "entry-special-drills";
const hSt = document.createElement("h3");
hSt.className = "entry-config-head";
hSt.textContent = "Stress drill";
stDrills.appendChild(hSt);
const stRow = document.createElement("div");
stRow.className = "entry-chip-row";
stRow.appendChild(entryChip("Stress drill", v.stressDrill,
  () => { v.stressDrill = !v.stressDrill; if (v.stressDrill) { v.genderDrill = false; v.spellingDrill = false; } renderEntryScreen(); },
  { title: "Hear the word in your head and pick the syllable that carries the stress" }));
stDrills.appendChild(stRow);
```

Next, the explanation. Smith's request: "an explanation that makes people realize what this is — pronunciation — where does the stress fall? *cap*itano or ca*pit*ano or capit*an*o or capitan*o*".

So I want a hint like this:
"Pronunciation: where does the stress fall? Say the word in your head and pick the stressed syllable — e.g. for capitano:"
And then four examples: **cap**itano · ca**pit**ano · capit**an**o · capitan**o**

Let me build this with DOM. The hint paragraph uses `entry-config-hint`. I'll create the explanatory text and an example row.

Structure:
- p.entry-config-hint: "Pronunciation — where does the stress fall? Say each word in your head, then pick the syllable that carries the stress."
- A div for the example with the four bolded variants.

Let me make the example clear. Use appendBoldedText for each variant, separated by "or". Actually, Smith wrote it as "*cap*itano or ca*pit*ano or capit*an*o or capitan*o*". So I'll render four spans separated by " or ".

Building it:
```
const exRow = document.createElement("div");
exRow.className = "entry-stress-example";
const variants = ["**cap**itano", "ca**pit**ano", "capit**an**o", "capitan**o**"];
variants.forEach((vt, i) => {
  if (i > 0) exRow.appendChild(document.createTextNode("  or  "));
  const span = document.createElement("span");
  span.className = "entry-stress-variant";
  appendBoldedText(span, vt);
  exRow.appendChild(span);
});
stDrills.appendChild(exRow);
```

Wait — but there's a subtle point. In the actual drill, the options bold one syllable and the position is counted from the end. The capitano example: ca-pi-ta-no. The four variants bold syllables 4, 3, 2, 1 from the end respectively. This matches the drill's option layout (option i bolds syllable i+1 from the end... no wait, let me re-check).

From the summary: "choices[i] bolds the syllable at position (i+1) from the end (1=tronca/last, 2=piana, 3=sdrucciola, 4=bisdrucciola)". So choices[0] bolds the last syllable (capitan**o**), choices[1] bolds the second-to-last (capit**an**o), choices[2] bolds the third-to-last (ca**pit**ano), choices[3] bolds the fourth-to-last (**cap**itano).

But in the drill, the options are shuffled. So the display order on the intro page doesn't need to match the drill's order. Smith's example order (*cap*itano, ca*pit*ano, capit*an*o, capitan*o*) is from the beginning of the word: 4th from end, 3rd, 2nd, 1st. That's fine as an illustration. It reads naturally left to right.

Actually, to make it pedagogically clearest and to match the ambient legend ("1 = last (tronca) · 2 = 2nd-last (piana) · ..."), maybe I should present it in a way that conveys "pick where the stress falls". But Smith explicitly gave the example format, so I'll follow his order. It shows the same word with the stress moving, which is exactly the "make people realize what this is" moment.

Also to consider: should the hint text change when active vs inactive, like gender/spelling do? Those show different text when active. For stress, I'll keep the explanation always visible (since that's the whole point — the explanation), and maybe add a small note when active. Actually, let me keep it simple and always show the full explanation, since Smith's core request is the explanation. But to match the pattern, I could add a second line when active. Let me keep the explanation constant and not over-engineer.

Actually, let me reconsider. The gender/spelling hints toggle text based on active state. For stress, the explanation is the star. I'll always show the explanation paragraph + example. This directly satisfies "an explanation that makes people realize what this is".

Now, the mutual exclusion: the gender drill chip sets `if (v.genderDrill) v.spellingDrill = false;` and spelling sets `if (v.spellingDrill) v.genderDrill = false;`. I need to extend these to also clear stressDrill, and the stress chip clears genderDrill and spellingDrill. Note: accentDrill is not in entrySel.vocab (the accent drill is only accessible from the in-session mode chips, not from the entry screen). So I only handle gender/spelling/stress mutual exclusion.

Wait, let me double-check: is accentDrill in entrySel.vocab? Looking at defaultEntrySel: `vocab: { direction, subBand, genderDrill, spellingDrill, lens, rankRanges, cefr, theme, catIds, catLabel }`. No accentDrill. So the accent drill is only reachable from the in-session mode switcher, not from the entry screen. OK. So on the entry screen I only handle gender/spelling/stress.

But there's a subtle point: when starting a session with stressDrill, `vocabFilter.accentDrill` might still be true from a previous in-session toggle? No — vocabFilter is a module-level object. Let me check its initial state and whether startVocabSession resets accentDrill. Looking at startVocabSession (lines 8053–8073): it sets genderDrill, spellingDrill, spellingClass, direction, subBand, topN, rankRange, rankRanges, themes, theme. It doesn't reset accentDrill or stressDrill. Hmm. But vocabFilter is initialized with accentDrill: false, stressDrill: false. If a user starts a vocab session, then toggles to accent drill via the mode chip, then returns to the entry screen and starts a new session, accentDrill would remain true. That's an existing latent issue, but I should make sure stressDrill is handled correctly.

Actually, to be safe and correct, in startVocabSession I'll explicitly set both accentDrill and stressDrill:
```
vocabFilter.accentDrill = false;   // accent drill is in-session only; reset on new session
vocabFilter.stressDrill = !!v.stressDrill;
```
And if stressDrill is true, call buildStressDeck().

Wait, but buildStressDeck requires stressQuestions to be loaded. If content is still loading, stressQuestions will be empty and the deck will be empty. But startVocabSession is only reachable when content is loaded (the entry config only renders when !LL.contentLoading). Actually, renderVocabConfig is only called when !LL.contentLoading (line 7607 checks LL.contentLoading and shows a loading message instead). So by the time startVocabSession runs via the Start button, content is loaded. Good. And buildSpellingDeck is called the same way. So calling buildStressDeck() there is consistent.

Let me also verify: does the stress drill use the direction/knownDir filters? buildStressDeck uses knownDir (via practisedLemmas). That's fine — knownDir is a separate vocabFilter field that persists. The entry screen doesn't set knownDir, so it stays at its previous value. That's consistent with the accent drill's behavior.

#### Thinking

Next, I'll insert the stress drill section. I'll place it after `panel.appendChild(spDrills);` and before `appendStart(panel, () => startVocabSession());`.

The description text needs to convey that this is about pronunciation—where the stress falls—and I'll use the capitano example. I'll build it with DOM and use appendBoldedText for the bold syllables.

Let me write it like this:

```js
    // ---- Stress drill: pronunciation — where does the stress fall? (Smith 2026-07-23) ----
    const stDrills = document.createElement("div");
    stDrills.className = "entry-special-drills";
    const hSt = document.createElement("h3");
    hSt.className = "entry-config-head";
    hSt.textContent = "Stress drill";
    stDrills.appendChild(hSt);
    const stRow = document.createElement("div");
    stRow.className = "entry-chip-row";
    stRow.appendChild(entryChip("Stress drill", v.stressDrill,
      () => { v.stressDrill = !v.stressDrill; if (v.stressDrill) { v.genderDrill = false; v.spellingDrill = false; } renderEntryScreen(); },
      { title: "Say the word in your head and pick the syllable that carries the stress" }));
    stDrills.appendChild(stRow);
    // What it is, made concrete: the same word, four possible stresses.  QoderWork 2026-07-23
    const stNote = document.createElement("p");
    stNote.className = "entry-config-hint";
    stNote.textContent = "Pronunciation \u2014 where does the stress fall? Say each word in your head, then pick the syllable that carries it. The same letters, four different stresses:";
    stDrills.appendChild(stNote);
    const stEx = document.createElement("div");
    stEx.className = "entry-stress-example";
    ["**cap**itano", "ca**pit**ano", "capit**an**o", "capitan**o**"].forEach((vt, i) => {
      if (i > 0) {
        const or = document.createElement("span");
        or.className = "entry-stress-or";
        or.textContent = "or";
        stEx.appendChild(or);
      }
      const span = document.createElement("span");
      span.className = "entry-stress-variant";
      appendBoldedText(span, vt);
      stEx.appendChild(span);
    });
    stDrills.appendChild(stEx);
    panel.appendChild(stDrills);
```

This is good. The description text is always displayed (that's the whole point). The example shows capitano with the stress position moving around.

I'll insert it after `panel.appendChild(spDrills);`.

#### Thinking

Now I'll edit startVocabSession. The necessary tasks are:
1. Set `vocabFilter.stressDrill = !!v.stressDrill;`
2. Reset `vocabFilter.accentDrill = false;` (accent is in-session only. To prevent an old true from a previous mode switch from remaining in a new session started from the intro)
3. Call `buildStressDeck()` when stress is on
4. Add `stress_drill` to track

Alright, let's make the edits.

```json
  function startVocabSession() {
    const v = entrySel.vocab;
    vocabFilter.genderDrill = !!v.genderDrill;
    vocabFilter.spellingDrill = !!v.spellingDrill;   // QoderWork 2026-07-22
    vocabFilter.spellingClass = v.spellingClass || "";   // QoderWork 2026-07-22
    vocabFilter.stressDrill = !!v.stressDrill;   // QoderWork 2026-07-23
    vocabFilter.accentDrill = false;   // accent drill is in-session-only; never carried in from the intro  QoderWork 2026-07-23
    vocabFilter.direction = v.genderDrill ? "en_it" : v.direction;   // gender drill: direction unused by the classifier
    // The builder's frequency selection replaces the legacy subBand/topN caps.
    vocabFilter.subBand = "";
    vocabFilter.topN = 0;
    vocabFilter.rankRange = null;
...
      ? v.rankRanges.map(r => ({ start: r.start, end: r.end })) : null;
    vocabFilter.themes = (v.catIds && v.catIds.length) ? v.catIds.slice() : null;
    vocabFilter.theme = (v.catIds && v.catIds.length === 1) ? v.catIds[0] : "";  // single-id picks light the in-session themes axis
    vocabDeck = []; vocabDeckDirs = []; vocabIndex = 0;
    if (vocabFilter.spellingDrill) buildSpellingDeck();   // QoderWork 2026-07-22
    if (vocabFilter.stressDrill) buildStressDeck();   // QoderWork 2026-07-23
    saveLastSession("vocab");
    track("session_start", { strand: "vocab", source: "welcome", direction: vocabFilter.direction, gender_drill: vocabFilter.genderDrill, spelling_drill: vocabFilter.spellingDrill, spelling_class: vocabFilter.spellingClass, stress_drill: vocabFilter.stressDrill, lens: v.lens || "numeric", category: v.catLabel || "", ranges: (v.rankRanges || []).map(r => r.start + "-" + r.end).join("+") });
    renderVocab();
    showStrand("vocab");
  }
```
